gmw_00000133.name: MCG-3-34-64
gmw_00000133.overview: MCG-3-34-64 is a nearby luminous infrared spiral galaxy [arXiv:2403.07717, arXiv:1310.2770] hosting a Seyfert 1.8 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1706.02088, arXiv:1109.0921]. The galaxy exhibits extended radio and mid-infrared emissions [arXiv:2403.07717], a radio jet [arXiv:1310.2770], and a complex, heavily absorbed X-ray spectrum [arXiv:1706.02088, arXiv:2403.07717].

sources.summary: The paper studies the Seyfert AGN IRAS 13197-1627, modeling its complex X-ray spectrum, inner-disk reflection, spin constraints, and variable neutral and ionized absorption.

quotes.quote: \ (also known as MCG--03-34-64) is a source of particular interest in this respect. \ is a bright, nearby ( z=0.01654 ), type 1.8 Seyfert AGN with a complex X-ray spectrum. Previous observations with , , \ and \ have shown that \ is typically absorbed by a moderate neutral column ( N_ H 5 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , although on rare occasions it can exhibit a much greater level of obscuration, reaching column densities up to N_ H 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ), and that it also exhibits evidence for a relativistically broadened iron line from the innermost accretion flow ( Dadina04, Miniutti07iras, torus ).

The broadband X-ray spectrum exhibited by this source is highly complex, with strong contributions from relativistic reflection from the inner accretion disk, absorption and further reprocessing by more distant material, and ionised absorption from an outflow all combining to sculpt the observed spectral form, similar to the well-studied AGN in NGC 1365 ( Risaliti13nat, Walton14, Rivers15 ). By combining the high S/N broadband data provided by \ and \ with the multi-epoch archival data obtained with \ and , we are able to disentangle the relative contributions from all these various components for a variety of different scenarios, allowing us to place constraints on the parameters of the innermost accretion flow, and in turn the spin of the black hole, despite the heavy absorption present in this system. Metallicity and the Inner Disk Based on our analysis of epoch XN1, the key inner disk parameters ( \ black hole spin, disk inclination) appear to be largely independent of the precise treatment of the distant reflection (slab vs torus geometry) and are only moderately sensitive to the form of the primary continuum (powerlaw with an exponential cutoff vs realistic Comptonized continuum). However, the results are strongly dependent on the treatment of the iron abundance for the neutral absorption (and by extension the distant reflection, which we assume to be associated with the same medium). We test two different scenarios for the iron abundance, focusing initially on epoch XN1 as the highest S/N broadband dataset.

sources.summary: The paper presents MCG-03-34-64 as a nearby luminous infrared Seyfert with broad lines, extended radio/mid-IR emission, and multiwavelength evidence for a sub-100 pc dual SMBH system.

quotes.quote: MCG-03-34-64, also known as IRAS 13197-1627, is a nearby luminous infrared galaxy ( z =0.01654), and identified as one of the hardest X-ray sources in the local Universe . Earlier X-ray observations with ASCA, XMM-Newton, and BeppoSAX unveiled an extremely hard and complex source spectrum, believed to arise from heavy absorption from a multi-layered and clumpy medium. This galaxy exhibits extended radio ( 300 pc) and mid-infrared ( 700 pc) emissions, roughly aligned with the major axis of the host galaxy . Optical emission lines in the spectra classify MCG-03-34-64 as a Seyfert type 1.8-2 AGN .

In this work, we present a multi-wavelength analysis of the circumnuclear region of MCG-03-34-64. Chandra /ACIS-S X-ray imaging-spectroscopy reveals two spatially-resolved emission peaks of equal intensity in the neutral Fe K line (6.3-6.5 keV), consistent with the existence of a dual SMBH system separated by 99 29 pc, and hosted by a Seyfert galaxy in the local Universe ( D 78 Mpc, 340 pc/ '' ). These findings are supported by HST /ACS optical imaging, and VLA in A configuration radio imaging.

sources.summary: The paper classifies MCG 03-34-064 as a Seyfert 1.8 whose soft X-rays mix star formation and AGN photoionization, while absorbed AGN emission dominates hard X-rays.

quotes.quote: MCG 03-34-064 This galaxy is classified as Seyfert 1.8. The soft X-ray spectrum is dominated by a thermal component likely produced by star-formation and gas photoionized by the AGN. The AGN absorbed component dominates the spectrum in the hard X-ray range. An \ emission line is detected at 6.4 keV, consistent with neutral iron. A detailed analysis of the \ data of this galaxy was presented by Miniutti2007 .

sources.summary: MCG 03 34 064 has a companion contributing to IRAS flux, but the main source shows sharply peaked X-rays and a clear absorbed AGN spectrum.

quotes.quote: [ 127 ] MCG 03 34 064 : This source has a north-eastern companion at 1.8 ', MCG 03 34 063, which is responsible for about 25 of the IRAS flux ChuSan2017 . The analysis of this companion source is not included in this work because it is undetected in the Chandra data. A correction to the IR luminosity for the contribution of MCG 02 34 063 has been considered for this source. MCG-03-34-064 presents a very peaked central emission in all X-ray bands, and is a clear absorbed AGN, as seen from the spectrum. Fitting with a fixed photon index of 1.8 yields an absorbing column density of N_ H 5 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of M-3-34-64, describing its best-fit components, Fe K emission or flux behavior, and comparison with previous modeling.

quotes.quote: M-3-34-64 The XMM-Newton spectra were fit by a double absorbed powerlaw with a thermal component and a Gaussian at the Fe K line energy. Miniutti et al. (2007) fit this source with a reflection model, with the soft emission accommodated by a power law model with two thermal components and a photoionized gas model and Gaussian components at 6.4 and 6.8 keV.

sources.summary: The atlas gives MCG-3-34-64's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: MCG-3-34-64 is an early-type infrared-luminous spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0165 ( D Mpc) with a Sy 1.8-2 nucleus aguero_spectral_1994,de_grijp_warm_1992 with polarized broad emission lines young_polarimetry_1996 . It is a member of the nine-month BAT AGN sample and possesses a jet visible in radio with an inner PA of 39 schmitt_jet_2001 . Not that MCG-3-34-64 is often confused with or erroneously called MCG-3-34-63.

gmw_00000134.name: MCG-5-23-16
gmw_00000134.overview: MCG-5-23-16 is an early-type S0 Seyfert 1.9 galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0304037, arXiv:1310.2770] located at a redshift of z = 0.0085 [arXiv:2403.14169, arXiv:1310.2770]. It is a bright, variable X-ray source [arXiv:astro-ph/0304037, arXiv:2403.14169] featuring an extended ionization cone [arXiv:2305.10213, arXiv:1310.2770] and a relativistically broad iron K line [arXiv:1006.1982, arXiv:1405.3674].

sources.summary: The paper studies the Seyfert galaxy MCG-5-23-16 with IXPE and NuSTAR, measuring weak X-ray polarization and modeling its corona, accretion rates, and outflow geometry.

quotes.quote: MCG-5-23-16 is a Seyfert 1.9 galaxy Veronetal1980 located at redshift z = 0.00849 WegnerEtal2003AJ....126.2268W . The source is relatively bright in X-rays with F _ 2-10 = 7-10 10^ 11 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 MattsonWeaver2004ApJ...601..771M and characterized by moderate neutral absorption ( N_H 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ). It has been extensively studied in the X-ray band to estimate corona properties and spectral high-energy cut-off using INTEGRAL and NuSTAR observations BeckmannEtal2008A&A...492...93B,MolinaEtal2013MNRAS.433.1687M,BalokovicEtal2015ApJ...800...62B,ZoghbiEtal2017ApJ...836....2Z . Furthermore, the X-ray spectrum of this source showed the presence of a soft excess and complex Fe K emission with a broad and asymmetric narrow line features in ASCA WeaverEtal1997ApJ...474..675W , BeppoSAX , Chandra , and XMM-Newton DewanganEtal2003ApJ...592...52D,BalestraEtal2004A&A...415..437B,BraitoEtal2007ApJ...670..978B,LiuEtal2023arXiv231216354L observations.

In this work, we have performed the spectro-polarimetric analysis of the Seyfert galaxy MCG-5-23-16 to understand the polarization properties and the X-ray emitting corona using two epochs of data from IXPE and a decade of observations from NuSTAR . Our model-independent analysis poses the PD with an upper limit of 3.8 , while the model-dependent estimates give PD 1.55 0.99 and 1.31 0.95 for the epochs I1 and I2, while the PA values are 37.3^ 20.1^ and 73.2^ 23.1^ , which are in accord with the previous studies within error bar MarinucciEtal2022MNRAS.516.5907M,TagliacozzoEtal2023MNRAS.525.4735T . The joint IXPE and NuSTAR broadband spectroscopic data fitting gives also the estimate of disk mass accretion rate to the central SMBH which is 0.013 M_ Edd , while the halo accretion rate is 0.4 M_ Edd . The location of the shock changed from 28 to 40 r_s and the shock compression ratio varied between 4.4-5.3 .

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes MCG-05-23-16 as a Seyfert 2 galaxy, finding BeppoSAX/ISGRI parameters consistent with prior work, no significant flux variation, and a well-constrained primary continuum.

quotes.quote: Table 4: Spectral Best-Fits for the Previously Published Sources intrinsic column density reflection parameter MCG-05-23-16 The broadband spectrum of this source has already been discussed by b29 and b23 using the same BeppoSAX data employed in this paper, with additional RXTE observations below 30 keV in the case of the Mattson and Weaver paper. However, only Risaliti takes into account the possible presence of a cut-off in the high energy spectrum of this source so that our results can be directly compared to his ones. Combining our ISGRI data with the BeppoSAX points provides a good fit (see Table 4) and a set of parameters which are substantially in agreement with those obtained by b29 , once the relative uncertainties and the use of a different inclination angle between the reflecting material and the line of sight (30 ^ in Risaliti and 63 ^ in the present work) are taken into account. Our choice of inclination is dictated by the classification of MCG-05-23-16 as a Seyfert 2 galaxy, since this type of objects are more likely to be seen edge-on.

Fixing the angle to 30 ^ provides an equally good fit with parameters similar to those reported in Table 4, except for a slightly higher energy cut-off energy (124 keV) and a stronger reflection (R=0.9). In both cases the reflection we obtain is higher than the one measured by b29 and b23 . To account for a possible cross-calibration mismatch between ISGRI and MECS as well as for flux variations between the two observing periods, the constant factor C has been added to the fit; since the value obtained for this constant is close to 1, we assume that no significant variation has occured in the source between BeppoSAX and INTEGRAL observing periods. In this source the primary continuum is well constrained both in terms of photon index and cut-off energy as evident in figure mcg_cont , where one parameter is plotted against the other.

sources.summary: The paper revises NuSTAR spectral modeling of MCG -5-23-16, finding reflection changes explain the spectra ratio and that there is no clear evidence for cut-off energy variation.

quotes.quote: MCG -5-23-16: fitted four NuSTAR (and simultaneous Swift XRT) spectra with cutoff powerlaw plus ionized reflection xillver and relativistic reflection component relxill . Galactic absorption with N_H=9 10^ 20 cm^ 2 and local galaxy absorption are included using the tbabs and ztbabs model. The model in xspec is tbabs * ztbabs * (cutoffpl+relxill+xillver) .

The decoupled approach yields no variation in E _c , but a change in the proportion of disc reflection, the latter explains the 20 -- 30 keV bump and high energy downward curvature in the spectra ratio plot.

We conclude that the claimed E _c variation pattern in literature (hotter-when-brighter) of MCG -5-23-16 is inconsistent with the spectra ratio plot. Our revised spectral modeling yields statistically improved fitting results. Such results agree with the spectra ratio, but suggest no E _c variation.

sources.summary: The paper presents MCG-05-23-16 as a nearby Seyfert 1.9 observed with IXPE/NuSTAR, constraining its X-ray polarization, coronal geometry, and possible alignment with the ionization cone.

quotes.quote: MCG-05-23-16 is a nearby ( z=0.0085 , 2003AJ....126.2268W ) Seyfert 1.9 galaxy 1980A&A....87..245V with broad emission lines in the infrared infr1994ApJ...422..521G . It is a relatively bright X-ray source ( F_ 2-10 =7-10 10^ 11 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , 2004ApJ...601..771M ) showing moderate cold absorption ( N_ H 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ). It has been widely studied in the X-ray band be2008A&A...492...93B,moli2013MNRAS.433.1687M , and its high energy cut-off ( E_ C ) and coronal physical parameters, i.e. temperature and Thomson optical depth, are quite well estimated ( 2015ApJ...800...62B ).

We obtained (at 68 per cent .l. for one interesting parameter) a polarization degree =1.6 0.7 per cent and a polarization angle =53 13 . This translate into a polarization degree upper limit (at 99 per cent .l.) =3.2 per cent. This represents a significant improvement with respect to the results obtained for the May 2022 observation alone.
Hubble Space Telescope 's WFPC2 images showed that the ionization cone of MCG-05-23-16 has a roughly 40 \ position angle, as probed by [ O iii ] emission hub2000ApJS..128..139F .

sources.summary: Suzaku observed MCG -5-23-16 as a Compton-thin Seyfert 1.9 with high X-ray flux and a spectrum showing a detected narrow iron K core.

quotes.quote: The Compton-thin Seyfert 1.9 galaxy MCG -5-23-16 (at z=0.008486 ) was observed by \ between 7--10 December 2005, for a total exposure time of 98.1 ks with XIS. The \ observation was performed simultaneously with \ and . The broad band spectral energy distribution from XIS and HXD is shown in Figure 2. The spectrum is plotted with respect to a baseline continuum model consisting of a power-law of =1.93 0.03 , absorbed by neutral matter of column density N_ H =1.65 0.03 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 .

Overall, the source flux of MCG 5-23-16 was high, 8.8 10^ 11 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 in the 2-10 keV band or 1.9 10^ 10 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 from 15-100 keV. Figure 2 also shows a close-up of the iron K line profile for MCG 5-23-16, notice the excellent agreement between the XMM-Newton EPIC-pn data and Suzaku XIS. The narrow iron K line core at 6.40 keV as well as the K line are both clearly detected, the narrow K core has an equivalent width of 70 eV and is unresolved in both \ XIS and \ ( <4600 km s ^ 1 FWHM), consistent with an origin in distant matter far from the black hole.

sources.summary: The paper classifies MCG--05-23-16 as a Seyfert 1.9 and argues its BLR extinction occurs on compact scales, making the torus the likely obscuring structure.

quotes.quote: MCG--05-23-16 : This object has been previously classified as a Seyfert 2 and as a near-infrared Seyfert 1 (where near-infrared, but not optical, broad lines are seen), but a careful subtraction of the narrow emission lines revealed a broad H line. No broad H is observed, however. Thus we classify this object as a Seyfert 1.9.

This implies the extinction to the BLR of MCG--05-23-16 occurs on a much smaller scale.The mid-IR emission in AGNs tracing the warm dust is typically located on scales < 50 pc for the AGNs in our luminosity range, and specifically in the case of MCG--05-23-016, the half-light radius of the mid-IR emission is 2 pc as found by interferometry leonard13 . Thus, in the case of MCG--05-23-16, considering the large difference between the extinction to the NLR and BLR, the fact that high spatial resolution observations showed that the dust filament crossing its nucleus causes only a small amount of extinction prieto14 and the size of the half-light radius of the mid-IR emission leonard13 , we argue the torus is the structure obscuring the BLR.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SWIFT J0947.6-3057 as a special Seyfert 2 galaxy: five NuSTAR spectra show strong reflection-strength and power-law-slope variability while absorption stays roughly constant.

quotes.quote: SWIFT J0947.6-3057 was observed five times with NuSTAR from July 2012 until March 2015. We fitted all the five spectra of the source with the baseline model (Sect. Sec:base_model ). The best-fit results are listed in Table tab:best_fit and plotted in Fig. fig:mcg_fit . SWIFT J0947.6-3057 is the only object of our sample that exhibits a strong variation in the power-law slope and reflection strength, while its absorption level remains roughly constant.
SWIFT J0947.6-3057 is a Seyfert 2 galaxy and a torus contribution to the reflection emission is not surprising. The predicted line is fully consistent with the observed values within the errors.

sources.summary: The paper studies MCG-5-23-16 as an obscured S0/Seyfert 1.9, noting its X-ray discovery, hard-X-ray brightness and variability, absorbed spectrum, and strong iron K fluorescence.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present a detailed study of the iron K emission from an obscured Seyfert galaxy MCG-5-23-16 using observations. MCG-5-23-16 is an S0 galaxy FWM00 first discovered in X-rays by Schnopperetal78 who also found its' optical spectrum to be that of a narrow emission-line galaxy at a redshift of 0.0083 . Veronetal80 classified this galaxy as a Seyfert 1.9 based on the possible presence of a broad component in H .

MCG-5-23-16 with a V-magnitude of 13.7 is one of the brightest Seyfert galaxies in hard X-rays and is also one of the few Seyferts detected at ray energies Bignamietal79,Pollocketal81 . Its 2-10 flux, measured to be 8 10^ 11 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 in 1978 Tennant83 , decreased by a factor of four in 1989 NP94 , and then increased to 9 10^ 11 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 in 1996 WKP98 before decreasing again to 7 10^ 11 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 in 2001 (this work). Previous X-ray observations of MCG -5-23-16 showed a highly absorbed X-ray spectrum ( 10^ 22 cm^ 2 , TP89 ) and a strong iron K fluorescence line with equivalent width of 300-400 SRV92,NP94,Weaveretal97,WKP98 .

sources.summary: INTEGRAL detected MCG --05--23--16 as part of its Galactic Plane AGN sample, but the source was too faint for ISGRI spectral extraction; BeppoSAX found comparable flux and a cutoff.

quotes.quote: We present results on approximately one year of INTEGRAL observations of six AGN detected during the regular scans of the Galactic Plane. The sample is composed by five Seyfert 2 objects ( MCG --05--23--16 , NGC 4945 , the Circinus galaxy, NGC 6300 , ESO 103--G35 ) and the radio galaxy Centaurus A. The continuum emission of each of these sources is well represented by a highly absorbed ( N_ H >10^ 22 cm^ 2 ) power law, with average spectral index = 1.9 0.3 .

The low detection significance of MCG --05--23--16 does not allow spectral extraction. MCG--05--23--16 For MCG--05--23--16 only a comparison of the flux level is possible, and this shows that BeppoSAX caught the source at about the same level as INTEGRAL did, with a flux of 6.7 and 4.0 10^ 11 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 in the 20--40 and 40--60 keV bands, respectively. The PDS spectrum alone is best fitted by an absorbed power law with photon index = 1.4 0.3 and cut-off at E_c = 60^ +56 _ 21 keV.

sources.summary: The paper applies the uneven-sampling lag method to MCG-5-23-16, finding Suzaku lag-energy spectra that match XMM-Newton results and support relativistic reverberation near its black hole.

quotes.quote: In this section, we discuss the application of the above method to calculate time lags in Suzaku observations of two sources: NGC 4151 and MCG-5-23-16. Time delays have been seen in this two objects using XMM-Newton and standard lag calculation methods 2012MNRAS.422..129Z,2013ApJ...767..121Z .

Fig. fig:data shows the lag-energy plots for NGC 4151 (left) and MCG-5-23-16 (right), along with plots from previously published XMM-Newton data 2012MNRAS.422..129Z,2013ApJ...767..121Z . Each point in the plots is a result of maximizing the likelihood function for the power spectra and phase lags between individual light curves and the total 2-10 keV light curves, excluding the current energies see for details on lag-energy plots 2012MNRAS.422..129Z . The plotted frequencies are 3 10^ 5 3 10^ 4 Hz and 10^ 5 3 10^ 4 Hz respectively. Although the uncertainties at the highest energies are relatively large, it is clear that there is a structure at 6-7 keV consistent that seen in the XMM-Newton data. The match between the Suzaku and XMM-Newton plots in the case of MCG-5-23-16 (Fig. fig:data right) is remarkable.

We applied the method to Suzaku archival observations of NGC 4151 and MCG-5-23-16, and showed that their lag-energy spectra are consistent with those observed using XMM-Newton, giving further support to their interpretations as being due to relativistic reverberation close to the black holes.

sources.summary: The paper studies MCG--5-23-16 with Suzaku, XMM-Newton, and NuSTAR, detecting iron-line reverberation and the first AGN Compton-hump lag above 10 keV.

quotes.quote: MCG--5-23-16 is one of the first AGN where relativistic reverberation in the iron K line originating in the vicinity of the supermassive black hole was found, based on a short XMM- Newton observation. In this work, we present the results from long X-ray observations using Suzaku , XMM- Newton and NuSTAR designed to map the emission region using X-ray reverberation. A relativistic iron line is detected in the lag spectra on three different time-scales, allowing the emission from different regions around the black hole to be separated. Using NuSTAR coverage of energies above 10 keV reveals a lag between these energies and the primary continuum, which is detected for the first time in an AGN. This lag is a result of the Compton reflection hump responding to changes in the primary source in a manner similar to the response of the relativistic iron K line.

sources.summary: The paper treats MCG--5-23-16 as a bright, moderately absorbed Seyfert galaxy with soft excess, absorption, and prior broad Fe K-line detections.

quotes.quote: MCG--5-23-16 is a moderately absorbed Seyfert galaxy of intermediate type (Sy 1.9). It is relatively nearby at a redshift of z=0.0085 , and with a typical 2-10 flux of F_ 2-10 8 10^ 11 it is one of the brightest known Seyfert galaxies Reeves2006 . The source has been observed previously to have a soft excess below 1 and an absorbing column of N_ H 10^ 22 Dewangan2003,Balestra2004 , and ASCA observations have indicated the presence of a broad Fe K line of EW 200 Weaver1997,Weaver1998 . The line was successfully modeled with a narrow core at the rest-frame energy of 6.4 and a broad component superposed on it.

sources.summary: MCG-5-23-16 is one of the well-covered AGNs where the authors find subparsec 12 micron emission consistent with a near-circular rather than elongated shape.

quotes.quote: Two other objects, NGC4507 and MCG-5-23-16 with a reasonably well mapped ( u,v ) coverage and good signal-to-noise ratios, appear to have a less elongated 12 m emission.

For MCG-5-23-16 and NGC4507, on the other hand, the 12 m emission at subparsec scales is consistent with a near circular shape, see Fig. fig:results2 . The best fit axis ratios are 1.3 ^ +0.5 and 1.2 ^ +0.3 for the two sources respectively.
However, we also find two sources (NGC4507 and MCG-5-23-16) which are compatible with non-elongated, i.e. circular emission.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes MCG-5-23-16 as a bright X-ray Seyfert AGN, detecting asymmetry in its narrow Fe K line and locating the emission at roughly 200-650 gravitational radii.

quotes.quote: Here, we present an analysis of the narrow component of the Fe K line in the Seyfert AGN MCG-5-23-16, one of the brightest AGN in X-rays and in Fe K emission, to localize the emitting region. Spectra derived from Chandra/HETGS observations show asymmetry in the narrow Fe K line, which has only been confirmed before in the AGN NGC 4151. Models including relativistic Doppler broadening and gravitational redshifts are preferred over simple Gaussians and measure radii consistent with R 200-650 _g . These results are consistent with those of NGC 4151 and indicate that the narrow Fe K line in MCG-5-23-16 is primarily excited in the innermost part of the optical broad line region (BLR), or X-ray BLR.

sources.summary: MCG-5-23-16 is described as a marginal but plausible excess case with a relativistically broad iron line and strong red-wing variability.

quotes.quote: A further four show lower-confidence (80-95 ) excesses only, but in the cases of MCG-5-23-16(2) and NGC 4051 these might well be real, as they exhibit more than one bin with such an excess.
NGC 3516(2) and NGC 5548(2) are the clearest cases, along with NGC 4151(2) and MCG-5-23-16(2) showing narrower or more marginal excesses.
MCG-5-23-16(2) and NGC 3516(2) are different in that they possess relativistically broad iron lines, as well as having strong variability excesses across the red wing.

sources.summary: The atlas gives MCG-5-23-16's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: MCG-5-23-16 is an early-type galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0085 ( D 39.5 Mpc) with an AGN classified as a Sy 1.9 wilson_kinematics_1985 with broad emission lines in the near-infrared goodrich_infrared_1994 . It possesses an extended NLR on each side of the nucleus ( 1 190 pc; PA 40 ; ferruit_hubble_2000 ). MCG-5-23-16 was extensively studied in the X-rays and belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample.

sources.summary: The paper compares MCG-5-23-16 as a radio-quiet galaxy with low cut-off energy, obscured accretion, variable photon index, and coronal values similar to NGC 5273.

quotes.quote: Using the and /XRT simultaneous observations of the radio-quiet galaxy MCG-5-23-16, zo17 found the cut-off energy in the range 107-152 keV and showed that the cut-off energy increases with the increase in the hard X-ray flux. Although the source is obscured, MCG-5-23-16 is accreting at 2-3 per cent of Eddington mass accretion rate (assuming 2-10 keV unabsorbed X-ray flux of 3-8 10 ^ 11 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 zo14 , the black hole mass of 3 10 ^7 M wa86 and the redshift of 0.0085 wa86 ). The photon power-law index varies in the range 1.78-1.85.

sources.summary: The appendix treats MCG-5-23-16 as a non-detection: residuals align somewhat, but peaks appear to be over-fitting and literature velocities do not improve the match.

quotes.quote: MCG-5-23-16 is an interesting case where the normalized residuals show considerable alignment with the variability features. However, when examining by eye, one observes that they are not clear peaks, merely manifestations of over-fitting. Moreover, the outflow measurements of 0.118c and 0.116 0.004c, found by Tombesi10, Tombesi11 do not match with any higher significance compared to the one depicted on Figure B6.

sources.summary: The paper says MCG-05-23-016 has a 57 pc nuclear spectral scale and only tentative 6 micrometer water-ice detection because it is the least obscured, molecular-gas-poor AGN.

quotes.quote: We note that the nuclear spectra of MCG-05-23-016, NGC 5506, NGC 5728, NGC 7172, NGC 7319, and ESO 137-G034 correspond to physical scales of 57, 55, 53, 38, 55, 116 and 52 pc (at 6 m), respectively.
This feature is only tentatively detected in the case of MCG-05-23-016, which is the source with the lowest N _H^ X-ray in the sample and is poor in molecular gas Rosario18 .
This feature is only tentatively detected in the case of MCG-05-23-016, which is the least obscured AGN in the sample (log N _ H ^ X-Ray (cm ^ 2 ) 22 ).

sources.summary: MCG-5-23-16 shows broad Brackett emission and redshifted lines; its line ratios do not match Galactic extinction, possibly because non-simultaneous measurements sampled BLR variability.

quotes.quote: In MCG-5-23-16, both lines are redshifted by 80 km/s, possibly indicating an inaccuracy of the (optical-based) NED redshift.

For MCG-5-23-16 the location is not even consistent with the Galactic extinction, because of the very low Br /Pa ratio. This might be due to variability of the BLR, since Br (this paper) and Pa (Veilleux et al. ) were not measured simultaneously but with a time lag of 9 years.

gmw_00000135.name: MCG-6-30-15
gmw_00000135.overview: MCG-6-30-15 is an elongated lenticular Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0211453, arXiv:astro-ph/0608502] featuring a dust lane [arXiv:astro-ph/0211453] and a counter-rotating core [arXiv:2105.06867]. Its active galactic nucleus is characterized by a rapidly rotating supermassive black hole [arXiv:1105.4889, arXiv:2007.15914] and highly variable X-ray emission [arXiv:1112.0172, arXiv:1310.1945].

sources.summary: The paper treats MCG-6-30-15 as a complex Seyfert spectrum: its fits remain formally poor, and He-like absorption leaves uncertainty in derived broad iron-line parameters.

quotes.quote: Despite the multiple complexities, the fits to MCG-6-30-15(1,2) and NGC 7469(2) remain unacceptable. It should be noted that these spectra have very high signal-to-noise ratio, so that any residual systematic uncertainty e.g. in the instrumental calibration may contribute to the poor fit. Also, while these spectra have formally poor fits, none is ``catastrophic" by our previous definition ( _ ^ 2 >2.0 ).

In three observations of two objects, NGC 3516(2) and MCG-6-30-15(1,2) we see evidence for absorption by the He-like species. This implies a very highly ionized zone of gas in the line--of--sight which is not well modeled by our XSTAR models. The inability of our absorption models to account for this feature in these two cases is perhaps unsurprising, given that the strength of absorption lines in the spectra is heavily dependent on the turbulent velocity of the gas. We have assumed this to be 100 km s ^ 1 in our models, but there is no reason to think that this might in reality be larger and hence the features stronger. Because we have not modeled the absorption features from the ionized gas in these observations, there will always be some doubt as to the exact broad iron line parameters we have derived, and this should be borne in mind when these parameters are interpreted.

sources.summary: The paper models MCG--6-30-15 as an active galaxy whose X-ray spectrum has ionized absorption and a broad iron excess consistent with relativistic disc reflection.

quotes.quote: MCG--6-30-15 Having demonstrated that the line profile observed in \ has an astrophysical origin, and that the data significantly prefer the interpretation in which relativistic effects dominate, we now consider the case of the active galaxy . Continuum Modelling We began by modelling the spectrum of \ with the standard AGN powerlaw-like Comptonised continuum. However, the 0.5--10.0 \ X-ray spectra of active galaxies are rarely featureless. Type 1 AGN commonly display additional emission at soft energies (see Gierlin04 , Crummy06 , Miniutti09 ).

As \ is an extragalactic source, we initially allowed for both Galactic and intrinsic absorption with two \ components, one local component and the other at the redshift of \ ( z = 0.007749; Fisher95 ). The Galactic column used was 3.92 10^ 20 , based on recent 21 cm measurements ( NH ), while the intrinsic column was free to vary. However, in the course of the modelling we found that the intrinsic neutral component was not required, and so was removed. As has been widely reported in previous work (see Ballantyne03b, Young05, Miller08 ; Chiang11 ), the spectrum of \ also displays clear signatures of ionised absorption.

sources.summary: The paper models X-ray polarisation for MCG-6-30-15, contrasting relativistic reflection and complex absorption explanations for its broad Fe K line.

quotes.quote: We present modelling of X-ray polarisation spectra emerging from the two competing scenarios that are proposed to explain the broad Fe K line in the Seyfert 1 galaxy MCG-6-30-15. The polarisation signature of complex absorption is studied for a partial covering scenario using a clumpy wind and compared to a reflection model based on the lamp-post geometry. The shape of the polarisation percentage and angle as a function of photon energy are found to be distinctly different between the reflection and the absorption case. Relativistic reflection produces significantly stronger polarisation in the 1--10 keV energy band than absorption.

The results plotted in Fig. Fig2 show that, in principle, X-ray polarisation can distinguish between the relativistic reflection and the absorption for the broad iron line in MCG-6-30-15. Practically no polarisation signal should be measured in the 1--5 keV band for the absorption case and in the 10--50 keV band, the polarisation due to relativistic reflection is much higher than for the absorption scenario. The variation of the polarisation angle gives an additional handle on the scenario because the absorption model produces zero variation of while the reflection model induces smooth and characteristic variation of with photon energy. A small X-ray polarimetry mission such as the X-ray Imaging Polarimetry Explorer (XIPE) being currently evaluated by the European Space Agency could already constrain the soft X-ray polarisation of MCG-6-30-15.

sources.summary: The paper studies XMM-Newton time lags in MCG-6-30-15, finding hard and soft lags, no clear iron K lag, and linking its variability to reverberation plus coronal changes.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage 2014 MCG-6-30-15 is one of the most observed Narrow Line Seyfert 1 galaxies in the X-ray band. In this paper we examine the X-ray time lags in this source using a total of 600 ks in observations (440 ks exposure) taken with the XMM-Newton telescope (300 ks in 2001 and 300 ks in 2013). Both the old and new observations show the usual hard lag that increases with energy, however, the hard lag turns over to a soft lag at frequencies below 10^ 4 Hz. The highest frequencies ( 10^ 3 Hz) in this source show a clear soft lag, as previously presented for the first 300 ks observation, but no clear iron K lag is detected in either the old or new observation. The soft lag is more significant in the old observation than the new.

The observations are consistent with a reverberation interpretation, where the soft, reflected emission is delayed with respect to the hard powerlaw component. These spectral timing results suggest that two distinct variability mechanisms are important in this source: intrinsic coronal variations (which lead to correlated variability in the reprocessed emission), and geometrical changes in the corona. Variability due to geometrical changes does not result in correlated variability in the reflection, and therefore inhibits the clear detection of an iron K lag. black hole physics -- galaxies: active -- X-rays: galaxies -- galaxy: individual : MCG-6-30-15.

sources.summary: The paper monitors MCG-6-30-15 across X-ray, optical, and near-IR bands, linking lags and variability to an accretion disk, possible truncation, and a torus.

quotes.quote: We present long term monitoring of MCG-6-30-15 in X-rays, optical and near-IR wavelengths, collected over five years of observations. We determined the power spectral density of all the observed bands and find that the host contribution needs to be taken into account to obtain reasonable results. The lag determined between the X-ray Q flux and the optical bands is consistent with zero days, while the lags between optical and near-IR bands correspond to values in the 10 to 20 day range. Filtering the light curves in frequency space shows that most of the correlation is due to the fastest variability.

It is also found that the lags agree with the relation ^ 4/3 , as expected for an optically thick geometrically thin accretion disc, although for a larger disc than that predicted by the measured black hole mass and accretion rate in MCG-6-30-15. We find some evidence for a truncation of the disc at a distance of 15 light-days. Indirect evidence suggests that the torus might located at 20 \ R_g from the central source. This implies an AGN bolometric luminosity of 3 10^ 43 ergs/s/cm ^2 .

sources.summary: The paper finds MCG--6-30-15 has a flat iron-line versus continuum lag spectrum, acceptable constant-delay fits, and a very low fitted X-ray source height.

quotes.quote: The observed time-lag spectra of 1H 0707--495, MCG--6-30-15, and Mrk 766 are flat. The fit is thus contrived for these sources, in the sense that the best-fit s value is 0 . In Col. 3 of Table table3 we therefore list only the upper limit on s . We re-fitted the observed time-lag spectra of these sources to a constant delay (i.e. we set s=0 ). The resulting fit is statistically acceptable ( ^2_ min / dof =15.9/13 , 19.0/24, and 19.5/14 for 1H 0707--495, MCG--6-30-15, and Mrk 766, respectively), and the best-fit normalization (i.e. the best-fit constant delay in this case) is A=-29^ +21 _ 20 sec , 21 9 sec and 71 19 sec for 1H 0707--495, MCG--6-30-15, and Mrk 766, respectively.

Column 5 of Table table3 lists our best-fit results for h . The X-ray source height is well defined only for Mrk 766 and Mrk 335. For 1H 0707--495 and MCG--6-30-15 the best-fit h values are 4r_ g and 2.3r_ g (which is the lowest allowed fitting value for h ), respectively.

sources.summary: The paper derives MCG-06-30-15 bulge dispersion from spatially resolved kinematics, excluding the AGN-contaminated center and a SINFONI artifact while using the remaining effective-radius region.

quotes.quote: Spatially resolved stellar kinematics are available for NGC 5273, from the ATLAS ^ 3D survey of early-type galaxies in the northern hemisphere http://www-astro.physics.ox.ac.uk/atlas3d/ Cappellari11,Cappellari13 , and for MGC-06-30-15, from the work of . Following the method of B17, _ is determined for these galaxies by taking an error-weighted average of the values for each spaxel within a circular aperture defined by the effective radius, r_ e . Accurate measurements of r_ e are available from the works of for NGC 5273, and for MGC-06-30-15. They are determined from detailed surface brightness decompositions of Hubble Space Telescope (HST) images, using GALFIT Peng02,Peng10 . The AGN is isolated from the galaxy surface brightness features, and substructure such as bars and disks are accounted for.

For MCG-06-30-15, we follow and exclude the central 0 1 from our calculation of _ , as the noise associated with the AGN continuum precludes secure measurement of the stellar kinematics. Furthermore, the kinematic map in Figure fig:MCG63015 shows that a small region within r_ e ( 1 01 ) was cut off ( y 0 6 , the bottom of the map), due to an illumination artifact in the SINFONI data (see Raimundo13 for details). We assume that the kinematics within r_ e are well represented by the region that remains, and determine _ without applying any correction.

sources.summary: The paper reports a possible one-hour X-ray QPO in the NLSy1 galaxy MCG--06--30--15, with a 3670 s period, 16% modulation, persistent WWZ signal, and broad-iron-line SMBH context.

quotes.quote: We found a possible 1 hour quasi-periodic oscillation (QPO) in a 55 ks X-ray observation of the narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy MCG--06--30--15 made with the XMM-Newton EPIC/pn detector in the energy range 0.3 -- 10 keV. We identify a total modulation of 16 in the light curve and find a 3670 s quasi-period using Lomb-Scargle periodogram (LSP) and weighted wavelet Z-transform (WWZ) techniques. Our analyses of eight light curves of MCG--06--30--15, indicated the possible presence of an oscillation during one of them. The LSP indicates a statistically significant ( 3 ) QPO detection. A WWZ analysis shows that the signal at this possible roughly 3670 s period is present, and rather persistent, throughout the observation; however, a signal around 8735 s is more persistent.

We report a third probable QPO detection in a NLSy1 for MCG--06--30--15 ( z = 0.00775 ), once again using XMM-Newton data. MCG--06--30--15 is well known, particularly for its broad iron K line; this line that provides strong evidence for the presence of a supermassive black hole (SMBH; e.g., \ Tanaka et al. \ 1995), which is probably spinning rapidly (e.g., \ Iwasawa et al. \ 1996). Because its H line width (FWHM: full width at half maxima) of 1933 82 km s ^ 1 is < 2000 km s ^ 1 (Hu et al. \ 2016), this SMBH is classified as a NLSy1.

sources.summary: The paper classifies MCG--6-30-15 as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy and finds reverberation timing is complicated by variable warm absorbers and UFO-related spectral changes.

quotes.quote: Wavelet spectral timing analysis was used to explore the time variability of X-ray reverberation signals from the inner accretion disc in the AGN IRAS 13224 3809 and MCG--6-30-15. These are both classified as narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxies that have been noted for strong relativistically broadened iron K lines in their X-ray spectra, attributed to the reflection of the primary X-ray continuum from the inner disc fabian+2013, tanaka+95 . Alongside the broad iron K line, reverberation time lags are observed, consistent with the light travel time between a compact corona and the inner disc, are observed.

Despite the detection of reverberation in the soft X-ray band, the corresponding iron K reverberation lags are mysteriously not detected in IRAS 13224 3809 and MCG--6-30-15, using conventional Fourier spectral timing techniques that average the cross spectrum and phase lags over the course of long observations. Without the corresponding detection of the iron K reverberation lag, it is difficult to confirm that the soft X-ray lag arises due to reverberation from the inner disc, and not some other variable emission component in the soft X-ray band that is responding to changes in the continuum.

In MCG--6-30-15, there is not such a pronounced absorption feature from an ultrafast outflow, and the difference between the flux spectra between the time periods with and without reverberation is less clear. MCG--6-30-15 is, however, known to possess complex, variable warm absorbers, which are mildly ionised outflows with velocities around 1000 marinucci+2014,kammoun+2017 , in addition to evidence in the spectral variability for an ultrafast outflow igo+2020 . We can see in Fig. fig:spectra:mcg that the greatest difference in these flux spectra is the presence of the emission line-like feature at 6.8 .

sources.summary: The paper presents MCG-6-30-15 as an elongated lenticular Seyfert 1 with a dust lane, red central region, old bulge population, and nuclear spectral dilution by dust or AGN continuum.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert 1 MCG-6-30-15 is a very elongated lenticular galaxy. Colour maps show a small dust lane south of the nucleus roughly parallel to the major axis of the galaxy. The central region of the galaxy is much redder than the outer regions (Ferruit et al., 2000). From a stellar population study based on ultraviolet spectra, Bonatto et al. (2000) claim that an old bulge stellar population is the dominant contributor, with indications of a series of previous bursts of star formation.

The logarithmic flux profile shows a distinct nucleus on a weak, extended galaxy component (Fig. 2). In Figure 7a we show spectra of the nucleus (1.5 arcsec wide; 1'' 150pc) and that of an extended region formed by averaging together similar data extracted from both sides of the nucleus, 1.5 arcsec away. The nuclear and circumnuclear regions have similar spectral features although these are weaker in the nucleus. The nuclear spectrum is also redder.

sources.summary: The paper models X-ray time delays in MCG-6-30-15 as delayed reflection from a flare, favoring cold reprocessing blobs over an ionized disk surface.

quotes.quote: We propose a reflection model of the time delays detected during an exceptionally bright, single flare in MCG-6-30-15. We consider a scenario in which the delays of the hard X-rays with respect to the soft X-rays are caused by the presence of the delayed reflection component. We employ a model of the flare, which is accompanied by reprocessed emission. We consider two geometries/thermal states of the reprocessing medium: a partially ionized accretion disk surface and a distribution of magnetically confined, cold blobs.

The reprocessing by cold blobs predicts positive time delays and a saturation in the time delay -- energy relation, which is likely present in the data. The model requires a strong reflection component and relies on the apparent pivoting of the combined primary and reflected spectrum. The reflection by the ionized disk surface does not reproduce the observed delays. We discuss the relation between the two reflection scenarios and argue that they are both present in MCG-6-30-15.

sources.summary: The paper models MCG 6 30-15 as a Seyfert I galaxy whose X-ray red wing and spectral variability can be explained by multi-zone absorption and partial covering.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert I galaxy MCG 6 30-15 shows one of the best examples of a broad ``red wing'' of emission in its X-ray spectrum at energies 2 < E < 6.4 keV, commonly interpreted as being caused by relativistically-blurred reflection close to the event horizon of the black hole. We aim to test an alternative model in which absorption creates the observed spectral shape, explains the puzzling lack of variability of the red wing and reduces the high reflection albedo, substantially greater than unity, that is otherwise inferred at energies E > 20 keV. We compiled all the available long-exposure, high-quality data for MCG 6 30-15: 522 ks of hetgs , 282 ks of pn / rgs and 253 ks of xis / pin data. This is the first analysis of this full dataset. We investigated the spectral variability on timescales >20 ks using principal components analysis and fitted spectral models to ``flux state'' and mean spectra over the energy range 0.5-45 keV (depending on detector). The absorber model was based on the zones previously identified in the high-resolution grating data. Joint fits were carried out to any data that were simultaneous. Multiple absorbing zones covering a wide range of ionisation are required by the grating data, including a highly ionised outflowing zone. A variable partial-covering zone plus absorbed low-ionisation reflection, distant from the source, provides a complete description of the variable X-ray spectrum. A single model fits all the data.

We conclude that these zones are responsible for the red wing, its apparent lack of variability, the absorption structure around the Fe K line, the soft-band ``excess'' and the high flux seen in the hard X-ray band. A relativistically-blurred Fe line is not required in this model. We suggest the partial covering zone is a clumpy wind from the accretion disk. accretion; galaxies: active The galaxy MCG 6 30-15 is a well-known Seyfert type I at redshift z=0.00775

sources.summary: The paper uses MCG-6-30-15 as a well-sampled AGN example, placing a lower limit on its longer timescale and successfully modeling its X-ray light curve variability.

quotes.quote: However, for MCG-6-30-15, as with all other remaining AGN in our sample, there is still considerable probability on values of the longer time scale that are longer than the span of the lightcurve. As a result, we can only place a lower limit on the longer characteristic time scale, as we are only able to state with confidence that _L is not less than some value. For MCG-6-30-15, we find a lower limit of _L 37 days.

As an example, in Figure f-postcheck1 we compare the lightcurve of MCG-6-30-15 with the running average for the best-fitting mixed OU process. There do not appear to be any systematic trends in the residuals accross the lightcurve. In addition, in Figure f-postcheck2 we compare the periodogram of the lightcurve for MCG-6-30-15 with that expected from the best-fitting mixed OU process, after incorporating the uncertainty in the model parameters. We used the Lomb-Scargle periodogram for the RXTE data because of its irregular time sampling, while we used the standard discrete Fourier transform for the XMM data. The mixed OU process, with weights given by the bending power-law approximation (Eq. [ eq-plaw_weights ] ), provides a good description of the X-ray lightcurve of MCG-6-30-15, being able to model the variability amplitude of luminosity fluctuations across a range of time scales.

sources.summary: The chapter uses MCG-6-30-15 as a Seyfert case study for broad Fe K emission, light-bending interpretations, and competing complex absorption models.

quotes.quote: Observations by ASCA presented by showed the first clear example of a very skewed Fe K emission line in the Seyfert galaxy MCG-6-30-15. The very broad line was inferred to be due to reprocessing in the very inner parts of the disc by an X-ray source of emission very compact and very close to the disc .

The temporal behaviour of the broadened Fe K line in the Seyfert galaxy MCG-6-30-15 also gave puzzling results to interpret , in particular uncorrelated variability of the Fe K line and continuum flux emission. The puzzle can be solved assuming a very low height of the X-ray continuum emission source above the reflecting material, which is very close to ISCO around a maximally rotating BH; in this case, light bending due to the strong SMBH gravitational field explains the observed temporal and spectral properties of the broadened Fe K line in MCG-6-30-15 .

The archetypal broad Fe K emission line of MCG-6-30-15 can be reproduced in complex absorption scenarios, with layers of gas only partially covering the X-ray continuum emission source , albeit with a contrived geometry . Broadband and deep X-ray observations of MCG-6-30-15 performed with NuSTAR and XMM-Newton still did not allow to rule out the complex absorption scenario, although the relativistically blurred reflection is preferred on a statistical ground . While a maximally rotating BH is almost invariably inferred to be in action in MCG-6-30-15 from spectral fits performed within a lamp-post model, this scenario is ruled out by who consider the effects of absorption in the Fe K band before modelling the relativistic emission line.

sources.summary: The paper uses MCG-6-30-15 as a Seyfert-galaxy case for broad Fe K line profiles and magnetic-field limits, noting X-ray observations, variability, and accretion-disc modeling.

quotes.quote: E.g., the maximum velocity is about v 80000 - 100000 km/s for the galaxy MCG--6--30--15 tanaka1,Fabian02 and v 48000 km/s for MCG--5--23--16 krolik1 . For both galaxies line profiles are known rather well. analyzed results of long-time observations of MCG-6-30-15 using XMM-Newton and BeppoSAX . They confirmed in general the qualitative conclusions about the features of the Fe K_ line, which were discovered by ASCA satellite. yaqoob02 discussed the essential importance of ASCA calibrations and the reliability of obtained results.

Similar to considerations presented in paper by , analyzing Fe K shapes for MCG-6-30-15 galaxy and using ASCA data tanaka1 one could evaluate a magnetic field for this case; namely a magnetic field should be less than 5 10^ 10 G and the estimate is independent on a character of accretion flow. So, we could use the estimate for non-flat accretion flows for MCG-6-30-15 Seyfert galaxy and generalize conclusions by for more general cases of accretion flows.

sources.summary: The paper uses MCG-6-30-15 as a Seyfert 1 case study, constraining warm-absorber density, ionization, oxygen-edge behavior, and coronal-line equivalent widths.

quotes.quote: This result is obtained for the mean observed Seyfert 1 features, as well as for the case study of MCG-6-30-15 . It implies that the distance of the WA from the incident radiation source is of the order of that of the Broad Line Region (BLR).

It is interesting to apply our computations to the Seyfert 1 galaxy MCG-6-30-15 , for which several emission lines have been measured, as well as the optical depths of the O vii and O viii edges. With ASCA, Reynolds et al. ( ) observed in this object that the optical depth at the O viii edge ( _ O viii ) responds to continuum changes with a characteristic time-scale of about 10^ 4 s, whereas the optical depth of O vii ( _ O vii ) seems to be almost constant during all the observations. Otani et al. ( ) made the hypothesis that the WA could consist of two different components: the inner absorber which has a high ionization, responsible in large part of the O viii edge and located in or just outside the BLR (R < 10 ^ 17 cm), and the outer absorber mainly responsible of the O vii edge, less ionized, and located near the molecular torus (R > 1 pc).
Data for the EWs of iron coronal lines are taken from Reynolds et al. ( ). They gave EW( [ Fe x ] )=3.0 0.4 , EW( [ Fe xi ] )=3.0 0.7 and EW( [ Fe xiv ] )=5.2 0.4 .

sources.summary: The review cites MCG--6-30-15 as a Seyfert galaxy with a broad iron line, high inferred spin, no relevant intermediate-ionization iron absorption, and quasi-constant reflection.

quotes.quote: The broad iron line and reflection hump are clearly seen in the Seyfert galaxy MCG--6-30-15 and in the GBH J1650-400 (Fig. 4). More recently it has been realised that the soft excess in many AGN can be well explained by smeared reflection (Crummy et al 2005). The extent of the blurring of the reflection spectrum is determined by the innermost radius of the disk (Fig. 4). Assuming that this is the radius of marginal stability then enables the spin parameter a of the hole to be measured. Objects with a very broad iron line like MCG--6-30-15 are inferred to have high spin a>0.95 (Dabrowski et al 1997, Brennemann et al 2006).

Observations of MCG--6-30-15 fail to show absorption lines or feature associated with iron of intermediate ionization. Such gas could cause some curvature of the appearent continuum mimicking a very broad line.

In the best objects where a very broad line is seen (e.g. MCG--6-30-15 Fabian et al 2002; NGC4051 Ponti et al 2006) the reflection appears to change little despite large variations in the continuum. The spectral variability can be decomposed into a highly variable power-law and a quasi-constant reflection component.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes MCG--6-30-15 as a nearby Seyfert galaxy whose X-ray spectrum favors relativistic reflection over an absorption-dominated model for the broad iron line and hard X-ray hump.

quotes.quote: Complexities in the X-ray spectrum of the nearby Seyfert 1.2 galaxy MCG--6-30-15 are commonly interpreted in terms of a broad iron line and the associated Compton reflection hump from the innermost relativistic regions of an accretion disk around a rapidly spinning black hole. However, an alternative model has recently been proposed in which these spectral features are caused entirely by complex (ionized and partial-covering) absorption. By considering the fluorescent emission that must accompany photoelectric absorption, we show that the absorption-dominated model over-predicts the 6.4 keV iron line flux unless the marginally Compton-thick absorber responsible for the hard X-ray hump satisfies very restrictive geometric constraints.

The X-ray spectrum of the nearby Seyfert 1.2 galaxy MCG--6-30-15 displays the archetypal relativistic broad iron line (Tanaka et al. 1995). The line shape is well explained by relativistic blurring (Fabian et al 1989) of the X-ray reflection spectrum expected from the inner accretion disc around the central black hole. The red wing of the line is so broad that extreme redshifts are required indicating that the inner radius of the disc is close to the black hole and thus that the hole is rapidly spinning (Dabrowski et al 1997; Brenneman Reynolds 2006).

sources.summary: The review notes MCG-6-30-15 has a low-frequency PSD slope of -0.8 and discusses a variable-height lamppost model for its continuum and iron-line variability.

quotes.quote: In NGC 4051 NGC 4051 mch04 and MCG-6-30-15 MCG-6-30-15 mch05 the slope of =1 continues for at least 4 decades below the upper bend and in a number of other AGN (e.g. NGC 3227 NGC 3227 , ) the slope can be traced for at least 3 decades. In such cases we can be reasonably sure that a hard state PSD is ruled out and a soft state PSD is required.

Small differences in normalisation (e.g. compare NGC 3227 and NGC 5506 uttleymch05 ) or in PSD slopes (the low frequency slope in MCG-6-30-15 is -0.8 whereas in NGC 4051 it is -1.0 mch05,mch04 ) can easily move the derived bend frequency by half a decade, or more, which is sufficient to blur any dependence on accretion rate.

We note that a variable height lamppost model has been invoked to explain continuum variability coupled with much lower variability of the iron line in MCG-6-30-15 . When the source is close to the disc, a greater fraction of the continuum is gravitationally bent back to the disc or into the black hole, thereby reducing the flux as seen by a distant observer but not altering the reprocessed iron line flux greatly. Although a reasonable explanation of spectral variability, this model cannot explain the rms-flux relationship where the observed variability is least at the lowest continuum fluxes.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes MCG --6-30-15 X-ray absorption, identifying two intrinsic outflow systems, a local absorber, and constraints on the fast component near the active nucleus.

quotes.quote: X-Ray Absorption Analysis of MCG --6-30-15: Discerning Three Kinematic Systems Tomer Holczer 1 , Ehud Behar 2,3 , and Nahum Arav 4 1 Department of Physics, Technion, Haifa 32000, Israel. tomer@physics.technion.ac.il (TH), behar@physics.technion.ac.il (EB). 2 Code 662, NASA / Goddard Space Flight Center, Greenbelt, MD 20771, USA 3 Senior NPP Fellow on leave from the Technion, Israel 4 Department of Physics, Virginia Tech, Blacksburg, Va 24061. arav@vt.edu AMD of Holczer et al. By analyzing the \ spectrum of \ obtained with the \ spectrometer on board the \ observatory, we identify three kinematically distinct absorption systems; two outflow components intrinsic to , and one local at z = 0 . The slow outflow at 100 \ 50 \ has a large range of ionization manifested by absorption from 24 different charge states of Fe, which enables a detailed reconstruction of the absorption measure distribution ( AMD ). This AMD spans five orders of magnitude in ionization parameter: 1.5 < < 3.5 ( ), with a total column density of N_H = (5.3 0.7) 10^ 21 .

The fast outflow at 1900 150 \ has a well defined ionization parameter with = 3.82 0.03 ( ) and column density N_H = 8.1 \ 0.7 10^ 22 . Assuming this component is a thin, uniform, spherical shell, it can be estimated to lie within 11 light days of the \ center. \ is a bright Seyfert 1 galaxy ( L_X 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ) at a redshift z = 0.007749 fisher95 .

sources.summary: The paper treats MCG--6--30--15 as a Seyfert, radio-quiet AGN with a likely maximally rotating SMBH, arguing its low black-hole mass prevents radio loudness.

quotes.quote: Note the location of the Seyfert galaxy MCG--6--30--15 on the left hand side of the plane, at a BH mass of 6 10^6 mchardy05 . This is an extremely important object, since it is probably the most compelling example of maximally spinning SMBH in AGN e.g iwasawa96,miniutti07 . A high BH spin has been often claimed to be the origin of radio-loudness blandfordsaasfee , but MCG--6--30--15 is radio quiet.

MCG--6--30--15 probably represents the best case of maximally rotating black hole in AGNs e.g. iwasawa96 , and it has often been used to contradict the ``spin paradigm''. The object is a well known radio quiet AGN hosted by a E/S0 galaxy, and its estimated black hole mass is 6 10^6 M _ mchardy05 , over 1 dex smaller than any radio loud AGN. We speculate that the reason why MCG--6--30--15 is radio-quiet ultimately resides in the fact that its back hole mass is not large enough to produce a radio loud AGN (i.e. to power a powerful relativistic jet), even if its BH is maximally rotating.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes MCG--6-30-15 as a complex absorbed Seyfert, with soft and hard excesses, Fe K emission and absorption, and residuals suggesting possible relativistically broadened emission.

quotes.quote: In this analysis we take the time averaged spectrum of MCG--6-30-15 during the January 2006 observations, preliminary analysis reveals that the dervied Fe K region parameters are consistent for all three observations and as such they are co-added as per Miniutti et al. (2007). Most apparent is the presence of a small soft and a hard excess, in addition to the requirement for some degree of warm absorption. A very poor fit to the data is found without any warm absorption with ^ 2 _ >60 .

There is strong neutral Fe K and what may be ionized Fe XXV emission improving the fit by ^ 2 =-331 and -51 respectively (see Table tab:parametrisation ); it is, however, possible the ionized feature may be the blue-wing to a broadened Fe K line profile. We also find ionized Fe XXVI emission which is fixed at 6.97 keV throughout the fits. In addition to this, very clear in the Fe K region are two ionized absorption features at 6.76^ +0.02 _ 0.02 keV and 7.05^ +0.02 _ 0.02 keV (see Figure fig:FeK_none ) indicative of the presence of a highly ionized absorber (as also found in a Chandra HETG observation of MCG--6-30-15 by Young et al. 2005).

sources.summary: The paper contrasts MCG--6-30-15 with NGC 5548, emphasizing its stronger broad Fe K line, lower black-hole mass, higher Eddington ratio, and relatively stronger hard X-ray output.

quotes.quote: Though the supermassive black hole in NGC 5548 is 15 times more massive than its counterpart in MCG--6-30-15, the latter has a higher Eddington ratio by a factor of 5 . The difference in optical luminosity between the two is nearly identical to their mass ratio, suggesting that the accretion disk is responsible for most of the radiative output in each nucleus. Yet NGC 5548 is only a factor of 4 times more luminous than MCG--6-30-15 in the 2-10 band, implying that the radiative output of the hard X-ray source (i.e., the inner disk/corona) is relatively weak in NGC 5548 compared to that from the rest of the disk.

However, the flatter power-law slope of NGC 5548 in the 2-10 band ( 1.69 vs. 2.18 for MCG--6-30-15) means that, relatively speaking, MCG--6-30-15 would have the higher flux at 2 , even if their integrated 2-10 fluxes were equal. This relative dearth in X-rays exhibited by NGC 5548 may be a reflection of the lower Eddington ratio of this source compared with MCG--6-30-15; in general, less accretion results in fewer seed photons that can be Comptonized by the corona, reducing the relative strength of the power-law continuum in this source. The difference in the optical luminosities of NGC 5548 and MCG--6-30-15 seems to scale linearly with their mass ratio, implying that the total number of seed photons per unit mass from the disk is the same in both cases.

sources.summary: The review describes MCG--6-30-15 as the first broad-iron-line spin case, where full reflection modeling restored and confirmed evidence for rapid black-hole spin.

quotes.quote: Early History The first quantitative attempt to constrain black spin was based on modeling of the broad iron line profile in the Seyfert galaxy MCG--6-30-15. A long ASCA observation of this source had caught its transition to the so-called ``Deep Minimum State" in which Iwasawa et al. (1996) noticed that the iron line was too broadened and redshifted to be explained by a non-rotating black hole. Subsequent modeling of this line profile by Dabrowski et al. (1997) concluded that a>0.95 under the assumption that the irradiation profile of the reflection follows a Novikov-Thorne profile.

Young, Ross Fabian (1998) showed that the need for rapid spin in MCG--6-30-15 was restored when one considered the full reflection continuum, showing that the Reynolds Begelman [ 1997 ] scenario predicted a strong, and unobserved, absorption edge from ionized iron. The first spin-analysis to follow the modern methodology (Section sec:methodology ) was by Brenneman Reynolds (2006), also for MCG--6-30-15. Using a long and high s/n XMM-Newton observation, they confirmed the rapid spin in MCG--6-30-15, obtaining a formal limit of a>0.98 (although finite disk-thickness effects can relax this limit to a>0.92 ; Reynolds Fabian [ 2008 ] ).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes MCG--06-30-15 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy whose X-ray variability is dominated by power-law changes, with weak reflected variability attributed to light bending near the black hole.

quotes.quote: MCG--06-30-15 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy, with a highly variable central X-ray source. It has been very well studied, and shows a strong, broad iron line feature Tanaka95 , along with an excess at soft energies, and a Compton hump at high energies Miniutti07 . This is indicative of a relativistic reflection spectrum, caused by the primary coronal emission hitting the accretion disk and causing fluorescent line emission. This spectrum is then blurred by relativistic effects, close to the event horizon. MCG--06-30-15 also shows evidence of complex absorption features, requiring multiple absorbing zones to describe them fully Otani96,Lee01,Turner03,Young05,Chiang11 .

We conclude that the observed relatively weak variability in the reflected component of the spectrum of MCG--06-30-15 is due to the effects of light-bending close to the event horizon of the black hole, and demonstrate that principal component analysis is an effective tool for analysing spectral variability in this regime.

sources.summary: The paper fits MCG-06-30-15 with a double-Gaussian broad-line profile, classifies it as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 from low line widths and ratios, and applies an average rotation correction.

quotes.quote: For fitting the BLR profiles, we used a single Gaussian model for some of AGN, but a second Gaussian profile was required to characterize the BLR profile for the following galaxies MCG-05-14-12, MCG-06-30-15, NGC 3783, NGC 4593, NGC 4235, NGC 6814 and NGC 7213. For the broad-line profiles that required double Gaussian models, both Gaussian profiles are combined with each other, and the resulting FWHM is estimated from the new, combined profile.

MCG-05-14-12 and MCG-06-30-15 both show low emission line widths (FWHM < 1700 km s ^ 1 ) and low [ O III ] /H ratios (0.2 and 0.9, respectively). According to the definition of narrow-line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) galaxies (FWHM < 2000 km s ^ 1 and [ O III ] /H < 3) reported by OsPo , we classify them as such.

However, We are still missing SINFONI observations for the following galaxies: MCG-05-14-12, NGC 4235, NGC 5128, and the spatially resolved stellar kinematics for NGC 3783, MCG-06-30-15. For these galaxies, the obtained stellar velocity dispersion estimates are reduced 10 the average galaxy rotation contribution to _ for the LLAMA sample (Shimizu et al. in preparation).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes MCG--06--30--15 as a bright Seyfert 1 AGN with broad iron-line and Compton-hump X-ray features suitable for reflection-spectroscopy tests.

quotes.quote: In this work, we analyze the X-ray spectra of the Seyfert 1 galaxy MCG--06--30--15 as observed simultaneously by XMM-Newton and NuSTAR telescopes in 2013.

The presence of a very prominent and broad iron line in the spectrum and the unprecedented high quality of simultaneous XMM-Newton and NuSTAR observations make the 2013 data of MCG--06--30--15 particularly suitable for our test.

MCG--06--30--15 is a bright Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN) in which a broad iron line was clearly detected by ASCA for the first time . The iron K line was extended to lower energies which indicates its origin in the innermost regions of the BH . MCG--06--30--15 has been observed by many X-ray missions like BeppoSAX , RXTE , XMM-Newton , Suzaku , and NuSTAR . The observation of MCG--06--30--15 by NuSTAR , along with the simultaneous XMM-Newton observation, displays a prominent Compton hump around 20-30 keV and the iron K line peaked at 6-7-keV.

sources.summary: The paper treats MCG-06-30-15 as a Seyfert 1.2 Sab galaxy with a counter-rotating core and gas; its nuclear CO kinematics are dominated by regular rotation.

quotes.quote: The early-type Sab galaxy MCG-06-30-15 is a Seyfert 1.2 galaxy ( 2014A&A...570A..13M ) located at a distance of 37 Mpc, z = 0.008 ( 2009ApJ...701..658W ). This active galaxy has a 400 pc diameter stellar kinematically distinct core (KDC) counter-rotating with respect to the main body of the galaxy ( raimundo2016tracing ). The molecular gas, traced by the H _2 2.12 m emission is also counter rotating with respect to the main stellar body of the galaxy, implying that the formation of the distinct core is associated with inflow of external gas into the centre of MCG-6-30-15 and the event that formed the counter-rotating core is also the main mechanism providing gas for the AGN fuelling. Moreover, this shows that external gas accretion is able to significantly replenish the fuelling reservoir in such active galaxies.

Pure rotation (flat disk) DiskFit model reproduces the observation well for MCG-06-30-15, leaving only very small residuals (see upper panels in Fig. Diskfit-mcg ), and this can be considered the best-fit model for this galaxy. For MCG-06-30-15, unlike NGC 4968, incorporating a radial component into a pure rotation DiskFit model (rotation plus radial motion model) does not reduce the residuals observed in the pure rotation model, indicating the absence of significant radial motions in the nuclear region of this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper argues MCG--6-30-15 has variable Granger lags, average lags similar to IRAS 13224--3809, and a corona whose geometry may be analogous.

quotes.quote: Previous studies based on the lag-frequency spectra suggested a compact corona for MCG--6-30-15 that is located at 3 r_ g Emmanoulopoulos2014, Epitropakis2016 . The extended corona model has never been adopted to formally fit their lag-frequency spectra before. However, our results conclude that the corona in MCG--6-30-15 should also be varied since we observe the variable Granger-lags as in IRAS 13224--3809 and 1H 0707--495.

Interestingly, the averaged lags of MCG--6-30-15 are mostly within 500 -1500 s, similar to IRAS 13224--3809. The MCG--6-30-15 mass is also 2 10^ 6 M_ Ponti2012 which is comparable to the IRAS 13224--3809 mass. This further suggests that its size and geometry may be analogous to that of IRAS 13224--3809.

sources.summary: The paper treats MCG--6-30-15 as a Seyfert 1 whose dusty warm absorber and reddening likely arise partly in a host-galaxy dust lane below the nucleus.

quotes.quote: On the Location and Composition of the Dust in the \ Warm Absorber Dust in the MCG--6-30-15 warm absorber D. R. Ballantyne, Joseph C. Weingartner and N. Murray Canada Research Chair in Astrophysics Ballantyne et al. D. Ballantyne Canadian Institute for Theoretical Astrophysics, McLennan Labs, 60 St. George Street, Toronto, Ontario, Canada M5S 3H8 ballantyne, weingart, murray@cita.utoronto.ca The warm absorber observed in the Seyfert 1 galaxy \ is known to consist of at least two zones and very likely contains dust. Hubble Space Telescope images of \ show a dust lane crossing the galaxy just below the nucleus. In this paper, we argue that this dust lane is responsible for the observed reddening of the nuclear emission and the Fe i edge hinted at in the Chandra spectrum of .

A Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) image of \ shows a distinct dust lane that cuts across the southern part of the galactic disk (Fig. fig:hst ; mgt98 ; see also fwm00 ). This material is likely intrinsic to the galaxy and not a foreground effect such as Galactic cirrus (e.g., M81; ar87 ). Therefore, in Sect. sect:mcg , we follow the ideas of kra00 , but argue that at least part of the low ionization warm absorber and all of the observed reddening in \ originates within this dust lane.

sources.summary: The review uses MCG--6-30-15 as a key AGN example with a robust broad iron line whose variability resists partial-covering explanations and whose hard spectral component is consistent with blurred reflection.

quotes.quote: MCG--6-30-15 has a robust broad iron line (Tanaka et al 1995; Wilms et al 2001; Fabian et al 2002). Chandra grating observations and RXTE data have sufficient resolution and coverage to rule out partial covering solutions (Young et al 2005). A problem emerges with the lack of variability seen in the line, if the effects of strong gravity are ignored. The strength of the iron line should follow the brightness of the power-law component, but it does not. The iron line does vary on short timescales but not in any simple manner (Iwasawa et al 1996, 1999; Nandra Edelson 2000; Matsumoto et al 2002; Fabian et al 2002).
When applied to MCG--6-30-15 all these methods indicate that the hard component has the shape of blurred reflection and the variable soft component is a power-law.

sources.summary: The paper studies MCG--6-30-15 spectral variability, modeling its power-law and relativistically blurred reflection components and emphasizing its broad iron line as a black-hole accretion probe.

quotes.quote: We present a study of the spectral variability of the Seyfert I galaxy MCG--6-30-15 based on the two long observations from 2000 and 2001. The X--ray spectrum and variability properties of the 2001 data have previously been well described with a two-component model consisting of a variable power law and a much less variable reflection component, containing a broad relativistic iron line from the accretion disc around a rapidly rotating Kerr black hole. The lack of variability of the reflection component has been interpreted as an effect of strong gravitational light bending very close to the central black hole.

The Seyfert I galaxy MCG--6-30-15 is a S0-type galaxy located at a distance of 37 Mpc ( z=0.00775 ) in the constellation of Centaurus. Because of a very prominent broad, relativistic iron line in its X--ray spectrum (first observed by ASCA , Tanaka et al. 1995), this object has come to play an important role in studies of accretion on to black holes. The spectrum of MCG--6-30-15 can be decomposed into two main components, a simple power law component and a relativistically-blurred reflection component.

sources.summary: The paper applies relativistic light-bending models to MCG--6-30-15, connecting its broad iron-line profile, low-flux states, XMM-Newton variability, and inferred black-hole mass to strong-gravity effects.

quotes.quote: The profile of broad Fe line in the X-ray spectrum of Seyfert 1 galaxy MCG--6-30-15, first clearly resolved by ASCA (Tanaka et al. \ 1995), is consistent with that predicted from an accretion disc inclined at 30 . In part of the ASCA observation, when the 2--10 keV flux was 2 times lower than average, F_ 2-10 =4 10^ 11 erg/s/cm ^2 , for the whole observation, the line extended below 4 keV (Iwasawa et al. \ 1996; that low flux state was defined as the deep minimum). Profiles of the Fe line, extending below 4 keV, were revealed also in two XMM-Newton observations (Wilms et al. \ 2001; Fabian et al. \ 2002).

The most tightly constrained value of the black hole mass in MCG--6-30-15, from X-ray variability, is 3 10^6 M_ (in agreement with optical measurements yielding values between 3 and 6 10^6 M_ ; McHardy et al. \ 2005). The best studied example of a relativistic Fe line - with elongated wing formed by such strongly redshifted photons - comes from XMM-Newton observation of MCG--6-30-15 (Fabian et al. \ 2002).

sources.summary: The comparison describes MCG-6-30-15 as an NLS1 with strong RGS emission and narrow absorption, competing spectral interpretations, and two warm-absorber phases with distinct columns and outflow velocities.

quotes.quote: The NLS1s MCG-6-30-15 and Markarian 766 also show strong emission and narrow absorption lines in their RGS soft X-ray spectra ( branduardi01 Branduardi-Raymont et al. 2001). Their fits include steep power laws ( =2.14 and 2.53 respectively) and relativistic emission lines from O VIII, N VII and C VI, as well as narrow absorption lines, in order to explain the peculiar shape of the spectrum. lee03 have also analysed Chandra HETGS data of MCG-6-30-15 and favour a fit of the spectrum using purely a dusty warm absorber model. The analysis of the object is still the subject of much controversy; sako03 have shown that the lee03 model is a poor fit outside of the HETG range and have also made a detailed analysis of the warm absorber present in the two sources. sako03 identify two warm absorber phases in MCG-6-30-15 with column densities of 2 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 and outflow velocities of 150 and 1900 km s ^ 1 respectively.

sources.summary: The paper treats MCG--6-30-15 as a type I active galaxy whose red-wing Fe K emission, hard X-ray excess, and spectral variability can be explained by clumpy absorption with moderate global covering factor.

quotes.quote: MCG--6-30-15 is the archetypal example of a type I active galaxy showing broad ``red-wing'' emission in its X-ray spectrum at energies below the 6.4 keV Fe K emission line and a continuum excess above 20 keV. miller08a showed that these spectral features could be caused by clumpy absorbing material, but reynolds09a have argued that the observed Fe K line luminosity is inconsistent with this explanation unless the global covering factor of the absorber(s) is very low. However, the reynolds09a calculation effectively considers the only source of opacity to be the Fe K bound-free transition and neglects the opacity at the line energy: correction to realistic opacity decreases the predicted line flux by a large factor. We also discuss the interpretation of the covering factor and the possible effect of occultation by the accretion disk. Finally, we consider a model for MCG--6-30-15 dominated by clumpy absorption, which is consistent with global covering factor 0.45, although models that include the effects of Compton scattering are required to reach a full understanding.

sources.summary: The paper uses stellar velocity dispersion for MCG -6-30-15, finds an edge-varying ePLG model best, and detects significant iron-line EW variation.

quotes.quote: Data selection and reduction In Tab. tab:tab1 , we list the 10 AGN in our sample, some of their properties (distance, redshift and BH mass estimates), and details of the \ observations we used in this work. We used the petersonea:2004 estimates, from reverbaration mapping, for the BH mass (M _ BH ) in Fairall 9, NGC 4051, NGC 3227, NGC 5548, NGC 3783, and NGC 3516. In the case of Mrk 766 and MCG-6-30-15 we used the stellar velocity dispecrsion measurements of botteea:2004 and mchardyea:2005 , respectively, and the tremaineea:2002 M_ BH relation to estimate BH mass. The BH mass estimates for Ark 564 and NGC 5506 were taken from zhangwang:2006 and wangzhang:2007 , respectively.

Summary of the acceptable model fits = = 1. A simple PL model describes well the spectra of Fairall 9, Akn 564 and Mkn 766. 2. In NGC 4051, NGC 3227, and NGC 5548 a statistically acceptable global fit to the data is obtained with the PLG model. 3. In NGC 3783 and NGC 5506, the addition of an absorption edge with energy fixed at 7.1 keV is needed to achieve an acceptable model fit (ePLG). 4.
