In MCG 6-30-15 the best global fit is reached by the ePLG model but only when the edge energy is allowed to vary (the resulting mean edge energy is E _ edge 7.4 keV). 5. Finally, the same ePLG model provides an acceptable global fit to the NGC 3516 spectra but only when both the energy of the iron line and the energy of the absorption edge were left free to vary ( E _ line 6.1 , and E _ edge 7.95 ).

An iron K_ line is detected in many spectra of all sources (except for Akn 564, Fairall 9 and Mrk 766). To investigate the line's EW variations, we considered the spectral fit results in the case when the line is detected at the 2 level in a spectrum of a source. The number of spectra which do show such a strong line, and the average equivalent width, EW , using the individual EW values from these spectra only, are listed in Tab. tab:tab4 . The fourth column in Tab. tab:tab4 lists the ^2 values when we fit a constant to the EW light curves. The result is that we do not detect significant EW variations, except for NGC 5548, MCG -6-30-15, and NGC 3516.

sources.summary: The paper identifies MCG-6-30-15 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy where ASCA revealed relativistically broadened, highly variable Fe K line profiles from the inner accretion disk.

quotes.quote: This discovery was made after four-day observations of Seyfert 1 galaxy MCG-6-30-15 (see Fig. fig05 ).
In some cases (e.g. for Seyfert 1 galaxy MCG-6-30-15), FWHM velocity reaches 30 of speed of light see e.g. Nandra07 .

Fig. fig12 shows one of the most illustrative examples for such variations which was found in ASCA observations of MCG--6-30-15 during 1994 and 1997 fab00 . As it can be seen from Fig. fig12 , in the 1994 observation, a very broad profile with a pronounced red wing is seen during a period of deep minimum of the light curve (lower left panel), compared to the time-averaged line profile shown in the upper panel. In contrast, during a sharp flare in the 1997 observation, the whole line emission is shifted to energies below 6 keV, and there is no significant emission at the rest line energy of 6.4 keV (lower right panel).

sources.summary: The paper uses MCG-6-30-15 as its AGN test case, describing its broad 6.4 keV iron-line red wing and comparing light-bending and absorption explanations.

quotes.quote: To illustrate the predictive power of X-ray polarimetry in AGN, we focus on the famous candidate MCG-6-30-15 rather than going through a generic model, as precise fitting parameters for different spectroscopic models of the object are given in the literature. The Seyfert 1 galaxy is one of the best examples showing an asymmetric, broad, 6.4 keV iron line. MCG-6-30-15's red-wing is well established since its ASCA discovery by Tanaka1995 and following long observations with XMM-Newton Wilms2001,Fabian2002 and Suzaku Miniutti2007 . Its 4 --7 keV spectrum is well fitted applying the light bending scenario with a rotating SMBH Wilms2001,Fabian2002 . However, Inoue2003 and Miller2008,Miller2009 also claimed to be able to reproduce the excess emission above 10 keV, the spectral shape, and the time-invariant red wing of the iron line in MCG-6-30-15 using absorption models.

sources.summary: The paper contrasts MCG--6-30-15 with NGC 1365, arguing that PCA favors intrinsic continuum plus relativistic reflection variability rather than the absorption-dominated behavior seen in NGC 1365.

quotes.quote: Miller08 use PCA to argue that the spectral shape and variability in the AGN MCG--6-30-15 can be explained using a partial covering absorption model, where the source is obscured by intervening material which varies in column density and ionisation. However, a more detailed analysis using a similar method but with a larger dataset by Parker13 found that the variability was most likely dominated by variations intrinsic to the source, in the form of a power law continuum and a separate relativistically blurred reflection component. In general, it can be difficult to distinguish between relativistic reflection and absorption models in AGN from spectral fitting alone (see e.g. the comparison of the two in MCG--6-30-15 by Marinucci et al., submitted), as these can often give statistically equivalent fits.

These components are qualitatively different from those found in a similar analysis of MCG--6-30-15, demonstrating that the main driver of variability in the two sources must have a different origin. While the variability in NGC 1365 is dominated primarily by variable absorption, when focusing specifically on the least obscured periods we also see evidence for an increased contribution from intrinsic variability. Simulated spectra, based on simple models of AGN spectra, are used to investigate the origin of the variability in these two sources, and show that we can reproduce the PCA spectra from the data using absorption and reflection models for NGC 1365 and MCG--6-30-15, respectively.

sources.summary: PCA of MCG--6-30-15 finds principal components explained by variable power-law emission plus relatively constant reflection, consistent with light-bending variability.

quotes.quote: MCG--6-30-15 is very well studied, bright and highly variable narrow line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) galaxy. It was the first AGN in which a relativistically broadened iron line was found Tanaka95 , and also shows the characteristic features of warm absorption Otani96 Fig. mcg6_spectra shows the three PC spectra presented by P14a, along with the weak fourth component not discussed in that work. The first three PCs found in this object were analysed in detail by P14a, who found that they were well explained by the effects of a powerlaw varying in normalisation and photon index, and uncorrelated variations in a relatively constant reflection spectrum. These findings are consistent with the light-bending interpretation of the variability in this AGN, in which the height of the primary X-ray source above the disk changes Martocchia00,Miniutti03,Miniutti04 leading to more extreme variations in the primary emission than in the reflected emission.

sources.summary: The paper highlights MCG-6-30-15 as a repeatedly observed Seyfert whose stable low-frequency hard lag turns sharply into a soft lag near 1.5e-4 Hz.

quotes.quote: Lastly, MCG-6-30-15 is a source that has been observed by XMM-Newton several times, and all observations show roughly the same mean count rate. The low-frequency lags are also stable over all observations. MCG-6-30-15 shows a low-frequency hard lag, similar to most sources, but at around 1.5 10^ 4 Hz, it turns over sharply to a low-frequency soft lag. This is different from the low-frequency soft lags in NGC 1365 and NGC 4051 in that it is present in all observations and at all flux regimes. Also, unlike NGC 4051 where the low-frequency hard lag disappears, in MCG-6-30-15, the hard lag is always present.

sources.summary: The paper shows MCG--6-30-15 power spectra, notes it is brighter than IRAS 13224-3809 in the iron band, and includes it among sources with well-defined low-frequency iron-line PSDs.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:softHardExample shows the observed power spectra of MCG--6-30-15 and IRAS 13224-3809 (upper and lower panels, respectively).
This is mainly due to the fact that this source is not as bright as MCG--6-30-15 in the 5-7 keV band.

The Poisson noise dominates the observed variations at frequencies higher than 10^ 3 Hz, except from four sources, namely MCG--6-30-15, NGC 4051, Ark 564, and NGC 7314. The iron-line band PSD in these sources is well defined over the lowest sampled frequency decade, i.e. \ between 10^ 4 and 10^ 3 Hz. This is not surprising, as these sources are among the X-ray brightest in our sample (hence the Poisson noise level is low) and they probably host a low mass BH as well, implying large amplitude variations at high-frequencies.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes MCG--6--30--15 as a bright Seyfert 1 galaxy with a broad iron line, strong variability, and literature evidence for a rapidly spinning black hole.

quotes.quote: MCG--6--30--15 is a very bright Seyfert 1 galaxy at redshift z = 0.007749 . This source has been studied by many authors with different observations and different X-ray missions see, e.g., 1996MNRAS.282.1038I,1999A&A...341L..27G,2002MNRAS.335L...1F,2006ApJ...652.1028B,2007PASJ...59S.315M,2014ApJ...787...83M . It is the source in which a relativistically broadened iron line was unambiguously detected for the first time 1995Natur.375..659T . Indeed its spectrum often shows a very broad and prominent iron K line. This makes MCG--6--30--15 an excellent candidate for a number of studies of relativistic reflection spectra.

sources.summary: MCG-06-30-15 is included as an NLSy1 X-ray QPO case; the GRPM contour analysis places its QPO-emission radius near 14.2, close to the black hole.

quotes.quote: Another significant X-ray QPO was reported in NLSy1 MCG-06-30-15 of timescale 3600 s .

We discuss our results below: We have computed the contours of _ , a , using Equation nuphicirc , for the QPO frequencies (given in Table AGNXrayQPO ) of RE J1034 + 396 (blue), MS 2254.9-3712 (red), and MCG-06-30-15 (magenta), shown in the , a plane in Figure plotra1QPO a. The masses of these black holes were assumed from the previous estimations (see Table AGNXrayQPO ). We see that the QPO emission originates from a very narrow region of the accretion disk, where 9.4-9.9 for RE J1034 + 396, 10.4-11.4 for MS 2254.9-3712, and 14.2 for MCG-06-30-15 even though a ranges from 0 to 1. This implies that the QPO emission region is very close to the black hole, and this emission region remains very narrow and nearly independent of the spin of the black hole.

sources.summary: The paper applies a multi-Compton reflection model to MCG-6-30-15, requiring cold and ionised material, high covering factor, and reproducing the broad red wing of its iron line.

quotes.quote: We have applied this model to the intensively observed source MCG-6-30-15. Abrassart (1998) showed that in order to ensure a Compton hump strong enough, as shown by RXTE data, cold material is requested while the red wing requires the presence of ionised material. A high covering factor ( 0.9 ) is needed. This model also applies to the ASCA data at higher spectral resolution than the RXTE ones. A satisfactory fit is obtained for the 1994 SIS observations, using an incident spectrum described by the average AGN distribution derived by Laor et al. (1997), a high covering factor of 0.9, and clouds of different ionisation states (30 highly ionized ( =30000 erg.s ^ 1 ) and 70 near neutral ( =300)) (see fig. 3).

sources.summary: The paper uses MCG--6-30-15 as a key Seyfert case where light bending can explain continuum variability, nearly constant reflection, and broad iron-line behavior.

quotes.quote: MCG--6-30-15 The spectrum and variability of the Seyfert 1 galaxy MCG--6-30-15 in its normal flux states can be accounted for by a phenomenological two--component model consisting of a variable power law representing the direct continuum and an almost constant reflection--dominated component which contains the broad iron emission line . This model is also supported by the analysis of the correlation between fluxes in different energy ranges using both RXTE data and XMM--Newton observations . The analysis reveals the existence of a soft component varying in normalisation and an additional almost constant harder spectral component.

The lack of correlation between the two main spectral components represents a challenge for the standard picture of reflection models because, as already stressed, any reflection feature should respond to the variations in the continuum, especially if it is produced in the inner regions of the accretion disc as the reflection spectrum of MCG--6-30-15 (and most remarkably the iron line shape) indicates. However, as pointed out by Fabian Vaughan (2003), the two--component model for the variability of this source could be perhaps explained self--consistently by taking into account light bending effects in the near vicinity of the black hole.

sources.summary: The paper uses the broad iron K line in the Seyfert galaxy MCG--6-30-15 to test coronal-flare reconstruction and probe strong gravity near its black hole.

quotes.quote: The broad, skewed emission line around 6.4 keV observed in the Seyfert 1 galaxy MCG--6-30-15 was first resolved with ASCA 1995Natur.375..659T . A widely accepted model for the production of this line is one where hard X-rays are reprocessed by the innermost regions of a cold, thin accretion disc around a super-massive black hole 1991MNRAS.249..352G,1991A&A...247...25M . X-ray photons emitted from this material suffer large gravitational redshifts, special relativistic beaming and Doppler shifts, combining to give a characteristic shape which is in good agreement with the data. In addition, a number of alternative models were considered by , but a convincing rival candidate was not found. The extended red wing of this line 2001MNRAS.328L..27W,2002MNRAS.335L...1F,fabianvaughan2003 has provided the first direct observational probes of the strong gravity environment near a black hole and has been used to constrain parameters such as the inclination of the accretion disc and the black hole's spin 1996MNRAS.282.1038I,1997MNRAS.288L..11D .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes XMM-Newton spectra of the Seyfert galaxy MCG-6-30-15, finding a state-dependent 6.9 keV iron-line component interpreted as blob ejection near its black hole.

quotes.quote: The EPIC data of MCG-6-30-15 observed by XMM-Newton were analyzed for the complexities of the iron K line. Here we report that the additional line component (ALC) at 6.9 keV undoubtedly appears within the broad iron K line profile at high state whereas it disappears at low state. These state-dependent behaviors exclude several possible origins and suggest an origin of the ALC in matter being ejected from the vicinity of the black hole. At the low state, the newborn blob ejected from the accretion disk is so Thomson thick that hard X-ray is blocked not to ionize the old ones, leading to the disappearance of the ALC. When the blob becomes Thomson thin due to expansion, the hard X-ray will penetrate it and ionize the old ones, emitting the ALC at the high state.

sources.summary: The paper uses MCG-6-30-15 as a Seyfert-1 test case, modeling its XMM-Newton fractional variability and suppressed iron-line-band variability with a multi-flare accretion-disk model.

quotes.quote: We compare our results to the observed fractional variability spectrum of the Seyfert galaxy MCG-6-30-15.

Two long X-ray exposures were obtained with XMM-Newton for the Seyfert-1 galaxy MCG-6-30-15: one observation lasting 95 ks was performed in June 2000 (Wilms et al. 2001), and the second one lasting about 325 ks was done in July 2001 (Fabian et al. 2002). The time variability in the data from 2000 was analyzed in detail by Ponti et al. (2004), while the time variability in the data from 2001 was studied by Vaughan Fabian (2004). We attempt to reproduce the shape of the fractional variability amplitude obtained by Ponti et al. (2004; see their Fig. 2 and Fig. fig:three in this paper). We also derive the point-to-point fractional variability amplitude for the same data sequence.

sources.summary: The paper compares XMM-Newton variability spectra for MCG--6-30-15 with smeared-absorber models, finding a composite variability model broadly descriptive but missing a 7 keV rms dip.

quotes.quote: We have calculated variability spectra from XMM-Newton data of NGC 4051 (2001 May 16/17 observation), MCG--6-30-15 (2001 August 2/3) and 1H 0707--495 (2002 October 13/14). We used background subtracted light curves with 256-s resolution, accumulated from all available instruments (PN only for NGC 4051, both MOS and PN for MCG--6-30-15 and 1H 0707--495).
Such a composite model of the variability can describe the rms spectra seen from MCG--6-30-15 and 1H 0707--495, as shown by solid curves in Figs. fig:3agn (a) and (b).

In particular, there is a dip in the rms around 7 keV in MCG--6-30-15, not covered by our model. This could be due to a reflected component which remains constant while the power law continuum varies (e.g. Vaughan Fabian 2004).

sources.summary: The paper describes MCG--6-30-15 as a Seyfert S0 galaxy with a powerful AGN, broad Fe-K emission, and evidence that its central black hole is rapidly rotating.

quotes.quote: MCG--6-30-15 is an S0-type galaxy in the constellation of Centaurus that hosts a Seyfert-1.2 nucleus. It has a measured redshift of z = 0.008 , placing it at a distance of d 37 using WMAP cosmological parameters. X-ray studies of this AGN have revealed a powerful central source along with a significantly broadened Fe-K feature. This broad line has led MCG--6-30-15 to become one of the most studied AGN in the X-ray band due to its potential as a probe of black hole and accretion disk physics.
Under this assumption we arrive at the conclusion that the black hole in MCG--6-30-15 is a rapid-rotator.

sources.summary: The paper models MCG-6-30-15 as a bright Seyfert 1 whose steep X-ray emissivity and iron-line profile can be reproduced with low black-hole spin in MCADAF.

quotes.quote: A very steep emissivity index can be produced in the innermost part of the MCADAF, which is consistent with the recent XMM-Newton observations of the nearby bright Seyfert 1 galaxy MCG-6-30-15 and with two X-ray binaries (XRBs): XTE J1655-500 and GX 339-4. In addition, we estimate the BH spins in Seyfert 1 galaxy MCG-6-30-15 and in the two XRBs based on this model.

As shown in the shaded region of Figure 7, we find that the recent XMM-Newton observation of the nearby bright Seyfert 1 galaxy MCG-6-30-15 (Wilms et al. 2001) can be simulated in MCADAF, while the index produced by the ADAF is far below the shaded region as shown by the dot-dashed lines in Figure 7. Comparing the results in Figure 7 with those obtained in W03, we find that the emissivity index in MCADAF corresponds to the spins less than 0.4, which are much lower than those obtained in MCTHIN with a_ > 0.99 . By analyzing the X-ray spectrum of the Seyfert 1 galaxy MCG-6-30-15, Dovciak et al. (2004) discussed a low-spin ( a_ = 0.25) best-fitting model for the iron line emission in MCG-6-30-15.

sources.summary: The paper models an XMM-Newton flare in the Seyfert galaxy MCG-6-30-15 as localized magnetic reconnection above the accretion disk, using light-curve modulation and X-ray delays to constrain the setup.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert galaxy MCG-6-30-15 was observed for 95 ksec with XMM-Newton in the year 2000 ( ). The X-ray lightcurve of this observation reveals a strong flare event of which conducted a detailed analysis. In this proceedings note we discuss the possibility that the flare is produced by localized magnetic reconnection and we constrain some details of such a flare setup.

We imagine that the strong flare in MCG-6-30-15 originates in a compact reconnection site elevated to a height H above the surface of the accretion disk. The radiation from this primary source partly shines toward the disk and creates a hot spot.

sources.summary: The paper coadds MCG --6-30-15 observations to constrain reflection, identifies it as the softest Seyfert with saturated reflection, and excludes it from rank-correlation tests.

quotes.quote: This criterion eliminates, in particular, all the observations of MCG --6-30-15 and Mkn 335. To improve statistics in those cases, we have coadded multiple observations of each of those objects. In the case of Mkn 335, the resulting R was still 2 , and thus we kept it out of the sample. For MCG --6-30-15, we coadded 4 (out of 5) observations that were performed in the standard mode with all the LAC detectors operating, which then resulted in data with an acceptable R <2 .
Reflection appears to saturate at R 2 as our softest Seyfert, MCG --6-30-15, has R 2 at the best-fit 2.4 , somewhat below an extrapolation of the trend for objects with harder spectra.
We use our sample of RQ Seyferts but excluding MCG --6-30-15, for which R appears saturated.

sources.summary: The chapter identifies MCG-6-30-15 as the best-studied broad Fe K-line AGN, noting ASCA evidence for the line and relativistic line widths reaching about 30 percent of light speed.

quotes.quote: The first and the best studied one is MCG-6-30-15 Tanaka95,Fabian03 .
This discovery was made after four-day observations of Seyfert 1 galaxy MCG-6-30-15 (see Fig. fig52_1 ).

Using ASCA satellite observations, Nandra97 found that, in case of 14 Seyfert 1 galaxies, Full-Widths at Half-Maximum (FWHM) of their Fe K lines correspond to velocities of 50,000 km/s. In some cases (e.g. for Seyfert 1 galaxy MCG-6-30-15), FWHM velocity reaches 30 of speed of light see e.g. Nandra07 . It means that in the vicinity of the central black hole, orbital velocities of the emitting material are relativistic, causing the enhancement of the Fe K line "blue" peak in regard to its "red" peak (relativistic beaming).

sources.summary: The paper uses MCG-6-30-15 as an NLS1 galaxy with long X-ray observations to examine X-ray variability amplitudes and black-hole mass uncertainties.

quotes.quote: We therefore use the RM mass of NGC 4395 (Peterson et al. 2005), and estimate the M_ BH of MCG-6-30-15 from the empirical virial relation using the line (Zhou Wang 2005).

Figure 6 shows the distribution of XVA for the NLS1 galaxy MCG-6-30-15 and 1H0707-495. MCG-6-30-15 was observed using starting on 2000 July 11 and ending on 2001 August 4, with a total exposure time of 480 ks. 1H0707-495 was observed using starting on 2000 October 21 and ending on 2008 February 6, with a total exposure time of 800 ks. We analyze all these data and filter the light curves to remove any background flares.

sources.summary: The paper treats MCG-6-30-15 as a key Seyfert 1 broad-iron-line source, citing its ASCA disk-line detection, sample inclusion despite poor fits, and ambiguous high/soft-state classification.

quotes.quote: The first clear example of relativistic dynamics affecting the line shape, that is a line originating in the innermost region, was found in the Seyfert 1 AGN MCG-6-30-15 by Tanaka et al. (1995), at that time using ASCA/SIS.

The Nandra sample contains most of the sources in Millers Tier 1, his best candidates, but the judgment on the emission lines is not always the same: for NGC 3783 and MCG-6-30-15 the model fits of Nandra et al. were classified as poor. We kept these sources in our chosen sample.

One of these sources, MCG-6-30-15, was classified as in high/soft state based on the comparison of the PSD (Uttley McHardy 2005), but the same was found for NGC 4051, for which a low accretion rate (scaled to the Eddington rate) was found.

sources.summary: The paper infers a break timescale for MCG-6-30-15, relates it to black-hole mass and spin, and sets a maximally spinning black-hole mass upper limit.

quotes.quote: MCG-6-30-15 with an inferred break timescale of 6026^ +4207 _ 2478 (from the current analysis) hosts a BH of mass (1.9^ +0.78 _ 0.78 ) 10^6 M_ and a lower limit on the spin of 0.80. Contours of T_ B = T_ B (,a) are plotted in Figure MCGbrkfig . The overlap in this case is only between the contours of T_ B and the spin a . An upper limit to the black hole mass of 4.68 10^7 M_ (for a maximally spinning black hole with a = 0.998 ) can be inferred.

sources.summary: The paper treats MCG-6-30-15 as the prototype VPC-model Seyfert, connecting its broad iron feature and spectral variability to partial covering absorbers.

quotes.quote: MCG-6-30-15 is a representative example; its iron emission line profile seems to be broadened and skewed (e.g., ), and fractional variation of the energy spectrum significantly drops at the iron line energy band Fabian2002,Matsumoto2003 . MEI2012 applied the VPC model to Suzaku observations of MCG-6-30-15, and successfully explained not only the small fractional variation in the iron energy band, but also the spectral variations in 1 -- 10 keV (see also ), besides an independent hard-tail variations above 10 keV.

The original VPC model for MCG-6-30-15 requires three types of the absorbers; optically-thick partial absorbing clouds, optically-thin envelopes of the clouds, and a uniformly surrounding highly ionized absorber (Group A in Figure figABCD ). In the case of MCG-6-30-15, all the high-ionized absorber ( W_ H ), low-ionized absorber ( W_ L ), and the heavy absorber ( W_ 2 ) were required (MEI2012).

sources.summary: The paper reports a blob-like mm-wave component in MCG 6 30 15 and argues its position angle differs from both host-galaxy and narrow-line-region structures.

quotes.quote: 65: MCG 6 30 15 At a PA of 140--150 , a blob-like component ( a ) is revealed at a resolution of 0 1. Radio observations at 8 GHz and 1.5 GHz were performed Nag99 , but the resolutions were much coarser (a few arcseconds), and detected radio components were not spatially resolved. HST images were reported in some works Fer00,Sch03 , and show that the host galaxy and also a NLR traced by the optical [ O iii ] emission have similar PAs of 65 , slightly different from the PA of the mm-wave component. Thus, the origin could be due neither to phenomena in the main body of the host galaxy nor to the AGN, and currently the origin is unclear.

sources.summary: The paper uses MCG--6--30--15 as a Seyfert line-profile test case, linking observed high disk emissivity and low/high-state Fe-line shapes to modeled radial drift and multi-species emission.

quotes.quote: This mechanism may provide the higher disk emissivities () ^ 4.5 as recently observed in Seyfert galaxy MCG--6--30--15 wilms .
The recent observation of Seyfert galaxy MCG--6--30--15 in the low state wilms serves as an example of bumpy shapes.

Finally, a more complicated line profile with a few species in application to a recent observation of Seyfert--1 galaxy MCG--6--30--15 in the high state fab2 is demonstrated. The characteristic shoulder--like shape of this observation can be reproduced theoretically with the following plausible parameters: rapidly spinning black hole, a=0.8 , typical Seyfert--1 inclination, i=40^ , inner edge at horizon, r_ in =r_ H =1.6 \ r_ g , outer edge at r_ out =20.0 \ r_ g , standard Keplerian rotation plus radial drift starting at R_ t =4.5 \ r_ g , Gaussian emissivity: R_ t =4.5 \ r_ g and _ =0.4 \ R_ t , multi--species line system consisting in Fe K , Fe K , Ni K and Cr K The relative line strength of the different species are modelled in an approximation found in Reynolds (1996).

sources.summary: The paper groups MCG-6-30-15 with NGC 4051 as an NLS1 whose X-ray variability resembles the high/soft state of Cyg X-1.

quotes.quote: Two of these AGN (NGC 4051 and MCG-6-30-15, both NLS1's) clearly show flat noise power extending over more than 4 decades in frequency. These look very like that of the high/soft state in Cyg X-1, taking into account uncertainties in the low frequency slope of the power spectra (McHardy et al. 2004; 2005). The total rms power in both is of order 25 per cent, again similar to that of the Comptonized tail in the high/soft state of Cyg X-1 (McHardy et al. 2004). Thus these AGN can be interpreted as the supermassive counterpart of the high/soft state Cyg X-1 data, which is not like the majority of transient GBH power spectra at high accretion rates.

sources.summary: The paper treats MCG--6-30-15 as a nearby type 1 Seyfert galaxy whose unusual iron-line profile can be qualitatively explained by a Bardeen-Petterson disk.

quotes.quote: We use our findings to show that some of the ``extra'' line features observed in the spectrum of the Seyfert-I galaxy MCG--6-30-15 may be attributable to a Bardeen-Petterson disk structure. MCG--6-30-15 One of the best studied iron-line sources is the nearby type 1 Seyfert galaxy MCG--6-30-15. We suggest therefore that, in the case of MCG--6-30-15, it is the outer component that produces the core line near 6.4 keV while an inner disk component produces both the extended red wing and the 6.9 keV feature.
Nevertheless, it is worth point out that our relatively simple physical model is successful in matching many of the unusual features of the MCG--6-30-15 line profile.

sources.summary: The paper uses MCG-6-30-15 as a key Seyfert example whose rms spectra show iron-line variability, a variable broad line, and evidence for a nearly constant reflection component.

quotes.quote: Miniutti et al. (2007) have studied the normalised rms spectrum of MCG-6-30-15 across a wider energy range with the broad-band instruments aboard Suzaku and found that the normalised rms decreased with energy above 3.0 keV and there is a drop in variability at 6.4 keV.

Normalised rms spectra and other techniques such as time-resolved and difference spectra have been used to suggest MCG-6-30-15 as a strong case for the two-component model where a variable power-law with a constant slope is superimposed on a hard reflection component that is nearly constant (McHardy et al.
MCG-6-30-15(1) has a variable, broad iron line that is as strong as the time-averaged line profile, as does NGC 3783 (although its iron line red wing is not as strong as in MCG-6-30-15).

sources.summary: The paper describes MCG-6-30-15 as a Seyfert-1 galaxy whose sub-keV absorption features were confirmed by Chandra and XMM-Newton as blueshifted outflowing plasma.

quotes.quote: For example, the X-ray bright QSO IRAS 13349+2438 ( z=0.10764 ) and the Seyfert-1 galaxy (e.g. MCG-6-30-15 at z=0.007749 ) observed extensively with ROSAT Brandt96 and ASCA Brandt97 gave indications of absorption features in the sub-keV regime. Their Chandra and XMM-Newton spectra confirmed this fact and exhibited a wealth of transitions such as , - , - , , - , and while successfully identifying almost all charge states of Fe and O in some cases hereafter HBK07 Behar03,HBK07 . Furthermore, these observations showed the various transitions to be blueshifted relative to the host galaxy, indicating that the warm absorber plasma is, in fact, outflowing.

sources.summary: The paper treats MCG-6-30-15 as a low-mass AGN with a broad Fe K line, citing its disk inclination and existing EPIC counts as benchmarks for ripple-effect searches.

quotes.quote: Because of the shorter timescales, low mass AGN exhibiting broad Fe K line profiles (e.g. MCG-6-30-15) are the prime targets for observations searching for this ripple effect, and the massive mergers that cause it.

To systematically obtain broad line spectra that can distinguish between the different cases in Fig. fig:feka requires a minimum telescope effective area of that planned for LOFT ( 10 m ^ 2 at 6.4keV), together with the planned spectral resolution of ( 5-7 eV at 6.4keV) for Astro-H and IXO/Athena . vf04 obtain 18,000 counts in the broad red wing of the Fe K line in a 320 ks observation of MCG-6-30-15 with the EPIC detector on board , yielding 600 average counts at the limiting energy resolution of 0.1 keV per bin and very high signal to noise.
The most famous example of a broad Fe K line in an AGN (MCG-6-30-15) is best-fit with disk-inclination angle of =27^ o reynow03 , where =0^ o corresponds to face-on.

sources.summary: The atlas gives MCG-6-30-15's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: MCG-6-30-15 is an elongated lenticular galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0077 ( D 35.8 Mpc) with a Sy 1.5 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , which was discovered in X-rays pineda_mcg_1978,pineda_x-ray_1980 and since then has been extensively studied in X-rays. MCG-6-30-15 also belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. It possesses a NLR extending several hundreds of parsec with a PA 115 ferruit_hubble_2000,schmitt_hubble_2003 , while the radio emission is unresolved nagar_radio_1999 .

sources.summary: The paper treats MCG--6-30-15 as a bright AGN with time-delayed X-ray behaviour and finds its spin parameter significantly differs from zero.

quotes.quote: Then, emmanoulopoulos11b found that this time delayed X-ray behaviour is much more common than was initially thought by analysing the data sets from two widely studied bright AGN, MCG--6-30-15 and Mrk 766.

Namely, for five sources: Mrk 766, MCG--6-30-15, IRAS 13224-3809, ESO 113-G010 and NGC 5548, the derived spin parameter values differ significantly from 0, with H_0 (i.e. \ the quantity is consistent with zero) probabilities of 0.0034, 8.19 10^ 5 , 3.94 10^ 4 , 1.76 10^ 5 and 0.018, respectively.

We find that five sources in our sample, namely Mrk 766, MCG--6-30-15, IRAS 13224-3809, ESO 113-G010 and NGC 5548, possibly host a rotating BH, since the best-fitting for these objects differs significantly from zero.

sources.summary: The review says STOKES modeled MCG-6-30-15 under a partial-covering AGN scenario, contrasting its predicted X-ray polarization spectrum with relativistic-reflection expectations.

quotes.quote: In Marin2012b , we modeled the polarization signature of MCG-6-30-15 resulting from a partial covering scenario where a clumpy gas distribution is thought to obscure the equatorial plane. We compared the results to a reflection model based on the lamppost geometry and found that the shape of the polarization degree and position angle as a function of photon energy are distinctly different between the reflection and the absorption cases: disk scattering and general relativistic effects produce significantly stronger polarization in the soft energy band than absorption. The spectrum of the polarization angle adds additional constraints: its has a constant value in the absorption case while smooth rotations of the polarization angle with photon energy are detected in the relativistic reflection scenario.

sources.summary: The paper cites MCG--6--30--15 as an object whose X-ray spectrum contains broad iron features; prior RMS and DVF studies found reduced variability near 6 keV and examined the iron K feature.

quotes.quote: These spectral features have been found in the X-ray spectrum of MCG--6--30--15 tan95 , and in many other AGN and BHB with CCD detectors such as EPIC on XMM-Newton and XIS on Suzaku ( mil07 for a review).
mat03 created root-mean-square (RMS) spectra of MCG--6--30--15 at long timescales, and found that the RMS variability is significantly reduced around 6 keV at timescales of 10^ 4-5 s.
miy12 effectively applied this method to the Suzaku data of MCG--6--30--15, and investigated nature of the iron K-spectral feature.

sources.summary: The paper classifies MGC--06-30-015 as a Seyfert 1.2, finds its predicted line ratio consistent with comparison samples, and notes its extinction agrees with a nuclear-continuum estimate.

quotes.quote: MGC--06-30-015 : This object is a Seyfert 1.2 as [ O III ] /H = 0.9 (but note that in the scheme of this object would be a Seyfert 1.5).

The line ratios we observe for the unreddened Seyfert 1s MGC-05--14--012, NGC 3783 and NGC 4593 are in good agreement with these other observational studies; as is the predicted line ratio for NGC 6814, MCG--06--30--015 and MCG-05--23--16, the latter based on the best fit to the H and Paschen lines, and for which we derived a substantial extinction of A_V(BLR) = 7.7 mag.
MCG -06-30-015 : reynolds97 estimated A _V = 2.0--2.4 from the slope of the nuclear continuum, in agreement with our estimate.

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe ESO 383-G35 as an E/S0 galaxy with a Seyfert 1 nucleus, coronal line emission, HST imaging, and an extensive narrow-line region along the galaxy major axis.

quotes.quote: ESO 383-G35: This E/S0 galaxy contains a Sy 1 with coronal line emission. It has been imaged in \ and \ using HST by Ferruit00 . We find an extensive ENLR that can be traced along the major axis of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper reports the first H I measurements for MCG-06-30-015, one of the faintest detections in the sample.

quotes.quote: MCG-06-30-015: The HI measurements in this work are the first reported for this galaxy. MCG-06-30-015 has one of the faintest emission lines that was detected (S/N=3.4) in our sample. To fit the asymmetric dual-horned profile, we held the central trough amplitude ( ) fixed at 0.011, the half-width ( w ) fixed at 5, and the polynomial degree ( n ) fixed at 2.8.

sources.summary: The review identifies MCG -6-30-15 as the only XMM-Newton type-I AGN case with a significant broad relativistic iron-line red wing, possibly requiring a Kerr metric.

quotes.quote: Few examples of the broad, relativistic iron line profile have been found by XMM-Newton, although in MCG -6-30-15, the extreme breadth of the broad line component may imply a Kerr metric for the central black hole.

The only line profile (Figure 1) where a significant red-wing (down to 3 keV) was found was in MCG -6-30-15, where the extreme red-shift requires a Kerr metric for the line profile (Wilms et al. 2001).
However the relativistic, redshifted profile associated with emission from the inner disc appears less common, here the only apparent case is MCG -6-30-15.

sources.summary: The paper uses MCG--6--30--15 as the textbook nearby Seyfert case for a relativistically broadened iron line, citing its skewed low-energy wing and robust multi-mission confirmation against absorption alternatives.

quotes.quote: The profile of the broad emission line in the nearby Seyfert galaxy MCG--6--30--15 represents a textbook case. The presence of a relativistic line with a skewed profile extending down to 4 keV (and during some episodes even at lower energies ), was first unambiguously determined by ASCA , and then confirmed by essentially all the X--ray experiments (i.e. BeppoSAX , XMM-- Newton and Chandra ). While the evidence for a line profile distorted by general relativistic effects is quite solid in MCG--6--30--15, and tested against alternative solutions such as complex absorption , the observational constraints on other nearby Seyfert galaxies are not such to rule out alternative possibilities.

sources.summary: The review uses MCG--6-30-15 as a Seyfert-1 benchmark with an extreme relativistic Fe K line, moderate absorption, and broad-line modeling consistent with high black-hole spin.

quotes.quote: Importantly, the line profile and overall disk reflection spectrum is well fitted with a model that is remarkably similar to that which describes the same components in an XMM-Newton observation of the Seyfert-1 galaxy MCG--6-30-15 (Fabian et al. \ 2001).

Again, the line profile and the overall continuum spectrum are remarkably similar to the most extreme line profiles detected in MCG--6-30-15 (e.g. Fabian et al. \ 2001), and the spectrum can be fit with very similar models.
At least in the case of MCG--6-30-15, rigorous work shows that the broad line is independent of low-energy curvature (e.g., Vaughan Fabian 2004; Youg et al. \ 2005).

sources.summary: The review presents MCG--6-30-15 as a Seyfert galaxy with a clear broad iron line and reflection hump, high inferred black-hole spin, and no relevant absorption features.

quotes.quote: The broad iron line and reflection hump are clearly seen in the Seyfert galaxy MCG--6-30-15 and in the GBH J1650-400.
Objects with a very broad iron line like MCG--6-30-15 are inferred to have high spin a>0.95 (Dabrowski \ 1997; Brennemann \ 2006).
Observations of MCG--6-30-15 fail to show absorption lines or feature associated with iron of intermediate ionization.

sources.summary: The paper uses MCG-6-30-15 as observational motivation: ASCA detected a broad Fe K line consistent with a compact accretion disk, and moment mapping favors a rotating black hole.

quotes.quote: In the context of the simple, thin disk paradigm, this moment-mapping scheme suggests formally that the iron line detected by the Advanced Satellite for Cosmology and Astrophysics mission from MCG-6-30-15 (Tanaka et al. 1995) is 3 times more likely to originate from a disk about a rotating black hole than from a Schwarzschild system.

Recent observations by the Japanese ASCA satellite have turned this speculation into reality. Broad iron K lines have been reported for three Seyfert galaxies (Fabian et al. 1994). A four-day exposure of one of them, MCG 6-30-15, has yielded a well-resolved Fe K _ line with an extremely broad profile that is consistent with an accretion disk extending from 6 - 20 \ (Tanaka et al. 1995). This significant discovery is good cause to believe that frame dragging can be detected in an astrophysical setting.

gmw_00000136.name: MR 2251-178
gmw_00000136.overview: MR 2251-178 is a nearby, radio-quiet type-1 quasar [arXiv:2009.11294] that exhibits optical, near-infrared, and X-ray variability [arXiv:1107.0099, arXiv:1504.01226]. It is surrounded by a giant, counter-rotating emission-line nebula [arXiv:2009.11294] and features weak, double jet-like radio emission [arXiv:2009.11294, arXiv:1402.4483].

sources.summary: The paper presents MR 2251-178 as a nearby quasar whose optical-to-near-IR variability is dominated by disc emission, with weak direct evidence for torus variability.

quotes.quote: For MR 2251-178, a very nearby Quasar ( z=0.0640 ), a black hole mass of M 2 10^8 M_ was recently reported by Wang, Mao Wei (2009) using spectroscopic data and applying the luminosity-radius relation for the Broad Line Region as calibrated by Green Ho (2005). We have also obtained spectroscopic data for MR 2251-178 using the RC spectrograph on the 1.5m telescope at CTIO and operated by the SMARTS consortium. The data were obtained on the 20th of December 2006 and reduced and calibrated in the standard way. Following Peterson et al. \ (2004) we measure a line-width for H of 4,145 km/s.

No direct evidence for the presence of a torus is found in the lightcurves of MR2251-178 (see also Section 6.2 for further analysis), while disc emission is clearly seen from the optical to the near-IR bands, as determined by our analysis of the cross-correlation and flux-flux plots (Ar evalo et al., 2008; Section 5.2). In fact, the cross-correlation results set constraints to the distance between the regions of the disc responsible for the variable emission at optical and near-IR wavelengths, with no more than a few days as upper limits. In Figure 5 the distance from the centre of symmetry of the system to a radius of 1 light-day is represented by a ``CC'' horizontal bar. Also, for MR 2251-178 the difference spectrum presented in Figure 5 is steep all the way from the optical to the near-IR, while the fast variability spectrum, which is dominated by the X-ray reprocessing, shows a slightly flatter spectral shape.

sources.summary: The paper describes MR 2251-178 as a radio-quiet luminous quasar with variable sub-pc UFOs, a peculiar extended nebula, weak double jet-like radio emission, and possible tidal or AGN-driven gas origins.

quotes.quote: MR 2251-178 This system is a radio-quiet, type-1 luminous ( (L_ BOL / ) 12.15 , see Table tab:properties ) quasar, the first QSO to be identified via X-ray observations Cooke+78,Ricker+78 , and the first system for which X-ray absorption by warm photoionised gas was established Halpern+84 . The absorbing gas, variable in terms of ionisation state and column density Pan+90 and visible also in UV Ganguly+01 , was found to feature a high-velocity and highly-ionised outflowing component traced by \ Gibson+05 . In the last decade, the sub-pc scale UFO of MR 2251-178 has been characterised in detail thanks to several X-ray observations at different resolutions, energies and epochs, indicating spatially and temporally variable outflows with velocities in the range 0.04-0.14c Gofford+11, Gofford+13, Reeves+13 . The circumgalactic environment of MR 2251-178 is peculiar. The QSO lies in the outskirt of a loose, irregular cluster Phillips80 and is surrounded by a giant ( 200 wide) emission-line nebula detected in \ and \ Bergeron+83,diSeregoAlighieri+84,Macchetto+90 .

The nebula is characterised by a regular kinematics Shopbell+99 but in apparent counter-rotation with respect to the gas located within the host galaxy Norgaard-Nielsen+86 . While the filamentary morphology of the nebula resembles that of the gaseous envelopes surrounding powerful radio galaxies, this QSO shows only a weak radio emission with a double jet-like appearance Macchetto+90 . Both internal mechanisms (like AGN-driven winds) and external ones (merger debris, cooling flow) have been proposed as possible origins for this extended gaseous structure.

sources.summary: The paper models MR 2251 178 as a nearby radio-quiet quasar with complex X-ray absorption, soft excess/reflection possibilities, high bolometric luminosity, and an extended ionized nebula.

quotes.quote: We report on a comprehensive X-ray spectral analysis of the nearby radio-quiet quasar MR 2251 178, based on the long-look ( 400 ks) XMM--Newton observation carried out in November 2011. As the properties of the multiphase warm absorber (thoroughly discussed in a recent, complementary work) hint at a steep photoionizing continuum, here we investigate into the nature of the intrinsic X-ray emission of MR 2251 178 by testing several physical models. The apparent 2--10 keV flatness as well as the subtle broadband curvature can be ascribed to partial covering of the X-ray source by a cold, clumpy absorption system with column densities ranging from a fraction to several 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 .

The environment of MR 2251 178 is likewise exceptional. The quasar lies in the outskirts of a loose, irregular cluster with several tens of galaxies (Phillips 1980), and is surrounded by a huge emission-line nebula detected in H and [ O iii ] at optical wavelengths out to a distance of 100 kpc from the central source (Bergeron et al. 1983; Shopbell, Veilleux Bland-Hawthorn 1999). The knotty and filamentary appearance of this gaseous envelope is more typical of powerful radio galaxies, yet MR 2251 178 only shows weak radio emission, whose elongated morphology resembles a double-lobed jet-like structure (Macchetto et al. 1990).

sources.summary: The paper compares MR2251-178 with prior combined-dataset results, reporting two earlier warm absorber components and two components detected in this work.

quotes.quote: MR2251-178 -This same dataset had been studied by . However they have combined it with other datasets. The =1.54 0.024 .

They detected two warm absorber components U=-1.78 , =1 10^ 21.51 , and U=-4 , =2 10^ 20.06 . Note that they have used Tartar's definition of ionisation parameter (represented by U) and there is no direct conversion until we know the appropriate SED. We also detected two WA components.

sources.summary: The paper describes MR 2251-178 as a bright X-ray quasar with optical and near-IR variability links, beam-dependent radio spectra, flatter low-frequency slope, and positive 95 GHz excess.

quotes.quote: MR 2251-178 is a bright X-ray quasar that has been observed often in the X-rays. The X-ray light curve of MR 2251-178 closely correlates with the optical light curves over a period of 2.5 years arevalo08 . MR 2251-178 as well as NGC 3783 show evidence for variable emission in the near-IR lira11 .

However, large flux discrepancies between different beams that have an appreciable effect on the spectrum are found only for MR 2251-178 and for NGC 5548 (see Figure  sed ), indicating the flux of the other sources likely originates in the inner sub-arcsec region of the AGN.

MR 2251-178 and NGC 5548 have flatter spectra ( = 0.39, 0.14 , respectively), when the NVSS (45 ) flux density at 1.4 GHz is ignored. For the most part, the 95 GHz flux density overshoots the low-frequency slope. The excess factor for each source is given at the rightmost column of Table  coreflux , providing a range when two slopes with different beam sizes are available. The excess is large ( 4 - 7 ) for NGC 3783, ARK 564, and NGC 3227, and smaller (but still > 1 ) for MR 2251-178, NGC 5506, and NGC 7469.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes MR 2251 178 with multi-epoch NuSTAR data, describing it as a low-redshift AGN with weak FRI-like radio morphology and no conclusive coronal-temperature variation.

quotes.quote: In this work we aimed to investigate the variation in kT_ e in three AGN, namely NGC 3227, NGC 5548 and MR 2251 178 using more than one epoch of data on a source from NuSTAR . We carried out spectral analysis of multiple epochs of data acquired using NuSTAR on the three sources including a few new observations not published so far. By fitting Comptonization model to the data we determined the temperature of the corona and also investigated changes in kT_ e if any in these sources.

MR 2251 178, with a black hole mass of 2.0 10 ^8 M_ 2009AJ....137.3388W , first discovered by its strong X-ray emission 1978Natur.271...35R was found to be a low redshift AGN at z = 0.06 1983MNRAS.202..125B . From VLA observations, found the source to show weak radio emission with elongated morphology resembling a FRI source.
Conclusive evidence for the variation in kT_ e is not found in MR 2251 178 and NGC 5548.

sources.summary: The paper presents MR 2251+178 as an X-ray-detected quasar whose grating spectrum requires warm absorbers, emission lines, and low-ionization or neutral absorption components.

quotes.quote: MR 2251+178 ( z = 0.6398 0.00006, V 14 ) is the first quasar detected by X-ray observations. In Figure 1 we present the high-resolution grating spectrum obtained by the RGS on May 2002. The spectrum shows emission lines from N vi , O vii , O viii , Ne ix , and Ne x , as well as absorption lines from O iii , O iv , O v , O vi , Ne ix and Ne x .

Extrapolating this power law to lower energies revels the presence of a WA around 0.8 keV, an additional absorber below 0.5 keV, and some excess emission around 0.5 keV. Our best fitted model for these data yields a WA with a column density, N_ H , of 10^ 21.51 0.03 cm ^ 2 , ionization parameter, U_ OX , of 10^ 1.78 0.05 and a line of sight covering factor of 0.8. Assuming gas with the same properties produces the emission, we find a global covering factor of 0.3.

sources.summary: The paper reports a late-2020 obscuration event in radio-quiet quasar MR 2251-178, connecting soft X-ray dimming, possible two-decade recurrence, UV absorption, and simultaneous multiwavelength observations.

quotes.quote: An X-ray obscuration event in MR 2251-178 was caught in late 2020, which triggered multi-wavelength (NIR to X-ray) observations targeting this radio-quiet quasar. In the X-ray band, the obscurer leads to a flux drop in the soft X-ray band from late 2020 to early 2021. X-ray obscuration events might have a quasi-period of two decades considering earlier events in 1980 and 1996. In the UV band, a forest of weak blueshifted absorption features emerged in the blue wing of Ly 1216 in late 2020. Our XMM-Newton, NuSTAR, and HST/COS observations are obtained simultaneously, hence, the transient X-ray obscuration event is expected to account for the UV outflow, although they are not necessarily caused by the same part of the wind.

sources.summary: The paper identifies MR 2251-178 as one of five poorly fit Seyfert/BLRG spectra with high signal-to-noise Suzaku data and strong O 7/O 8 absorption requiring more detailed warm-absorber modeling.

quotes.quote: The majority of sources have spectra that are well-fit with this model. Five sources, however, are not well-fit, with reduced ^2 > 2.0 . These include NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, and MR 2251-178. Each of these sources have high signal-to-noise Suzaku spectra with exposures from 79--275 ks in length and have strong detections of O 7 and O 8 absorption. The complexity in the soft X-ray emission is not fully accounted for with the simple addition of the absorption edges. This is clear in the bottom panels of Figure fig-basemodel , where the ratio of data/model for three of these sources clearly shows structure that is not accounted for with our model.

sources.summary: The atlas gives MR 2251-178's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: MR 2251-178 is a quasar-regime AGN at a redshift of z= 0.0640 ( D 271 Mpc) with an optical Sy 1.5 classification veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . It was the first quasar discovered in X-rays and the second quasar with X-ray detection after 3C 273 ricker_discovery_1978 . Since then it has been extensively studied in X-rays and also belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample.

sources.summary: The paper places the predicted Ly location for MR2251 178 on the wing of intrinsic broad O 6 emission and attributes the narrow absorption to foreground lines and airglow.

quotes.quote: MR2251 178 The predicted location of Ly in this FUSE spectrum of MR2251 178 lies on the far wing of broad O 6 emission intrinsic to MR2251 178. The narrow absorption lines in the spectrum are foreground interstellar lines. Prominent N 1 airglow is present at 1134.

gmw_00000137.name: Mrk 3
gmw_00000137.overview: Mrk 3 is an S0 lenticular galaxy hosting a luminous Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2212.02513, arXiv:1709.09171] that is fed externally through a tidal interaction with the gas-rich spiral galaxy UGC 3422 [arXiv:2212.02513]. The galaxy features a nuclear stellar disk [arXiv:2212.02513], an asymmetric double radio source [arXiv:2005.08569], and a Compton-thick X-ray absorbing column [arXiv:1505.01166, arXiv:astro-ph/0003298].

sources.summary: The paper fits Suzaku spectra of Mrk 3 with a Compton-thick torus model, constraining its viewing geometry, column density, reflected emission, and luminosity time-lag interpretation.

quotes.quote: Our simulation results were applied to fit the wide-band spectrum of the Seyfert 2 galaxy Mrk 3 obtained by Suzaku . The spectral analysis indicates that we observe the nucleus along a line of sight intercepting the torus near its edge, and the column density along the equatorial plane was estimated to be 10 ^ 24 cm ^ 2 . Using this model, we can estimate the luminosities of both the direct emission and the emission irradiating the surrounding matter.

We fitted the Suzaku Mrk 3 spectrum with the new model based on our simulations, and found that the spectrum could be represented by our model. The structure parameters of the torus of Mrk 3 were estimated with the new model: _ i _ oa +1^ and N_ H 10^ 24 \ cm ^ 2 , although it was hard to constrain _ oa from our spectral analysis. We estimated the intrinsic luminosity of the direct component and the intrinsic luminosity irradiating the surrounding matter. Assuming _ oa =50 ^ and N_ H1 =0.76 N_ H2 , the 2--10 keV luminosity of the direct component was estimated to be about 2/5 of that irradiating the surrounding matter. This may be explained by time variability of Mrk 3 and time lag between the direct and reflected lights.

sources.summary: Mrk 3 is presented as a bright prototypical Seyfert-2 whose spectrum shows absorbed continuum, torus scattering and reflection, iron fluorescence, and complex soft X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: As an illustration, Figure fig:mrk3_spec (left panel) shows the Suzaku spectrum of the prototypical Seyfert-2 galaxy Mrk 3 ( z=0.0135 ), one of the brightest objects among the class cappi99 . Despite having an absorption column that is marginally Compton-Thick, we can see still see the direct (absorbed) power-law continuum at high-energies. We also see that some fraction of this continuum is scattered around the the torus. A cold X-ray reflection component from, presumably, Compton-Thick regions of the torus out of our line of sight produces a strong 6.4 keV iron fluorescence line and contributes significantly to the continuum above 5 keV. The soft-band is very complex sako00,poupag05,awaki08 , requiring photoionized emitters with at least three different ionization parameters as well as a collisionally ionized thermal plasma component (see figure caption).

For example, Figure fig:mrk3_spec (right panel) shows a simulation of a 200 ks SXS observation of Mrk 3. Comparing with the Suzaku /XIS spectrum, we can see that the forrest of soft X-ray emission lines is resolved, allowing us to employ the full machinery of line ratio diagnostics for density, temperature, and excitation mechanism.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 3 as a type II AGN whose HEG data resolve the Si line blend and whose line ratios are evaluated against photo-ionization expectations.

quotes.quote: The Si line (at 1.74 keV) is adjacent to the \ line at 1.745 keV. For Circinus, Mrk 3, and NGC 1068, their HEG data are deep enough to resolve the peaks of these two lines, thus allowing the two lines to be measured simultaneously. In particular, the line width of the Si line can be measured with a spectral resolution 4 times better than that at 6.4 keV where the Fe line resides. Measurements of the Si line of Circinus, Mrk 3, and NGC 1068 were presented in Liu16 .

For Circinus, Mrk 3, and NGC 1068, the fluxes of the \ line can be measured Liu16 and the \ Ly / \ ratios (corrected for Galactic absorption) are 0.38 0.11 , 0.69 0.33 , and 0.19 0.07 , respectively. For photo-ionized plasmas, the emissivity ratio between \ Ly and \ is around 0.1-0.25 e.g. Kin03 . Considering the uncertainties in the measurement, the \ Ly / \ ratios of Circinus and NGC 1068 are consistent with photo-ionization models. For Mrk 3, the ratio is larger than the predictions of photo-ionization models. It is likely that the \ Ly line is over-estimated due to blending with the Si line Liu16 .

sources.summary: The paper includes Mrk 3 in its Compton-thick AGN sample, using HETGS spectra to measure Si-line width, compare line blending, and estimate the fluorescent line-emitting radius.

quotes.quote: The line complexes around 1.74 keV of Circinus, Mrk 3, and NGC 1068 observed with HEG are plotted in Fig. 1. The data are binned to 0.005 with a minimum signal-to-noise ratio of 3.3. As can be seen, the line complexes are dominated by the Si line at 1.74 keV, but skewed toward higher energy where the \ line is located. The peaks of the two lines are resolved for Circinus and NGC 1068, while they are blended for Mrk 3.

The fitted line widths of the Si line are 1.4 0.6 , 1.8 0.8 , and 0.8 0.7 eV, for Circinus, Mrk 3, and NGC 1068, respectively. They correspond to FWHM velocities ( v_ FWHM ) of 570 240 , 730 320 , and 320 280 km \ s ^ 1 . Assuming the line-emitting material is in a virialized orbit around the black hole, its distance to the black hole can be estimated as Net90 =4c^2/3v_ FWHM ^2r_g , where r_g=GM_ BH /^2 . Adopting the black hole masses listed in Table 1, the distances of the line-emitting region are 0.03 pc, 4.9 pc, and 0.5 pc, for Circinus, Mrk 3, and NGC 1068, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper studies Markarian 3 as a Seyfert 2 galaxy whose NLR contains S-shaped hot X-ray gas, shocks, and evidence for both photoionization and collisional ionization.

quotes.quote: We study the prototypical Seyfert 2 galaxy, Markarian 3, based on imaging and high-resolution spectroscopy observations taken by the Chandra X-ray Observatory. We construct a deconvolved X-ray image, which reveals the S-shaped morphology of the hot gas in the narrow line region (NLR). While this morphology is similar to the radio and [ O III ] emission, the distribution of the X-ray gas is broader than that obtained at these other wavelengths. By mapping the density and temperature distribution of the hot gas in the NLR, we demonstrate the presence of shocks towards the west ( M=2.5^ +1.0 _ 0.6 ) and east ( M=1.5^ +1.0 _ 0.5 ).

Mrk 3 (UGC 3426) is an early-type (S0) galaxy Morphological classification taken from HyperLeda. at z=0.013509 , which hosts a luminous Seyfert 2 AGN. Given its brightness and proximity, Mrk 3 was the subject of a wide range of multi-wavelength observations. The Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) [ O III ] survey of nearby AGN showed that Mrk 3 is the second brightest source, after the spiral galaxy, NGC 1068 schmitt03 .

sources.summary: Mrk 3 exemplifies small-PAH-equivalent-width Seyfert 2s with hidden broad lines; its high X-ray absorbing column contrasts with a hardly absorbed MIR continuum.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of Mrk 3 is shown in Fig. mrk3 as an example of such a small PAH EW Sf2. Among these ten Sf2s, four have been observed in spectropolarimetry and/or near IR spectroscopy (Heisler et al. ; Young et al. ; Veilleux Goodrich Hill ). All four galaxies display broad-lines in polarized light. These are Mrk 3 , NGC 7674 , IRAS 05189-2524 and NGC 4388 .

For instance, Mrk 3 has an X-ray absorbing column N_ H = 10^ 24 cm^ 2 (Turner et al ) corresponding to a 7 m extinction of 9 magnitudes, amply sufficient to block-out the MIR continuum. Nevertheless, the MIR continuum of Mrk 3 (see Fig. mrk3 ) is hardly absorbed. This indicates that our line-of-sight to the X-ray source is different from our line-of-sight to the MIR source, the former intercepting a much larger fraction of the torus than the latter.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 3 as a nearby S0 Seyfert 2 with a hidden Seyfert 1 nucleus, intricate narrow-line morphology, radio jets, and an unusually extended coronal-line region.

quotes.quote: Mrk 3 Mrk 3 is a nearby S0 galaxy. Although it is classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy from optical spectroscopy, it shows evidence of a hidden Seyfert 1 nucleus in polarised light (Miller Goodrich 1990). The morphology of the inner regions of Mrk 3 is highly intricate.

Higher ionisation lines are not present in the optical spectrum of Mrk 3 and, to our knowledge, neither in the infrared region (Osterbrock, Shaw Veilleux 1990; Wilson Nath 1990; Heisler De Robertis 1999; Knop et al. 2001). This makes Mrk 3 one of the lowest ionisation galaxies of the whole sample. Interestingly, Mrk 3 exhibits the most extended CLR of the ten AGN studied here (see Table extension ).

sources.summary: The paper studies Mrk 3 as a Compton-thick Seyfert 2, finding nuclear MIR lines, silicate absorption, extended circumnuclear emission, and a dusty torus model consistent with X-ray absorption.

quotes.quote: Mid-infrared (MIR) spectra observed with Gemini/Michelle were used to study the nuclear region of the Compton-thick Seyfert 2 (Sy 2) galaxy Mrk 3 at a spatial resolution of 200 pc. No polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) emission bands were detected in the N-band spectrum of Mrk 3. However, intense [ Ar iii ] 8.99 m, [ S iv ] 10.5 m and [ Ne ii ] 12.8 m ionic emission-lines, as well as silicate absorption feature at 9.7 m have been found in the nuclear extraction ( 200 pc). We also present subarcsecond-resolution Michelle N-band image of Mrk 3 which resolves its circumnuclear region. This diffuse MIR emission shows up as a wings towards East-West direction closely aligned with the S-shaped of the Narrow Line Region (NLR) observed at optical [ O iii ] 5007 image with Hubble/FOC .

The nuclear continuum spectrum can be well represented by a theoretical torus spectral energy distribution (SED), suggesting that the nucleus of Mrk 3 may host a dusty toroidal structure predicted by the unified model of active galactic nucleus (AGN). In addition, the hydrogen column density (N _H = 4.8^ +3.3 _ 3.1 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ) estimated with a torus model for Mrk 3 is consistent with the value derived from X-ray spectroscopy.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 3 Si-line measurements and higher-order HEG Fe-line data to show first-order Fe widths are overestimated and agree with Si-line velocities.

quotes.quote: In a recent paper Liu16b , we measured the FWHM of the Si line (at 1.74 keV) for Circinus, Mrk 3, and NGC 1068, which are 570 240 , 730 320 , and 320 280 km \ s ^ 1 , respectively. These values are 3-5 times smaller than those measured from the Fe line previously, and the estimated line-emitting regions are outside the dust sublimation radii of these AGN. It indicates that the intrinsic widths of the Fe line are likely to be over-estimated.

While for Mrk 3, NGC 1068, NGC 4388, and NGC 4507, the measured line widths are also smaller than those measured from the first order HEG spectra. The results clearly show that the line widths of the Fe line measured from the first order HEG spectra are over-estimated.
The corresponding FWHM velocities of the Fe line of Mrk 3 and NGC 1068 are consistent with those obtained from the Si line in Liu16b .

sources.summary: Mrk 3 is treated as a Compton-thick Seyfert 2 example whose nuclear spectra lack PAH bands and whose IRS-cleaned spectrum still differs because heavy absorption raises the Spitzer continuum.

quotes.quote: Also, no PAH emission bands were detected in the nuclear region ( 200 pc) of Mrk 3, a Compton-thick Sy 2, using Michelle/Gemini spectrograph sal14 . dav12 argued that the PAH molecules can not survive in a radius smaller than 50 pc, a value corresponding to a region larger than that of the torus extension parsec scale, tran92 .

To illustrate the effect of the starburst subtraction method, we show in Figure high the high resolution data from Michelle and T-ReCs of the galaxies Mrk 3, NGC 1386 and NGC 7213, compared to the IRS spectra. Also shown is the final spectra resulting from the subtraction of the PAH components. The clean IRS spectra tends to better approximate the nuclear spectra from high resolution observations, except for the Mrk 3. This is possibly due to the fact that this galaxy is a Compton-thick object and has a heavy absorbed dust/gas component N_ H 1.1 10^ 24 cm^ 2 , sal14 obscuring the nucleus, leading to a higher continuum in the Spitzer observation. Moreover, the fact of this galaxy has a small starburst contribution may explain why we see almost no differences between the IRS spectra and the subtracted one.

sources.summary: The paper reports a centrally peaked [ FeII ] line-to-continuum profile in Mrk 3, non-rotational or non-planar gas motions, and a centrally peaked velocity-dispersion profile across its central kiloparsec.

quotes.quote: The H _2 line-to-continuum ratio is constant or drops significantly towards the nucleus in most galaxies, except NGC 2273, NGC 5273, and NGC 7450. For Br , in contrast, the ratio peaks towards the nucleus in many galaxies, consistent with stronger ionization mechanisms towards the active nucleus. These two results both explain the observed central drop of the H _2 /Br ratio. Of the three emission lines, the [ FeII ] line-to-continuum ratio shows the most distinct radial variations. Its profile is centrally peaked in Mrk 1, Mrk 3, NGC 262, NGC 1320, NGC 2273, NGC 3081, NGC 5273, and NGC 7450. Several sources show two maxima (or a central drop, e.g. NGC 1667, NGC 3185, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, NGC 4388, and NGC 5506). Finally, Mrk 1066, NGC 931, NGC 2110, NGC 2992, and NGC 4051 show peculiar distributions, e.g. the distribution in NGC 2110 shows three separate peaks.

However, the gas velocity curves are similar to those of the stars, which define the galaxy disk. This is the case in Mrk 1, Mrk 1066, NGC 262, NGC 931, NGC 1667, NGC 2110, NGC 2273, NGC 3081, NGC 3185, NGC 4388, NGC 5273, and NGC 5506. In these galaxies, all three emission lines display a velocity curve that follows that of the stars and is reminiscent of rotation, thus strongly implying that the line-emitting gas is located in the disk of the galaxy and rotating with the disk. Other galaxies (Mrk 3, NGC 1320, NGC 2992, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, and NGC 7450) display velocity curves different from the stellar velocity curve in one or more emission lines, which may be a signature of non-rotational motion, either in- or outflows or other non-planar motion of the gas.

The radial dispersion profiles in Mrk 3, Mrk 1066, NGC 2110, NGC 2273, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, NGC 5273, and NGC 5506 have a central peak, which indicates a higher kinetic gas temperature in the nucleus. Either the ISM is more turbulent (possibly caused by a jet- or wind-driven outflow), and/or more intensely heated by the incident radiation field. In either case, a peaked dispersion distribution is a strong indication for the AGN governing the physical state of the gas at the nucleus. The dispersion peak is most often present in [ FeII ] (indicating dust destruction close to the nucleus), and/or in Br (indicating a strong radiation field), while the H _2 line shows the least-pronounced central rise. Examples for flat velocity dispersion profiles are NGC 3081, NGC 3185, and NGC 4388, which show no significant change over up to 1.2 kpc in diameter. The velocity dispersion in all three sources is of order 100 km/s, well within the range observed for star forming regions, which is consistent with star formation being the dominant the gas excitation mechanism. Finally, the dispersion profiles in Mrk 1, NGC 262, NGC 931, NGC 1320, NGC 1667, NGC 2992, and NGC 7450 show marked differences between the three emission lines, with variations of 100 km/s or more at a given radius. This indicates that the ionized atomic and molecular gas is responding differently to the central AGN in these sources, perhaps due to the presences of asymmetric energy input (a jet or shocks) together with a stratified medium, e.g. a thin molecular disk in a thick ionized medium.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk3 as a local comparison galaxy whose regional UV and Ly-alpha spectral data trace intrinsic gas conditions and follow the paper's AGN-plus-shock model trends.

quotes.quote: Also shown in Fig. 8 are the spectral data observed from the local galaxy Mrk3 (Collins et al 2005) because they include the and UV lines. The data (reddening corrected) refer to different regions within the galaxy. The spectra represent average conditions covering small regions within the galaxy. So they tend to show intrinsic conditions of the gas rather than the averaged ones obtained by modelling the observed spectra covering the entire galaxy, as necessarily occurs for high z galaxies. The Mrk3 data are less scattered than those observed from high z galaxies and follow the model trends.

sources.summary: The paper describes MARK 3 as a type 2 Seyfert with polarized broad lines, radio double nucleus, extended soft X-ray emission, and Compton-thick absorption.

quotes.quote: MARK 3 It was optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert galaxy see an optical spectrum in Appendix multiimages khachikian1974 . Broad lines have been found in polarized light millergoodrich1990 . A high resolution image at 2 cm with VLA data shows a double nucleus at radio frequencies ulvestadwilson1984 . This galaxy shows extended soft X-ray emission perpendicular to the IR emission and a point-like source at hard X-rays (see Appendix multiimages ). It is also a Compton thick source bassani1999,goulding2012 , with a column density of 1.1 10^ 24 cm^ 2 measured with BeppoSAX cappi1999 .

sources.summary: The paper finds its initial models inadequate for Mrk 3 below 5 keV, excludes that range, and obtains an accepted fit consistent with earlier NuSTAR analyses.

quotes.quote: All the models we considered seemed insufficient to provide a good fit for Mrk 3. A visual examination of the fit residuals revealed a higher emission than expected by the models in the energy range below 5 keV. This could be the result of a warm absorber in the AGN environment or just an artefact produced by adding the 11 individual spectra of this source. Because the response of NuSTAR is less well calibrated in the 3-5 keV energy range 2015ApJS..220....8M , we decided to ignore the data below 5 keV for this source. Thus, we obtained a statistically accepted fit, with ^2/dof = 2202 / 2106 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Mkn3 as an asymmetric double radio source; its new maps resolve an E-W double with steep components and possible hidden optically thin jet emission.

quotes.quote: Mkn3 Previous VLA maps reveal an asymmetric double morphology extending for 600 pc in the E-W direction, with the two components having steep spectra e.g. WilsonPooleyWillisClements1980 . High resolutions observations resolve it into eight components in an S-shaped structure, with the closest component to the optical position having flat spectrum and recognized as the nucleus e.g. Kukula1993,Kukula1999 . Our radio maps reveal a double morphology in the E-W direction, with components which are 1.5 arcsec apart ( 400 pc). Our maps reveal a double morphology in the E-W direction, on a scale of 1 kpc, in which both the components have steep spectra. The low (0.6) concentration index of the core (East) component suggests that additional optically-thin, steep spectrum jet components may hide in the kpc-component and significantly contribute at these frequencies.

sources.summary: Markarian 3 is discussed as a Seyfert 2 HETG target with jointly fitted spectra, thick-slab-like fluorescence ratios, and marginal Mg and Si excess-column requirements.

quotes.quote: Markarian 3 is a Seyfert 2 ( z=0.013 ), which was observed by the Chandra HETG nine times in 2000 and in 2011 for a total of 389250 s (see Table tab:obs ). These spectra were reported by sako2000chandra and bogdan2017probing . We performed the data fits simultaneously on all observed spectra.

From the graph we see that the measurements from NGC 1068, MRK 3, and NGC 4151 are well described by Equation eq:thick_slab . Fluorescence lines from M51 xu2015x , though not measured with gratings, show a similar trend. The nominal ratios imply the line-of-sight towards the fluorescing medium, as opposed to that towards the Seyfert 2 nucleus, is unobstructed. Vela X-1 also shows a decent agreement with I_f^ thick , which implies that the observed fluxes may be explained by reflection from a high N_ H wind. This is in contrast to sako1999x who assumed reflection from a low N_ H stellar wind, which caused the deduced N_ H values to increase with Z . Unlike the other sources, Circinus and GX 301-2 show a systematic discrepancy of all low-Z elements with theoretical values of I_f^ thick . The following sections will attempt to provide an explanation to this discrepancy. Resonant Auger Destruction sako1999x proposed that Auger destruction may have a significant effect on fluorescence lines in Vela X-1 (see Section sec:intro ). A later theoretical analysis done by liedahl2005resonant showed the process to be complex and highly dependent on the ionization and excitation state of each element. Indeed, K lines of L-shell Si in a laboratory photo-ionized plasma experiment showed no evidence of resonant Auger destruction loisel2017benchmark . Despite the complexity, several restrictions are immediately evident. First, K Auger destruction cannot occur in a neutral medium as it requires an L-shell vacancy. Hence, higher Z elements require higher ionization for K photo-excitation to occur. K , in contrast, can be quenched by Auger destruction in lower charge states. Additionally, the energy difference between the K energy of different L-shell Si ions is 10 eV. Thus, the velocity shear required to kinematically mix neighboring ions needs to be 1500 km s ^ 1 , which is not observed. In a partially ionized medium, the ions may act as resonant absorbers for the emitted K lines. Since _ K 1 , we can approximate the resulting Auger destruction as restricting our line of sight into the medium up to a photo-excitation optical depth of unity, ^ PE =1 . The absorption cross section at the center of a resonant line under assumption of a Gaussian line profile is: ^ PE = e^2 f m_e 2 where e is the electron charge, m_e is the mass of the electron, is the speed of light, f is the oscillator strength and is the Doppler width (standard deviation). We assume / = 0.001, which is just under the HETG resolving power. The column density of an element Z for an optical depth ^ PE =N_ Z ^ PE = 1 is: N_ Z = m_e 2 e^2 f Since N_ Z =A_ Z N_ H , the corresponding N_ H for abundant elements is smaller than for rare elements. Consequently, for abundant elements N_ H <10^ 22 cm ^ 2 and thus (E) ^ PE = 1 . We can therefore use Equation eq:thin_slab to get I_f^ thin in the case of dominant Auger destruction: I_f^ thin = 1 + 1.65 I_0 E_0^ _ K m_e 2 e^2 f E_ K,Z ^ 1- _ Z,K (E_ K,Z ) Equation eq:thin_Auger is independent of A_ Z , so all abundant elements will have similar I_f^ thin . For the rarer elements (E) ^ PE = 1 no longer holds. Thus, we must use the accurate expression (Equation eq:fluorescence with the corresponding N_ H ). Another possible approximation is for Auger destruction in an optically thick medium. We assume the cross section for resonant absorption to be _ eff,Z = (1- _ K ,Z ) _ Z ^ PE , effectively assuming the scattering is destructive with probability (1- _ K ,Z ) . This approximation effectively adds A_Z _ eff,Z to the denominator of Eq eq:thick_slab , which yields similar results to Equation eq:fluorescence with the N_ H corresponding to Equation eq:Nz . Both approximations are shown in Figure fig:Auger model compare , neither of which produces a good agreement with any observation. Selective Auger destruction could explain the low-Z relative intensities of Circinus, if somehow Fe is unaffected. The K /K nominal ratios presented in Sec. sec:kb (e.g., 0.14 for Circinus) further argue against a significant role of Auger destruction, which is discussed in Section sec:discussion . Excess Line of Sight Column Density We propose a simple explanation to the relatively weak low- Z lines observed in several of the present measurements. The deficiency of the low-Z relative intensities with respect to I_f^ thick (Figure fig:ka model compare ) decreases with Z. Therefore, excess line of sight column density beyond the Galactic column could explain the anomalous ratios. If correct, a single column density value N_ H ^ ex for each object would need to explain the quenching of the fluorescence lines of all low-Z elements. We can then use the measured fluxes to estimate N_ H ^ ex self-consistently for each object. The observed intensity ratio with an additional column density N_ H ^ ex would be: I_ f,Z ^ obs I_ f,Fe ^ obs = I_ f,Z ^ thick e^ (E_ K,Z ) N_ H ^ ex I_ f,Fe ^ thick e^ (E_ K,Fe ) N_ H ^ ex Thus we can extract N_ H ^ ex for each element: N_ H ^ ex = 1 (E_ K,Fe )- (E_ K,Z ) I_ f,Z ^ obs I_ f,Fe ^ obs I_ f,Fe ^ thick I_ f,Z ^ thick N_ H ^ ex as derived by Si K is the most reliable estimate, as it is the best measured line except for Fe K , and their cross section difference is the second highest. For Si Equation extra_nh_eq gives: N_ H ^ ex ( Si ) = 2.42 10^ 22 0.104 I_ f,Fe ^ obs I_ f,Si ^ obs In extra-galactic sources excess column density can be associated with dust extinction and reddening. In NGC 1068 the above line ratios show only marginal evidence for N_ H ^ ex , which is consistent with the low reddening towards the narrow line region of NGC 1068 A_V<0.15 , crenshaw2000resolved . In Mrk 3 only Mg and Si K require marginal excess column density of N_ H ^ ex ( Mg )=1.1^ +0.3 _ 0.6 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 and N_ H ^ ex ( Si )=0.4 0.3 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 respectively, while other elements provide only an upper limit of N_ H ^ ex <2 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper compares Mrk 3 with NGC 4151, describing its line-dominated HETG spectrum, photoionized/photoexcited plasma, weak iron L-shell emission, and limited triplet constraints.

quotes.quote: The comparison with the HETG observation of Mrk 3 The HETG observation of Mrk 3 was the first published high resolution soft X-ray spectrum of a Seyfert 2 galaxy and showed unambiguously that the soft X-ray emission in Mrk 3 is composed almost entirely of emission lines and RRC, with little or no continuum emission.
