sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 509 monitoring as one AGN basis for simulated lightcurves, specifying its observing span, DRW parameters, and a case where ICCF lag estimates performed poorly.

quotes.quote: We use the Swift UVW2-band lightcurves for the other three AGNs. Swift monitored NGC 4151 in 2016 from February 20 to April 29, yielding a lightcurve that consists of 319 visits with nearly five visits per day Edelson2017 . The lightcurve of NGC 4593 contains 148 epochs with a cadence of about 96 minutes from July 13 to July 18 in 2016 and a cadence of about 192 minutes in the following 16.2 days McHardy2018 . The observations of Mrk 509 span from March 17 to December 15 in 2017 with 257 epochs separated by about one day Edelson2019 .

We adopted DRW models with the parameters ( _ DRW , _ DRW ) = (17.38,125) , (0.72,136) , (0.49,86) , (0.77,146) for NGC 5548, NGC 4151, NGC 4593 and Mrk 509, respectively, where _ DRW is in units of days and _ DRW is in the same flux units as the observed lightcurves. We created 200 realizations of the simulated continuum for each object.
The only poorly estimated lags, in the sense that the medians of (t_ fit t_0) are more than 2 _ est , are the CCCD estimates for Mrk 509 with a median (t_ fit t_0) = - 1.2 days and _ est = 0.38 days.

sources.summary: The paper separates Mrk 509 into connected and detached outflow regions; the western, weaker region B is identified as a likely fossil outflow.

quotes.quote: Mrk 509 has an outflow that can be spatially separated into two regions: region A is connected to the nucleus, while region B is clearly spatially detached Fig. 6 Zanchettin2021AA . Both regions are also clearly offset to the west of the nucleus. Region B has lower M , p and E than region A (by factors 6, 6 and 10 respectively). Even though both regions have rather low energy loading factors, region B can be identified as a noticeably weaker fossil.

sources.summary: The paper reports Mrk 509 as unabsorbed in the simplified AIC model, contrasting that with prior campaign results showing multiple spectral and absorption components.

quotes.quote: Mrk 509 This source has been largely studied, in particular in a campaign lead by K2011 , where we can find reports on the many components of the spectrum. In particular, De2011 finds multiple absorption systems, with three different velocity components. This is not consistent with our results, where this source appears unabsorbed, according to the AIC. This is an example of how an over-simplified model like ours does not recover the spectral complexity of the source.

sources.summary: Mrk 509 is treated as an exception with a measured lag far below prediction, requiring lower covering factor but still entering the core sample through consistent lag methods.

quotes.quote: Here one exception is Mrk 509. Our result is far smaller than the predicted one.
But for NGC 4593 and Mrk 509, we found that if we use c_f = 0.2 , the predicted lags are too big, so we use c_f = 0.1 instead, as in 2022MNRAS.509.2637N .

We select a core sample from our parent sample of 6 AGNs, including 4 targets, NGC 5548, NGC 2617, Mrk 142 and Mrk 509, because they have consistent lag measurements by both the ICCF-Cut method and the JAVELIN Pmap Model.

sources.summary: The atlas gives Mrk 509's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: Mrk 509 is an early-type galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0344 ( D 141 Mpc) with a bright Sy 1.5 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 that has been extensively studied at all wavelengths (see kaastra_multiwavelength_2011 for a recent detailed study). The nucleus is known to be variable at almost all wavelengths, also in the infrared glass_long-term_2004 . Mrk 509 possesses a very extended NLR ( 10 6.4 kpc; phillips_outflow_1983 ) and also belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample.

sources.summary: The paper compares MRK 509 warm absorber measurements, citing three components in prior work and two warm absorber components detected here.

quotes.quote: MRK 509 - have studied this dataset and found three WA components, with =0.89 , =2.14 , and =3.26 . The slope =2.14 . We detected two WA components.

sources.summary: The paper says the host is not a typical elliptical in the HST image, instead showing spiral structures, dust features, and a ring-like component.

quotes.quote: Mrk 509 (Fig. 11.17) --- \ \ From ground-based NIR data, the host galaxy appears as an elliptical (e.g., Fischer et al. 2006). However, the \ image shows that it s not a typical elliptical galaxy as it has spiral structures, dusty features, and a ring. We model it with a small bulge, a disk, and a ring-like structure (outer ring); the latter is modeled with the hybrid method.

sources.summary: The paper says Mrk 509 has complex intrinsic UV absorption tied to X-ray warm absorbers, with observed narrow features attributed to intrinsic or foreground absorption.

quotes.quote: Mrk 509 As with many objects in this sample, Mrk 509 has a complex set of intrinsic UV absorption lines associated with a corresponding set of X-ray warm absorbers. The UV absorption spans a velocity range of 400 km s^ 1 to +200 km s^ 1 Kriss00,Kriss11b , similar to the soft X-ray absorption seen in the X-ray spectrum Detmers11,Kaastra11c . All the deep, narrow absorption features in Figures fig:1q - fig:1s are associated either with the intrinsic absorption features in Mrk 509 or foreground interstellar lines.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 509 as a well-studied, extremely bright, nearby Seyfert galaxy with redshift z 0.03 and foreground column density higher than Mrk 421.

quotes.quote: Mrk 509 is a well-studied and extremely bright Seyfert galaxy 42 . It is also extremely close with a redshift z 0.03 , compared to 1ES 1553 with a redshift of z 0.36 . In addition, it has a column density of N_ H = 4.17 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 2 , which is significantly higher than Mrk 421.

gmw_00000141.name: Mrk 520
gmw_00000141.overview: Mrk 520 is a peculiar galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0266 that features wide tidal arms [arXiv:1310.2770]. The galaxy hosts a Seyfert 1.9 nucleus classified as an active galactic nucleus and starburst composite [arXiv:1310.2770], which exhibits a diffuse millimeter-wave morphology [arXiv:2309.02776].

sources.summary: The paper describes diffuse high-resolution mm-wave morphology in Mrk 520 and compares it with coarser 5 GHz radio emission near the mm-wave peak.

quotes.quote: 91: Mrk 520 Our mm-wave image at a high resolution of 0 06, corresponding to 30 pc for the object, shows that overall the morphology looks diffuse. This can be noticed more clearly by considering fainter emission at 3 _ mm (dotted lines) additionally. A 5 GHz image was presented at a much coarser resolution of 1 4 by , and radio emission has a core-like morphology. This radio emission seems to coincide with the peak of the mm-wave image. However, to confirm whether most of the radio emission is indeed associated with the mm-wave peak or appears like the diffuse mm-wave emission, it is important to obtain a higher-resolution radio image.

sources.summary: The atlas gives Mrk 520's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: Mrk 520 is a peculiar galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0266 ( D 115 Mpc) with wide tidal arms and a compact object (LEDA 200376) superimposed 21 to the south-east, which is most likely a star (see 2MASS images). Mrk 520 contains a little-studied Sy 1.9 nucleus lonsdale_vlbi_1992 that belongs to nine-month BAT AGN sample. Furthermore, the nucleus is classified as an AGN/starburst composite by , and we adopt this classification.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 520 as one of five objects with very large iron-K equivalent width because the reflection continuum is weak, interpreting its torus as Compton-thin and reproducible with a Monte-Carlo torus model.

quotes.quote: In the rest of objects (NGC 4388, NGC 5252, LEDA 170194, Mrk 520, and Mrk 915), the EW is very large ( >3 keV) because the reflection continuum (i.e., R ) is apparently very weak. We interpret that the tori in these objects are Compton-thin, thus producing a much weaker hump at 30 keV and absorption iron-K edge features than what the pexrav model predicts. In fact, we confirm that a Monte-Carlo based torus model by can well reproduce the spectra of these objects including the continuum and iron-K emission line, as done in , , and .

gmw_00000142.name: Mrk 573
gmw_00000142.overview: Mrk 573 is a double-barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1505.01166, arXiv:1609.08927] containing a Seyfert 2 nucleus [arXiv:1704.08143, arXiv:1505.01166] that has also been classified as an obscured narrow-line Seyfert 1 [arXiv:1002.3152, arXiv:1310.2770]. The active nucleus drives a compact outflow [arXiv:1609.08927] and exhibits a biconical extended emission-line region [arXiv:1203.1279, arXiv:1310.2770] aligned with a triple radio source [arXiv:1505.01166].

sources.summary: The paper presents deep Chandra observations of Mrk 573, resolving ionizing-cone substructures, photoionized bicone gas, shock-related thermal components, nuclear variability, and extended emission inconsistent with a fully obscuring torus.

quotes.quote: We present results on Mrk 573 obtained as part of the CHandra survey of Extended Emission-line Regions in nearby Seyfert galaxies (CHEERS). Previous studies showed that this source features a biconical emission in the soft X-ray band closely related with the Narrow Line Region as mapped by the [ O iii ] emission line and the radio emission, though on a smaller scale; we investigate the properties of soft X-ray emission from this source with new deep Chandra observations. Making use of the subpixel resolution of the Chandra /ACIS image and PSF- deconvolution, we resolve and study substructures in each ionizing cone. The two cone spectra are fitted with photoionization model, showing a mildly photoionized phase diffused over the bicone. Thermal collisional gas at about 1.1 keV and 0.8 keV appears to be located between the nucleus and the ``knots" resolved in radio observations, and between the ``arcs" resolved in the optical images, respectively; this can be interpreted in terms of shock interaction with the host galactic plane.

The nucleus shows a significant flux decrease across the observations indicating variability of the AGN, with the nuclear region featuring higher ionization parameter with respect to the bicone region. The long exposure allows us to find extended emission up to 7 kpc from the nucleus along the bicone axis. Significant emission is also detected in the direction perpendicular to the ionizing cones, disagreeing with the fully obscuring torus prescribed in the AGN unified model, and suggesting instead the presence of a clumpy structure.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 573 as a barred Seyfert with rich coronal lines, possible NLS1 classification, STIS coverage aligned with ionization structures, and extended low- to medium-ionization gas.

quotes.quote: Mrk 573 This barred Seyfert 2 galaxy displays a very rich coronal line spectrum, from optical to near-infrared wavelengths (e.g., Koski 1978; Tsvetanov Walsh 1992; Storchi-Bergmann et al. 1996; Riffel, Rodr iguez-Ardila Pastoriza 2006; Mullaney Ward 2008). Recently, Ramos Almeida et al. (2008) proposed that Mrk 573 is, in fact, a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy (NLS1) rather than a Seyfert 2 based on the observations of O i and Fe ii permitted lines in the near-infrared. These features are characteristics of only type 1 galaxies, as they are produced in the BLR.

The HST/STIS spectra of this galaxy were obtained with the slit oriented at a PA of 71 , that is, nearly aligned to both the [ O iii ] ionisation cone (PA=120 ) reported by Regan Mulchaey (1999) and to the radio jet, whose NW component lies at PA= 51 (Nagar et al. 1999). Previous reports pointed out that the [ O iii ] emission is extended along the same direction as the radio emission (Haniff, Wilson Ward 1988; Pogge De Robertis 1995). The blue nuclear spectrum reveals strong coronal lines of [ Ne v ] , [ Fe vii ] , and [ Fe xiv ] , in addition to forbidden low- and medium-ionisation lines of [ O ii ] , [ Ne iii ] , and [ O iii ] , as well as recombination lines of H i and He ii . The red spectra display [ Fe x ] , along with low-ionisation lines of [ O i ] , [ N ii ] and [ S ii ] , as well as H .

sources.summary: The paper maps Mrk 573’s near-infrared stellar populations, finding old stars dominate the inner region, intermediate-age populations outside, and nuclear hot-dust and featureless-continuum components.

quotes.quote: We present a near infrared study of the spectral components of the continuum in the inner 500 500 pc ^2 of the nearby Seyfert galaxy Mrk 573 using adaptive optics near-infrared integral field spectroscopy with the instrument NIFS of the Gemini North Telescope at a spatial resolution of 50 pc. We performed spectral synthesis using the starlight code and constructed maps for the contributions of different age components of the stellar population: young ( age 100 Myr), young-intermediate ( 100<age 700 Myr), intermediate-old ( 700 Myr <age 2 Gyr) and old ( age>2 Gyr) to the near-IR K-band continuum, as well as their contribution to the total stellar mass. We found that the old stellar population is dominant within the inner 250 pc, while the intermediate age components dominate the continuum at larger distances. A young stellar component contributes up to 20 within the inner 70 pc, while hot dust emission and featureless continuum components are also necessary to fit the nuclear spectrum, contributing up to 20 of the K-band flux there.

Mrk 573 is a nearly face-on, early-type galaxy, morphologically classified as RSAB(rs) ^+ vaucoleurs91 , presenting a bright extended emission line region ( 1 kpc) along the direction of its radio emission unger87,haniff88 and high-ionization emission-lines thaisa96 . Three radio continuum sources were first detected by ulvestad84 , one in the nucleus of the galaxy and the two radio lobes along position angle PA=122^ . It harbors a Seyfert 2 nucleus tsvetanov92 and is located at a distance of 73 Mpc springob05 , for which 1 ^ corresponds to 350 pc at the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents infrared and optical kinematics of Seyfert 2 galaxy Mrk 573, linking its NLR gas to disk rotation plus in situ acceleration from fueling flows and finding compact outflows.

quotes.quote: We present near-infrared and optical emission-line and stellar kinematics of the Seyfert 2 galaxy Mrk 573 using the Near-Infrared Field Spectrograph (NIFS) at Gemini North and Dual Imaging Spectrograph (DIS) at Apache Point Observatory, respectively. By obtaining full kinematic maps of the infrared ionized and molecular gas and stellar kinematics in a 700 2100 pc ^2 circumnuclear region of Mrk 573, we find that kinematics within the Narrow-Line Region (NLR) are largely due to a combination of both rotation and in situ acceleration of material originating in the host disk. Combining these observations with large-scale, optical long-slit spectroscopy that traces ionized gas emission out to several kpcs, we find that rotation kinematics dominate the majority of the gas. We find that outflowing gas extends to distances less than 1 kpc, suggesting that outflows in Seyfert galaxies may not be powerful enough to evacuate their entire bulges.

Seyfert 2 AGN Mrk 573 was chosen as an ideal candidate to investigate the connection between fueling flows, in the form of dust spirals Pog02,Sim07 , and outflows of ionized gas in AGN, as the central engine is radiating near the Eddington limit (log( M_ BH /M_ ) = 7.28, L _ bol = 10^ 45.5 , Bia07,Kra09 ). As an (R)SAB0+(rs) spiral galaxy, Mrk 573 has an outer ring, inner ring and host disk generally aligned along a PA of 90 , with weak arms forming a bar along a PA 0 , visible in SDSS imaging as shown in Figure fig:structure .

sources.summary: Mrk 573 is discussed as a classical Sy2 or obscured NLSy1 whose X-ray spectrum is reflection dominated and whose intrinsic AGN luminosity is inferred from Fe-line diagnostics.

quotes.quote: Mrk 573. Mrk 573 is optically classified as a classical Sy2 nucleus Tsvetanov92 , and Ramos08 recently re-classify it as an obscured NLSy1 galaxy, based on near-IR spectroscopy and X-ray archival data. The X-ray observations of Mrk 573 do not straightforwardly reveal the intrinsic luminosity of this AGN because it suffers from Compton thick obscuration Guainazzi05 . The strong soft X-ray emission is likely photoionized line emission. It is not the AGN continuum in any case, so previous simplified fits of power law models over a broad energy range e.g., Guainazzi05,Shu07 fail to accurately measure the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper finds UGC 1214 is the clearest case where scaled [O III] accounts for filamentary near-UV structure, leaving diffuse central light and a substantial nebular contribution.

quotes.quote: UGC 1214 (Sy2) We used the procedure explained above, with f =0.30, and end up with a residual image free from the filamentary structure observed in both [ OIII ] and F330W images. This is the best example in which a reasonable scaling of the [ OIII ] image can account for all the filamentary structure. From this we deduce that 40 per cent of the near-UV light comes from nebular continuum and [ NeV ] emission. The resulting image shows a central oval structure (see Fig. fig:all3_01 ) of diffuse light. Although this object has no F547M exposure, it does have a NICMOS image which is dominated by the bulge.

sources.summary: The paper describes MARK 573 as a double-barred type 2 Seyfert with dust lanes, triple radio source, extended soft X-ray emission, and Compton-thick candidate status.

quotes.quote: MARK 573 MARK 573 (also called UCG 1214) is a double-barred galaxy that shows dust lanes see also Appendix multiimages martini2001 . It was optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert galaxy osterbrock1993 . Observations at 6 cm with VLA showed a triple radio source ulvestadwilson1984 . A point-like source is observed at hard X-rays, while extended emission can be observed at soft X-rays, aligned with the bars (see Appendix multiimages ). It was classified as a Compton thick candidate guainazzi2005a,bianchi2010,severgnini2012 .

sources.summary: Mrk 573 has a hard, double-cone ENLR with uniformly high oxygen abundance, low reddening, evidence for dust destruction, and possible AGN-driven mixing over kiloparsec scales.

quotes.quote: Mrk 573: Uniformly high abundances are observed across the 6 kpc of the ENLR, with the highest oxygen abundance of 12 + O/H = 9.1 ( 3 Z_ ) found in the nucleus, and measurements up to only 0.1 dex lower elsewhere in the ENLR (except for another anomalous outer bin).

In this ENLR we see a double-cone of higher \ and U values stretching from southeast to northwest across the field of view. The ENLR features low reddening, with a median of A_V = 0.4 mag increasing to only 1 mag in the nucleus, despite uniformly high measured oxygen abundances of 1.7 - 2.1 Solar. The presence of coronal lines in the nucleus together with the low reddening and high measured abundances suggest that dust destruction is occurring in this object.

The lack of a significant metallicity gradient over 3 kpc radially in the ENLR and the similar homogeneity of the measured pressures may indicate that radiation pressure-driven winds from the powerful AGN have mixed the ISM outwards.

sources.summary: The paper fits Mrk 573 with XMM-Newton and NuSTAR spectra, finds no variability, and reports Compton-thick line-of-sight absorption with two soft X-ray thermal components.

quotes.quote: Mrk 573: We fitted the \ EPIC-pn and the \ FPMA/FPMB spectra of this source using the four models that are described in Section sec:spectralfit . The source does not show any variability, thus we kept all the parameters tied for both observations. All models result in a CT column density in the LOS, in agreement with the results by . We note that MYTC does not provide a good fit using the standard configuration, i.e., by fixing the weight A to unity. Instead, it requires a large value of A=69_ 60 ^ +20 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses Mrk 573 through nuclear emission-line spectra, overlap with high-excitation Seyfert samples, STIS/APO photoionization modeling, and inferred densities and temperatures.

quotes.quote: Although a larger aperture yields higher S/N, it also results in greater contamination from the stellar continuum, which has a variety of complex absorption features in the vicinity of the \ lines, as illustrated by the spectra of Mrk 573 in Fig. fig:fig1 .

Observations of the \ doublet was reported by Re18a in only one object, Mrk 573. In their study, the authors combined with varying weights three different photoionization models corresponding to a high, intermediate and low U values at each location along the STIS slit observations as well as of the DIS long-slit from the Apache Point Observatory (APO).

Using , the densities we infer at the corresponding positions from the \ doublet ratios of Re18 are 22 600 and 4220 , which are values significantly lower. The corresponding temperatures inferred from the \ ratios are 16 600 and 13 800 , respectively To deblend the \ doublet ratio of Mrk 573, we used the \ ( 3889 / 4740 ) ratio since neither the \ 5876 \ nor the 4471 \ line were reported. .

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 573 as a Seyfert with an extended emission-line region, a strong-lined nuclear near-infrared spectrum, and evidence confirming its obscured narrow-line Seyfert 1 classification.

quotes.quote: Mrk 573 This galaxy is known to have an extended emission-line region Unger87,Haniff88,Tsvetanov92 .
The nuclear near-infrared spectrum of Mrk 573 presents very strong emission lines together with prominent absorption features in the H and K bands.

Mrk 573 has been classified by many authors as a Seyfert 2 galaxy, based on optical Tsvetanov92,Erkens97,Mullaney08 and infrared spectroscopy Veilleux97,Riffel06 . However, based on recent near-infrared spectroscopy in the ZJ range of Mrk 573, together with the detection of X-ray variability in the X-rays, Ramos08 have recently reclassified this object as an obscured NLSy1.

sources.summary: The atlas treats Mrk 573 as an individually observed active or starburst galaxy, summarizing its prior classification and near-infrared continuum, emission-line, and absorption properties.

quotes.quote: Mrk 573 . The CfA Seyfert 2 galaxy Mrk 573 hb92 is a well-studied AGN with two ionization cones seen in [ O iii ] maps pr95,fws98 . It is also known for the bright high-ionization emission lines displayed in its optical nuclear spectrum dur94 . Spectropolarimetric observations by nag04 show prominent scattered broad H emission and various narrow forbidden emission lines, the degree of polarization of the latter ones correlated with the ionization potential of the corresponding line. They interpret this correlation in terms of obscuration of the stratified NLR by the optically and geometrically thick dusty torus.

sources.summary: The atlas gives Mrk 573's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: Mrk 573 is a spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0172 ( D 67.4 Mpc) with a Sy 2 nucleus osterbrock_spectroscopic_1993 with broad emission lines in polarized light nagao_detection_2004 . It features an extended biconical NLR coinciding with extended radio emission (diameter 8.9 2.8 kpc; PA 124 ; e.g., ulvestad_radio_1984-1,pogge_imaging_1995,schmitt_comparison_1996 ). The AGN has recently also been classified as an obscured narrow-line Seyfert 1 (Sy 1n) by .

gmw_00000143.name: Mrk 590
gmw_00000143.overview: Mrk 590 is a spiral galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770] featuring an exponential bulge [arXiv:astro-ph/0407383] and a changing-look Seyfert nucleus [arXiv:2208.04067, arXiv:1603.00035]. The galaxy hosts a parsec-scale radio jet [arXiv:2101.04629] and a 1-kiloparsec gas ring [arXiv:1509.09203] that surrounds a nuclear gas spiral [arXiv:1903.08696].

sources.summary: The paper studies Mrk 590 as a changing-look AGN whose broad optical lines reappeared, with a star-forming ring, nuclear gas spiral, and outflow shaping gas inflow and feedback.

quotes.quote: In this work we study for the first time the host galaxy of a changing-look AGN, Mrk 590, using high spatial resolution optical and near-infrared observations. We discover that after 10 yr absence, the optical broad emission lines of Mrk 590 have reappeared. The AGN optical continuum flux however, is still 10 times lower than that observed during the most luminous state in the 1990s.

The host galaxy shows a 4.5 kpc radius star-forming ring with knots of ionised and cold molecular gas emission. Extended ionised and warm molecular gas emission are detected in the nucleus, indicating that there is a reservoir of gas as close as 60 pc from the black hole. We observe a nuclear gas spiral between radii 0.5 - 2 kpc, which has been suggested as a dynamical mechanism able to drive the necessary gas to fuel AGN. We also discover blue-shifted and high velocity dispersion [ O III ] emission out to a radius of 1 kpc, tracing a nuclear gas outflow. The gas dynamics in Mrk 590 suggest a complex balance between gas inflow and outflow in the nucleus of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper excludes Mrk 590 from its statistics because its changing-look behavior and luminosity decline make the dust-continuum and BLR measurements physically complicated.

quotes.quote: In the end, we are convinced that only Mrk 590 is not suitable to be included in our analysis. Mrk 590 transformed from a classical type 1 AGN to type 1.9--2, as its continuum luminosity decreased by 100 over the past four decades Denney2014 . The lack of evidence of intrinsic absorption indicates that changes in continuum and emission lines are due to the decline of the BH accretion rate instead of obscuration along the line of sight. The BLR of Mrk 590 was monitored through the \ line from 1990--1996 Peterson1998 and recently, with \ in 2018 Mandal2021b . The BLR size of Mrk 590, around 25 ld, does not change significantly at the bright and faint states.

The dust continuum RM campaign was conducted from 2003 to 2007 Koshida2014 during the rapid decline of the AGN luminosity in Mrk 590. found that the dust continuum size of Mrk 590 is quite small, only 33 ld, reflecting the rapid replenishment of the dust in the innermost region of the dusty interstellar medium. We exclude Mrk 590 in our statistics to avoid the complicated physics of this target.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA observations of Mrk 590, finding little central molecular gas, possible temporary AGN starvation, and a kiloparsec ring with disturbed gas that may refuel the nucleus.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The phase tracking centre was set to _ J2000 = 02^ h 14^ m 33.579120^ s , _ J2000 =-00^ 46'00 27840 .

quotes.quote: We investigate if the active galactic nucleus (AGN) of Mrk 590, whose supermassive black hole was until recently highly accreting, is turning off due to a lack of central gas to fuel it. We analyse new sub-arcsecond resolution ALMA maps of the ^ 12 CO(3--2) line and 344 GHz continuum emission in Mrk 590. We detect no ^ 12 CO(3--2) emission in the inner 150 pc, constraining the central molecular gas mass to M( H_2 ) 1.6 10^5 M_ , no more than a typical giant molecular gas cloud, for a CO luminosity to gas mass conversion factor of _ CO 0.8 M_ (K km s^ 1 pc^ 2 )^ 1 .

However, there is still potentially enough gas to fuel the black hole for another 2.6 10^5 years assuming Eddington-limited accretion. We therefore cannot rule out that the AGN may just be experiencing a temporary feeding break, and may turn on again in the near future. We discover a ring-like structure at a radius of 1 kpc, where a gas clump exhibiting disturbed kinematics and located just 200 pc west of the AGN, may be refueling the centre. Mrk 590 does not have significantly less gas than other nearby AGN host galaxies at kpc scales, confirming that gas reservoirs at these scales provide no direct indication of on-going AGN activity and accretion rates.

sources.summary: The paper studies Mrk 590 as a changing-look Seyfert AGN, finding a compact synchrotron radio core, fading radio flux, and variability driven by changing accretion rather than dust obscuration.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We investigate the origin of the parsec-scale radio emission from the changing-look active galactic nucleus (AGN) of Mrk 590, and examine whether the radio power has faded concurrently with the dramatic decrease in accretion rates observed between the 1990s and the present. We detect a compact core at 1.6 GHz and 8.4 GHz using new Very Long Baseline Array observations, finding no significant extended, jet-like features down to 1 pc scales. The flat spectral index ( _ 1.6 ^ 8.4 = 0.03 ) and high brightness temperature ( T_ b 10^ 8 K ) indicate self-absorbed synchrotron emission from the AGN. The radio to X-ray luminosity ratio of log (L_ R /L_ X ) 5 , similar to that in coronally active stars, suggests emission from magnetized coronal winds, although unresolved radio jets are also consistent with the data.

Comparing new Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array measurements with archival and published radio flux densities, we find 46 , 34 , and (insignificantly) 13 flux density decreases between the 1990s and the year 2015 at 1.4 GHz, 5 GHz and 8.4 GHz respectively. This trend, possibly due to the expansion and fading of internal shocks within the radio-emitting outflow after a recent outburst, is consistent with the decline of the optical-UV and X-ray luminosities over the same period. Such correlated variability demonstrates the AGN accretion-outflow connection, confirming that the changing-look behaviour in Mrk 590 originates from variable accretion rates rather than dust obscuration.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 590 as a Seyfert 1 with soft excess and narrow Fe-line emission; its disc reflection component requires very high density and high inclination.

quotes.quote: Mrk 590 Mrk 590 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy veron06 . Previous analysis of the quasi-simultaneous \ and \ observations in 2004 shows evidence for a strong soft excess and narrow Fe K , Fe xxv and Fe xxvi emissions longinotti07 . We first fit the 3--10 keV band spectrum with an absorbed power-law model and the ratio plot is shown in Fig. pic_fe . By fitting the narrow lines features with simple zgauss models, Two narrow line models are required, one line at 6.407 0.02 keV (EW= 135^ +12 _ 23 eV, <0.06 keV) and the other line at 7.04^ +0.06 _ 0.10 keV (EW= 46^ +23 _ 35 , <0.12 keV). The former line can be interpreted as Fe K emission line and the latter can be interpreted as Fe xxvi line.

The disc reflection component with a very high disc density of (n_ e )=18.4^ +0.2 _ 1.1 accounts for the soft excess, as shown in Fig. pic_final2 . We obtain a lower limit of the BH spin of a_*>0.1 and a high inclination angle of i=79^ +7 _ 4 ^ .

sources.summary: The paper studies Mrk 590 as a changing-look Seyfert galaxy, reporting long-term continuum fading and an EVN-detected parsec-scale radio jet supporting variable accretion-driven type changes.

quotes.quote: As a nearby galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.0264 , Mrk 590 suffered such a cycle of Seyfert type changes between 2006 and 2017. Over the last fifty years, Mrk 590 also underwent a powerful continuum outburst and a slow fading from X-rays to radio wavelengths with a peak bolometric luminosity reaching about ten per cent of the Eddington luminosity. To track its past accretion and ejection activity, we performed very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) observations with the European VLBI Network (EVN) at 1.6 GHz in 2015.

The EVN observations reveal a faint ( 1.7 mJy) radio jet extending up to 2.8 mas (projected scale 1.4 pc) toward north, and probably resulting from the very intensive AGN activity. To date, such a parsec-scale jet is rarely seen in the known changing-look AGN. The finding of the faint jet provides further strong support for variable accretion as the origin of the type changes in Mrk 590.

sources.summary: The paper studies Mrk 590 as a nearby X-ray bright changing-look AGN, finding its soft X-ray excess vanished after 2004 amid strong optical-UV and power-law variability.

quotes.quote: Ritesh Ghosh ritesh.ghosh1987@gmail.com, ritesh.ghosh@iucaa We have studied the nature and origin of the soft X-ray excess detected in the interesting changing-look AGN (CLAGN) Mrk 590 using two decades of multi-wavelength observations from , , and . In the light of vanishing soft excess in this CLAGN, we test two models, ``the warm Comptonization'' and ``the ionized disk reflection'' using extensive UV/X-ray spectral analysis. Our main findings are: (1) the soft X-ray excess emission, last observed in 2004, vanished in 2011, and never reappeared in any of the later observations, (2) we detected a significant variability ( 300 ) in the observed optical-UV and power-law flux between observations with the lowest state ( = 4.4 10^ 43 , in 2016) and the highest state ( = 1.2 10^ 44 , in 2018), (3) the UV and power-law fluxes follow same temporal pattern, (4) the photon index showed a significant variation ( =1.88^ +0.02 _ 0.08 and =1.58^ +0.02 _ 0.03 in 2002 and 2021 respectively) between observations, (5) no Compton hump was detected in the source spectra but a narrow Fe K_ line is present in all observations, (6) we detected a high-energy cut-off in power-law continuum ( 92^ +55 _ 25 and 60^ +10 _ 08 ) with the latest observations, (7) the warm Comptonization model needs an additional diskbb component to describe the source UV bump, (8) there is no correlation between the Eddington rate and the soft excess as found in other changing-look AGNs.

Mrk 590 (also known as NGC 863) is a nearby (z = 0.0264), X-ray bright CLAGN, which has shown similar dramatic changes in amplitude of broad Balmer emission lines 1993ApJ...414..552O, 2014ApJ...796..134D, 2018ApJ...866..123M,2019MNRAS.486..123R . The source has changed from type 1.5 1977ApJ...215..733O to type 1 1998ApJ...501...82P and then to type 1.9-2 2014ApJ...796..134D . Mrk 590 also showed significant variability in luminosity at optical wavelength as the central AGN brightened by a factor of 10 between the 1970s and 1990s, then faded by a factor of 100 between the 1990s and 2013. suggested the change in source luminosity due to drop in black hole accretion rate.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 590 as a low-luminosity reverberation-mapped AGN whose near-IR continuum is dominated by host-galaxy emission but still follows the near-IR black-hole scaling relations.

quotes.quote: Four AGN in our sample, namely, Mrk 590, NGC 3227, NGC 3516 and NGC 4051, were found to be in a very low luminosity state with a continuum SED dominated by host galaxy emission (see Fig. SEDgal , top panels, and L11a , their Fig. 6). Therefore, in these cases the observed 1 m continuum luminosity represents only an upper limit to the AGN value. As Fig. lag shows, these sources (shown as green filled circles) populate indeed the low end of the near-IR luminosity distribution of our sample. However, they follow not only the near-IR R - L relationship remarkably well, but also the so-called black hole mass-luminosity ( M L ) relationship Kaspi00, Pet04 , which we plot in Fig. masslum for the near-IR. Therefore, the AGN 1 m continuum luminosity of these sources cannot be significantly (more than a factor of 2-3 ) lower than that of the host galaxy.

sources.summary: Mrk 590 has an unusually large inferred relativistic-line equivalent width in a low-count spectrum, but overall broad-line constraints remain weak and signal-to-noise limited.

quotes.quote: Nevertheless, we do detect a relativistic Fe line in spectrum with fewer counts ( 3 10 ^3 ). In Mrk 590, the inferred equivalent width of the line ( 450 eV) is much larger than is typical, accounting for the detection.

Finally, there are two objects in our sample where the evidence for any relativistic Fe K emission line is very weak, those being NGC 5548 and Mrk 590. We note that no broad Fe K line was detected also in previous observations of these objects (NGC 5548: Brenneman+12 , Mrk 590: Longinotti+07 ). In both of these sources, however, the constraints on any broad component in the spectrum are poor, with upper limits typically several 100 eV, usually in excess of the typical EW for the detected broad lines of 50-100 eV.

sources.summary: The paper follows Mrk 590 through optical/NIR fading, finding a rapid shrinkage of its dust torus inner radius after the AGN luminosity drop.

quotes.quote: We examined long-term optical/near-infrared (NIR) flux variability of a ``changing-look'' active galactic nucleus (AGN) Mrk 590 between 1998 and 2007. Multi-band multi-epoch optical/NIR photometry data from the SDSS Stripe 82 database and the Multicolor Active Galactic Nuclei Monitoring (MAGNUM) project reveal that Mrk 590 experienced a sudden luminosity decrease during the period from 2000 to 2001. Detection of dust reverberation lag signals between V and K band light curves obtained by the MAGNUM project during the faint state in 2003-2007 suggests that the dust torus innermost radius R_ dust of Mrk 590 had become very small [ R_ dust 32   light-days (lt-days) ] by the year 2004 according to the aforementioned significant decrease in AGN luminosity. The R_ dust in the faint state is comparable to the H broad line region (BLR) radius of R_ H , BLR 26  lt-days measured by previous reverberation mapping observations during the bright state of Mrk 590 in 1990-1996 . These observations indicate that the innermost radius of the dust torus in Mrk 590 decreased rapidly after the AGN ultraviolet-optical luminosity drop, and that the replenishment time scale of the innermost dust distribution is less than 4 years, which is much shorter than the free fall time scale of BLR gas or dust clouds.

sources.summary: Photometric reverberation mapping of changing-look AGN Mrk 590 finds strong variability, a 21.44-day H-line lag, a 0.018 pc BLR, and a black hole mass near 2e8 solar masses.

quotes.quote: We present the results of photometric reverberation mapping observations on the changing look active galactic nucleus Mrk 590 at z = 0.026. The observations were carried out from July to December, 2018 using broad band B, R and narrow band H and S II filters.

All the light curves showed strong variation with a fractional root-mean-square variation of 0.132 0.001 in B band and 0.321 0.001 in H line. From cross-correlation function analysis, we obtained a delayed response of H line emission to the optical B-band continuum emission of 21.44^ +1.49 _ 2.11 days in the rest-frame of the source, corresponding to a linear size of the BLR of 0.018 pc.

By combining the BLR size with the H line full width at half maximum of 6478 240 km s ^ 1 measured from a single-epoch spectrum obtained with the Subaru telescope, we derived a black hole mass of 1.96^ +0.15 _ 0.21 10^8 M_ .

sources.summary: The CARS paper discusses Mrk 590 as a changing-look AGN observed with MUSE and used, with Mrk 1018, to test ENLR size scaling.

quotes.quote: HE 0212 0059 (Mrk 590) is a well-known changing-look AGN and was targeted as part of program 099.B-0249(A) (PI: Raimundo) for 8 1100 s with interleaved dedicated sky frame exposures due to the large size of the galaxy Raimundo:2019 . Luckily, the CARS sample contains 2 well-known changing-look AGN, namely HE 0212 0059 aka Mrk 590 Osterbrock:1977,Denney:2014 and HE 0203 0019 aka Mrk 1018 Cohen:1986,McElroy:2016,Krumpe:2017 , which we use here to test the impact on the ENLR size scaling.

We include both changing-look AGN as stripes in Fig. fig:ENLR_size_overview representing the range in AGN luminosity from the observed maximum to 1/100 and 1/10 of the luminosity as observed for Mrk 590 Denney:2014 and Mrk 1018 McElroy:2016,Krumpe:2017 , respectively, during their minimum state. As shown in Fig. fig:ENLR_MBH_relation , both changing-look AGN Mrk 1018 and Mrk 590 are in agreement with the ENLR size--BH mass relation. For Mrk 590 we use the broad H parameters as measured from the MUSE IFU data during a recent rebrightening phase, and for Mrk 1018 we used the BH mass estimated during its bright phase Bennert:2015 as the broad H line remained too faint after the last changing-look event.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 863 as a broad-line Seyfert 1 with molecular hydrogen emission but mostly unresolved or upper-limit forbidden-line kinematics.

quotes.quote: Molecular H _ 2 emission is also observed in these two objects as well as in the broad-line Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 863.

No correlation is observed for NGC 863. This fact can be explained if we recall that most NLR lines in that object were unresolved and/or detected only as upper limits. Except for [ S 8 ] 0.991 m (IP=280 eV), the lines of NGC 863 plotted in Figure ipvsfwhm have FWHM equal to the instrumental value.
For the remaining three galaxies (NGC 863, MRK 1044 and TON S 180), nothing can be said about the kinematics of the CLR due to the lack of sufficient information.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 590 as one of three anomalous AGN with unusually high [CII]/PAH ratios, reliable [CII] detections, and weak PAH emission not explained by measurement error.

quotes.quote: Among all of the sources in Figures 5 and 6, only three appear anomalous. These are all AGN and have the largest ratios f( [ CII ] )/f(PAH) - sources 3C 120, Mrk 590, and NGC 3393 in Table 1 - with f( [ CII ] )/f(PAH) about a factor of ten greater than the median. All have strong and reliable [ CII ] detections with no unusual profiles or evidence of spatial mismatches or source extension. The PAH 11.3 feature is weak in all three, but independent CASSIS spectra yield fluxes consistent to within 30 , so there is no indication that the anomalous weakness of PAH is a measurement error.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 590 as a well-studied variable AGN that transitioned from type 1 to type 2, then later showed UV and broad Balmer rebrightening.

quotes.quote: Mrk 590 Mrk 590 (BAT 116) is a well-known variable AGN and has been extensively studied in multiple wavebands. Between 1996 and 2006, the source transitioned from type 1 to type 2, as reported by 2014ApJ...796..134D , who also showed that broad Balmer emission was not present in 2013 February, 2013 December or 2014 January. 2018ApJ...866..123M reported a subsequent brightening in the ultraviolet continuum and the presence of broad Mg II 2800 emission in Hubble Space Telescope observations obtained in 2014 November. A BASS DR2 observation with Xshooter in 2017 December clearly show the re-appearance of broad \ and broad \ emission, in agreement with the MUSE observations of 2019MNRAS.486..123R who found broad Balmer emission in 2017 October and November.

sources.summary: BETIS identifies NGC863 as a Seyfert 2 showcase galaxy with high DIG surface brightness, AGN-like DIG line ratios, and fast-shock-consistent ionisation.

quotes.quote: The subsample includes 3 known AGNs: NGC863 and NGC3393 are Seyfert 2 1977ARA&A..15...69W, 1988SoSAO..55....5L , and NGC692 which is a Low-Luminosity AGN (LLAGN; 2019ApJ...872..134Z ). Furthermore, NGC863 also exhibits higher in the DIG than in the regions, notably towards greater galactocentric radii. However, when performing this diagnosis for individual galaxies, only NGC863 and NGC3393, both Seyfert-2 AGNs, exhibit line ratios characteristic of AGN emission.
The line ratios observed in NGC863 are consisted with fast-shock without precursor with v _s between 200 and 500 km/s and B between 0.0001 and 5.

sources.summary: The paper finds Mrk 590 is better fitted with a broad Gaussian iron feature whose velocity width suggests emission from the outer accretion disk rather than strongly relativistic regions.

quotes.quote: Four objects in the sample (UGC 3973 (second epoch), Mrk 590, MCG--02-14-009 (see also Porquet 2005), II Zw 177) were fitted better if a broad Gaussian profile was considered rather than an intrinsically narrow profile (see Table tab:adds ).

We note that in three objects: MCG--02-14-009, UGC 3973, and Mrk 590 (e.g. Figure fig:mrkln ), the full-width at half-maximum of the Gaussian profiles indicated velocities on the order of 10000 . For all three of these AGN, the widths of the iron lines are only about a factor of 2-4 greater then the velocities measured in the H emission line from the broad line region. This suggests that, at least in these cases, the iron emission could be originating from the outer regions of the accretion disc, where Doppler effects dominate relativistic effects.

sources.summary: The paper lists Mrk 590 among observations without broad iron-line evidence, similar to small-reflection-strength cases, while having a moderate disc inclination.

quotes.quote: The three narrow observations that do not show evidence for a broad iron line component with the ionised and/or blurred iron line models are: Mrk 590, IC 4329A(1) and NGC 5548(1).
Mrk 590 and IC 4329A(1) are similar to Mrk 6 and NGC 5506(1) in that they are best-fit with a small reflection strength and an emissivity index close to q=3 .
However, while Mrk 590 has a moderate disc inclination, our view of the disc in IC 4329A(1) is edge-on in Model SRI.

sources.summary: The atlas gives Mrk 590's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: Mrk 590 is a spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0264 ( D 107 Mpc) with a Sy 1.0 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 and an extended NLR (diameter 1.5 750 pc; PA 5 ; schmitt_hubble_2003 ) and an unresolved radio core schmitt_jet_2001 . It belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. Since , the source was observed in the MIR with the MMT maiolino_new_1995 , with /ISOCAM ramos_almeida_mid-infrared_2007 and /IRS (e.g., dasyra_high-ionization_2008,weaver_mid-infrared_2010 ) and MIPS.

sources.summary: The paper cites Mrk 590 as a changing-look AGN whose narrow-line gas responded to a decreased ionizing continuum after the central engine dimmed.

quotes.quote: Within the past 40 years, such changes have been detected in only a handful of AGN, including Mrk 590 denney , NGC 2617 shappee , NGC 7603 tohline , Mrk 1018 cohen , NGC 1097 storchi-bergmann , NGC 3065 ngc3065 and NGC 7582 aretxaga .

Narrow emission lines have been observed to vary in response to changes in the AGN ionizing continuum in other AGN, namely Mrk 590 denney and NGC 5548 peterson , though such changes occurred years after dimming of the central engine.

Indeed, the gas in the NLRs of Mrk 590 and NGC 5548 has responded to the decrease in the AGN ionizing continuum denney,peterson , indicating that variations in the NLR are sometimes observeable in a single object.

sources.summary: Mrk 590 has Fe K emission well inside the dust and smaller comparison scale, while its BLR clouds lie close to the torus inner edge.

quotes.quote: The remaining 4 sources (NGC 3516, NGC 7469, Mrk 590 and Fairall 9) have \ values significantly smaller than .
On the other hand, for Mrk 590, \ is significantly smaller than even .
Finally, for Mrk 509 and Mrk 590, \ lies within a factor of 1.5 of , implying a close proximity of the BLR clouds with the inner extent of the torus.

sources.summary: The paper discusses Mrk 590 as a well-studied changing-look Seyfert that shifted from type 1.5 to type 1 and then to type 1.9-2 as broad lines disappeared.

quotes.quote: Another well-studied example is Mrk 590 which has been observed over a 40-year time scale. First observed as a type-1.5, Mrk 590 transitioned to a type-1 before the broad lines disappeared, making it a type 1.9-2 Seyfert den14 .
den14 is the most notable example reporting Mrk 590 to transition from type-1.5 to type-1, then transition again to type 1.9-2.

sources.summary: The paper presents Mrk 590 as a changing-look AGN with strong X-ray variation and spectral correlations that separate narrow-line and broad-line states around 10^-3 Eddington ratio.

quotes.quote: The archived data for both NuSTAR, XMM-Newton and Chandra are selected and reduced for four famous CL AGNs -- Mrk 1018, NGC 3516, Mrk 590 and NGC 2992, where these sources suffer strong X-ray variations in last 20 years.

For Mrk 590, 8 data points roughly follow a positive L_ 2-10 keV /L_ Edd correlation, where two points with L_ 2-10 keV /L_ Edd 10^ 3 follow a roughly negative correlation. It should be noted that NEL and BEL CL AGNs also have the Eddington-scaled X-ray luminosity lower and higher than 10^ 3 respectively.

sources.summary: The paper groups Mrk 590 with Sy1 sources lacking absorption variability but having large covering factors and intermediate-to-high opening angles, preferring smooth gas with clumpy dust in a thick torus.

quotes.quote: Panel 3 of Figure fig:cartoon_Sy1 (e.g. Mrk 1383 and Mrk 590): Sy1 sources without absorption variability, large covering factors, intermediate-to-high half opening angles for both gas and dust. The gas is distributed smoothly while the dust is distributed into clumps. These two objects could be explained with a single geometrically-thick torus where dust is located in clumps while the gas is smoothly distributed

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 590 among Seyfert hosts with an exponential bulge, noting that its bulge profile is better fit by an exponential than an r1/4 law.

quotes.quote: Note the exponential bulges in Mrk 590 and Mrk 817.
We note in the descriptions of the individual galaxies below (section sec:galnotes ) that the bulge profiles of Mrk 590 and Mrk 817 are better fit by exponentials than ^ 1/4 laws.
Mrk 590 -- The bulge of this galaxy is better fit by a exponential than an ^ 1/4 law.

gmw_00000144.name: Mrk 841
gmw_00000144.overview: Mrk 841 is a nearby Seyfert 1 active galaxy [arXiv:1011.3224, arXiv:2308.08528] classified as an early-type host containing a radio-quiet quasar-regime nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770]. The source exhibits large spectral variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0204095, arXiv:2308.08528], characterized by a strong, variable soft X-ray excess [arXiv:astro-ph/0611665, arXiv:1301.4997] and historically changing H-beta emission line asymmetries [arXiv:2011.05902].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the IBIS hard-X-ray source with Seyfert galaxy Mkn 841, measures its optical and X-ray behavior, and models a Seyfert-like spectrum with cutoff and reflection.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Mkn 841 (optical catalogue coordinates J2000: RA = 15:04:01.20, Dec = +10:26:16.2)

quotes.quote: However, it is located at only 1.9 arcmin from Mkn 841 (optical catalogue coordinates J2000: RA = 15:04:01.20, Dec = +10:26:16.2), and therefore consistent with it (see Fig. 1). The error circle of the IBIS detection of January 2006 is also reported in Fig. 1: as already noted, this hard X-ray source is also consistent only with the Seyfert galaxy. From the count rates measured in our IBIS observation we derive a flux of (2.4 0.3) 10^ 11 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 in the range 15-60 keV for Mkn 841, assuming the spectrum measured by BeppoSAX ( = 2 , Petrucci et al. 2002).

In Figure 4 we report the spectral energy distribution of Mkn 841 based on the INTEGRAL , Swift , and NOT quasi-simultaneous (within few days) observations of August 2008, and compare it with the historical data and with the Swift XRT and UVOT observations of February 2007. The Swift BAT average flux and spectrum from the 39-month survey (Cusumano et al. 2010) are also shown. The spectrum has the overall typical shape of a Seyfert 1 nucleus, with a prominent rising optical/UV component, a relatively soft spectrum in the 0.3-10 keV range and a hard X-ray component with a cutoff around 150 keV, that suggests a relatively high coronal maximum temperature. The NOT, UVOT and XRT measurements of August 2008 indicate a relatively high state.

sources.summary: PG1501+106 shows a slightly inverted VLBA spectrum, a Gaia-consistent coordinate, extended emission, and variability; its VLA compact core supports low-Eddington radio emission dominated by an optically thick core.

quotes.quote: PG1501+106 has a slightly inverted total slope _ total = +0.1 and a more inverted core slope _ core = +0.6 . The VLBA coordinate is consistent with the Gaia position ( 3.6 mas), though the T_ B is not too high ( 10^ 6.6 K). There is some extended emission, as the VLBA core/total flux ratio < 0.5 . The VLBA/VLA flux ratio > 1 again manifests that the source is variable.

This object was observed with the VLA A configuration at 4.8 and 8.4 GHz Kellermann1989,Kukula1995 . It shows a compact core and an inverted VLA slope ( _ 4.8-8.4 = +0.4 ), which indicates that the emission detected in our VLBA observation is still dominant on the VLA scales. This is consistent with the picture that the radio emission in low L/L_ Edd objects is dominated by the compact optically thick core.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes XMM-Newton observations of the bright Seyfert 1 galaxy Mkn 841, finding a strong soft excess, complex variable iron-line components, and spectra fitted with blurred photoionized reflection.

quotes.quote: Mkn 841 has been observed during 3 different periods (January 2001, January 2005 and July 2005) by XMM-Newton for a total cumulated exposure time of 108 ks. We present in this paper a broad band spectral analysis of the complete EPIC-pn data sets. These observations confirm the presence of the strong soft excess and complex iron line profile known to be present in this source since a long time. They also reveal their extreme and puzzling spectral and temporal behaviors.

The broad-band 0.5-10 keV spectra are well described by a model including a primary power law continuum, a blurred photoionized reflection and a narrow iron line, the blurred reflection fitting self-consistently the soft excess and the broad line component. The origin and nature of the narrow component is unclear.
Mkn 841 is a bright Seyfert 1 galaxy (z=0.0364, Falco et al. 2000), one of the rare Seyfert 1 detected by OSSE at more than 3 (Johnson 1997).

sources.summary: The paper reports Mkn 841 as a bright Seyfert 1 with a rapidly variable narrow neutral iron K line, nearly constant continuum, and reflection behavior difficult for standard cold reflection models.

quotes.quote: Mkn 841 is a bright Seyfert 1 galaxy (z=0.0365), one of the rare Seyfert 1 detected by OSSE at more than 3 (Johnson ). It is known for its large spectral variability (George et al. ; Nandra et al. ), its strong soft excess (this was the first object where a soft excess was observed, Arnaud et al. ) and its variable iron line (at least on a year time scale, George et al. ). The latter was observed in some cases with a relatively large equivalent width (hereafter EW) of about 400 eV (Day et al. ; Bianchi et al. ) significantly above the value predicted by standard cold reflection model (e.g. George Fabian ).

The neutral narrow iron line of Mkn 841 appears to be highly variable on a timescale of 10 hours or less. This cannot be easily explained in the standard cold reflection model framework. None of the explanations proposed here appear completely conclusive.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 841 as a Seyfert test case for soft X-ray excess variability, finding year-scale changes best explained by a thermal Comptonization component.

quotes.quote: As an example, we present results from this technique applied to the Seyfert galaxy Mrk 841. We study all (3) XMM and some of the Suzaku pointings available and find that the source displays an impressive variability in the soft X-ray band on the timescale of years. We study several common soft excess models and their ability to physically consistently explain this spectral variability. Mrk 841 is found to show a distinct variability pattern that can be best explained by the soft excess originating mostly from a thermal Comptonization component. The variability timescale can be constrained to be on the order of a few days.

sources.summary: The paper models Mrk 841 near the predicted Ly location and shows that a broad added absorption feature would strongly disagree with the observed COS spectrum.

quotes.quote: Unfortunately, the predicted location of Ly in this spectrum falls right on the red wing of the damped Ly absorption due to foreground neutral hydrogen in the Milky Way. Nevertheless, we have modeled its profile, the surrounding AGN continuum, and the blue wing of the broad Ly emission line. This model is shown overlayed on the high-quality COS spectrum in Figure fig:1n . The blue curve in the same panel shows the appearance of the model if a 1 \ equivalent width absorption line with FWHM=1000 km s^ 1 is added. It is clear that this deviates significantly from the data.

sources.summary: The paper monitors Mrk 841 as a Seyfert with historically changing H-beta asymmetry and reports its reverberation lag and first black-hole mass estimate from the campaign.

quotes.quote: We report the results of reverberation mapping three bright Seyfert galaxies, Mrk 79, NGC 3227, and Mrk 841, from a campaign conducted from December 2016 to May 2017 with the Wyoming Infrared Observatory (WIRO) 2.3-meter telescope. All three of these targets have shown asymmetric broad H emission lines in the past, although their emission lines were relatively symmetric during our observations. We measured \ time lags for all three targets and estimated masses of their black holes -- for the first time in the case of Mrk 841.

In the early 1980s, Mrk 841 (also known as PG 1501+106) had a red asymmetric \ line (showing a significant tail of emission extending on the red side of the profile compared to the blue side), while Mrk 79 and NGC 3227 had rather symmetric profiles then DeRobertis1985 .
In 2011, Mrk 841's \ line showed a blue asymmetry barth2015 , very different from its red asymmetry in the 1980s and 1990s.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 841 as a local active galaxy in its bare-AGN sample and analyzes XMM-Newton/Swift X-ray spectra, including continuum, soft-excess, and spectral-fitting behavior.

quotes.quote: Mrk 841 is a nearby (z=0.00364) Seyfert 1 AGN which is known for its large spectral variability George1993 . This is the first object where a soft-excess Arnaud1985 was observed along Mrk 509. A variable Fe line was reported by George1993 . From the UV/X-ray observations, Ross1992 found that this source is a face-on source with a central black hole of mass 2 10^7 M _ . Recently, reported a substantial decrement in the soft-excess band compared to its harder counterpart over the last 15 years.

sources.summary: For Mrk 841, the paper finds consistent H lag distributions from three methods and similar extracted H light curves when compared with lagged continuum and SRM H data.

quotes.quote: For Mrk 841 in List I, we first calculate the time lag distributions with the ICCF-Cut, JAVELIN and ^2 methods for the whole observational duration. The obtained H lag distributions from three methods are consistent. The extracted H lightcurve is similar to the lagged continuum g band lightcurve, as shown in Figure 4 panel (b). Then we calculate the time lag distribution for part of the lightcurves which have simultaneous SRM H observations 2022ApJS..262...14B . By comparing with the SRM H lightcurve, we can find that the shape of the extracted H lightcurve is very similar to the SRM H lightcurve, and the ICCF result between the extracted H and SRM H lightcurves is close to zero (see panels () and (d) in Figure 5).

sources.summary: The paper models Mrk 841 with reflection plus a distant reflector and ionized absorber, finding a prograde spin solution consistent with prior inner-radius results.

quotes.quote: Mrk 841: In addition to the PLC+RDC components, we also detect a narrow emission feature consistent with neutral iron and therefore include a contribution from a second, distant reflector. We also include a moderately ionised absorber at the redshift of the galaxy similar to the most prominent absorption component detected by in their analysis of the high resolution \ RGS spectrum of this source, with = 2.2 0.2 \ and \ = 2.1^ +0.9 _ 0.8 10^ 21 . Although we present the prograde spin solution in Tables tab_c3_refl and tab_refl2 , there is also a local minimum with retrograde spin ( a^* 0.4 ) also just about satisfies ^ 2 < 2.71 (see Fig. fig_c3_spin ). The prograde solution is consistent with the inner radii obtained by in their analysis of the available \ data.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 841 as a case where reflection is not required, showing an absorbed power-law fit and suggesting variability may explain disagreement with previous work.

quotes.quote: Four sources, namely Mrk 382, IRAS 13224-3809, Mrk 841, and MR 2251-178 show a F-test probability of 0.09, 0.07, 0.05, and 0.78, respectively. As an example of objects where the reflection component is not needed, we show in Figure fig:Mrk841_non_reflection the absorbed power-law fit for Mrk 841. Note that they use several observations, 12 of XMM Newton and seven of NuSTAR , among which are the two observations used in this work. use simultaneous observations of XMM Newton and BeppoSAX to study the spectrum of Mrk 841. A plausible explanation for this discrepancy between our results and those found previously for IRAS 13224-3809 and Mrk 841 might be due to the variability of these AGN; i.e. these systems might be in a state of a very high continuum level.

sources.summary: BeppoSAX observations confirm Mrk 841's soft excess, with a warm absorber nearly matching the true-excess fit, and find a viable Comptonization continuum plus unusually strong reflection with normal iron-line strength.

quotes.quote: We present and discuss BeppoSAX observations of Mrk 841 and Mrk 335, two Seyfert 1 galaxies in which previous observations have established the presence of soft excesses. We confirm the soft excess in both sources, even if for Mrk 841 a warm absorber provides a fit almost as good as the one with a true excess. As far as the hard X--ray continuum is concerned, a Comptonization model provides a fit as good as a power law and a physically sound solution for Mrk 841.

For Mrk 841 we find a very large reflection continuum but an almost normal iron line equivalent width even if, within the errors, a solution in which both components are a factor 2 larger than expected for an accretion disc is still marginally acceptable.

sources.summary: The atlas gives Mrk 841's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: Mrk 841 is an early-type galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0364 ( D 157 Mpc) with a radio-quiet quasar-regime AGN optically classified as a Sy 1.5 veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . It has mainly been studies at X-rays and also belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. Apart from , Mrk 841 was observed at MIR wavelengths by , , , and with photometers, while imaging is reported in and .

sources.summary: The paper finds Mrk 841 favors warm absorption and specifically two warm absorbers, with prior work placing the absorber near the BLR scale.

quotes.quote: I Zw1, Mrk 841, KUV 18217+6419 and Akn 120 favour the scenario of warm absorption, yielding column densities of the orders of N_ H 10^ 20 10^ 21 \ cm^ 2 and a range of ionization parameters of 0.2< <3.0 .

Mrk 841 Our best fit for this source includes 2 warm absorbers of different ionization states, a result that is consistent with the in depth analysis made by AL2010 . In this work, they estimate the density of the absorbing gas below 10^ 3 cm^ 3 , and the distance to the central source of a few tens of pc, placing the absorber in the scale of the BLR.

gmw_00000145.name: Mrk 897
gmw_00000145.overview: Mrk 897 is a compact early-type galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770] that is interacting with UGC 11680 NED01 to form the galaxy pair CPG 552 [arXiv:1310.2770]. Although historically classified as a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:2301.02252, arXiv:1310.2770], its emission is dominated by starburst or star-formation activity [arXiv:2301.02252].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies Mrk 897 as non-Seyfert, finding optical and mid-infrared line ratios consistent with starburst or star-formation-dominated emission.

quotes.quote: The two non-Sy targets that we could place in these diagrams (i.e., CGCG381-051 and Mrk 897, flagged with (A) and (B), respectively, in Fig. fig:BPT ) lie in the H ii region locus below both the and demarcation curves (solid and dotted curves, respectively). These targets were originally classified as Seyfert galaxies but their classification was revised to non-Seyfert by . Our SALT data are in line with this latter classification.

Mrk 897, the only non-Seyfert of our sample with all three neon lines detected, shows line ratios consistent with those of starburst galaxies. Two of the non-Sy, Mrk 897 and NGC 6810, have log_ 10 / of 1.38 and 1.2 , respectively and they fall in the AGN-dominated area of the first two diagrams (i.e., / \ versus / , / \ versus / ) while in the composite (AGN and star formation) region in the / \ versus / [ Ar ii ] 6.9 \ diagram. Our AGN grid only agrees well for targets in the / \ versus the mid-IR ratios planes, with the exceptions of two non-Sy (Mrk 897 and NGC 6810), whose emission is explained well by star formation (e.g., bottom-right panel of Fig. fig:mir_opt_appendix ).

sources.summary: The atlas gives Mrk 897's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: Mrk 897 is a compact early-type galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0263 ( D 107 Mpc) with an active nucleus classified optically either as Sy 2 veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 or AGN/starburst composite ( yuan_role_2010 ; adopted). The galaxy is interacting with UGC 11680 NED01, which is 1.1 ( 32 kpc) towards the west, forming the galaxy pair CPG 552 (UGC 11680). A compact radio core was detected in Mrk 897 (e.g., thean_high-resolution_2000 ).

gmw_00000146.name: Mrk 915
gmw_00000146.overview: Mrk 915 is an inclined spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.0241 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a Seyfert active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0907.0180, arXiv:1310.2770] that exhibits strong extended near-ultraviolet emission [arXiv:0907.0180] and a Compton-thin torus [arXiv:1606.04941].

sources.summary: The paper reports that Mrk 915 has strong extended near-UV emission that remains after subtracting ionized gas and is unlikely to be explained by the gas component alone.

quotes.quote: Mrk 915 (Sy1) The best value of f is around 0.30, with which most of the flattened-O shaped emission to the southwest is subtracted. There remains however a strong extended emission, that is unlikely to be due to ionised gas. This galaxy is a Sy 1 with a strong nuclear component. As in the case of Mrk 6, the best nuclear subtraction still leaves some positive residuals in the centre which, after convolving with the PSF of WFPC2, resemble a compact nuclear source.

sources.summary: The atlas gives Mrk 915's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: Mrk 915 is an inclined spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0241 ( D 96.1 Mpc) with an AGN either classified as a Sy 1.5 bennert_size_2006 , Sy 1.8 or Sy 1.9 trippe_multi-wavelength_2010 . We treat it as Sy 1.9 following the argumentation of trippe_multi-wavelength_2010 . It possesses an extended NLR (diameter 4 1.8 kpc; PA 5 ; schmitt_hubble_2003 ), and appears essentially unresolved at radio wavelengths with only a weak emission blob to the south-west schmitt_jet_2001 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 915 as one of five objects with very large iron-K equivalent width because the reflection continuum is weak, interpreting its torus as Compton-thin and reproducible with a Monte-Carlo torus model.

quotes.quote: In the rest of objects (NGC 4388, NGC 5252, LEDA 170194, Mrk 520, and Mrk 915), the EW is very large ( >3 keV) because the reflection continuum (i.e., R ) is apparently very weak. We interpret that the tori in these objects are Compton-thin, thus producing a much weaker hump at 30 keV and absorption iron-K edge features than what the pexrav model predicts. In fact, we confirm that a Monte-Carlo based torus model by can well reproduce the spectra of these objects including the continuum and iron-K emission line, as done in , , and .

gmw_00000147.name: Mrk 926
gmw_00000147.overview: Mrk 926 is a low-inclination early-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770] classified as a Seyfert 1 [arXiv:1007.3595, arXiv:2206.05237] or changing-look active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2001.04681]. It forms a double system with a companion galaxy [arXiv:2110.10051, arXiv:2305.01335] and exhibits highly variable X-ray emission [arXiv:2104.14627, arXiv:2206.05237] alongside strong Balmer line variability [arXiv:1007.3595, arXiv:0912.4306].

sources.summary: Mkn 926, also called MCG --2-58-22, faded from a luminous broad-line AGN into a weak-lined, changing-look state while retaining peculiar narrow-line features.

quotes.quote: Mkn 926, also frequently referred to as MCG --2-58-22, was identified as the optical counterpart of the bright x-ray source 2A 2302--088 by . show a spectrum recorded in 1984, when the object was in a luminous state. The broad-line component weakened substantially from about 1987 ( ), and a further significant broad-line luminosity decline ensued in around 1993 ( ). Mkn 926 was also observed spectroscopically during the Sloan Digital Sky Survey ( ), and throughout the early years of the present century the Balmer broad lines remained weak (consistent with a Seyfert 1.8 classification), though with a complex profile that included a distinct red saddle at 10000 km.s ^ 1 . The evolution of Mkn 926 since its discovery is illustrated in Fig. fig3 in the form of an approximate V magnitude light curve.

The latest spectrum of Mkn 926 is shown in Fig. fig4 . The broad component is now even weaker than it was around the time of the SDSS observations, and the `red saddle' evident in some earlier spectra is no longer visible. The broad component of H is only detectable because of the high S/N, and a spectrum of lower quality might have led to Mkn 926 being described as a Seyfert 1.9. While the Balmer and helium emission in the current spectrum is dramatically lower than 40 years ago, indeed so different that Mkn 926 can be classified as a changing look AGN, the object's peculiar narrow line spectrum has not undergone any obvious change.

sources.summary: The paper monitors Mrk 926 as a highly variable broad-line AGN, measuring a sharp continuum decline, broad Balmer satellites, host contribution, and black-hole properties.

quotes.quote: Mrk 926 is known to be a highly variable active galactic nucleus. Furthermore, it is known to show very broad-line profiles. We intended to study the continuum and line profile variations of this object with high temporal resolution in order to determine its broad-line region (BLR) structure and to derive its black hole mass.

Mrk 926 is a compact source on direct images in the optical garnier96 . We show an SDSS DR16: sdss20 image of Mrk 926 in Fig. fig:mrk926_SDSS_image . Based on SDSS -band images, it has an isophotal diameter (major axis) of 57.12 arcsec. This corresponds to 53 kpc. The deVaucouleurs diameter amounts to 6.00 arcsec, corresponding to 5.56 kpc https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ . Mrk 926 is the brighter galaxy member (R.A. = 23:04:43.5, Dec. = 08:41:09 (2000), z=0.04701 ) in a double system with PGC 1000273.

sources.summary: The paper studies Mrk 926/MCG-2-58-22 as a broad-line Seyfert 1: a compact, X-ray-active galaxy with strong Balmer variability and very broad emission lines in a double system.

quotes.quote: We selected Mrk 926 (MCG-2-58-22) as a high-priority target for a detailed spectroscopic variability study with the 9.2m Hobby-Eberly Telescope (HET). Mrk 926 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy with very broad emission lines, its Balmer and helium lines having widths (FWHM) of more than 10 000 km s ^ 1 . In their study of the long-term variability of very broad-line AGNs, Kollatschny et al. ( ) demonstrated Mrk 926 has strong H line variability amplitudes.

The nuclear activity was first detected in Mrk 926 by Ward et al. ( ) when they acquired a spectrum of the optical counterpart to the strong X-ray source. Mrk 926 is a compact source in optical images (Garnier et al. ). It is the brighter galaxy member (RA=23:04:43.5, Dec=-08:41:09 (2000), z=0.04701) in a double system.

sources.summary: The paper studies Mrk 926 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy with a variable soft X-ray excess, distant reflection, possible warm corona, and optically thin radio component consistent with a jet.

quotes.quote: To constrain the origin of the soft excess in the Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 926, we carry out an multi-epoch X-ray spectral study using observations from Suzaku (2009), XMM-Newton and NuSTAR (2016), and NuSTAR and Swift-XRT (2021). The broadband X-ray spectra of Mrk 926 contains: a thermally Comptonized primary continuum, a variable soft excess, and distant reflection. We find that in Mrk 926 as in so many sources, it is difficult to make a definite statement as to what is causing the observed soft excess. A warm coronal-like component is slightly preferred by the data but a reflection origin is also possible. Using archival radio data, we detect an optically-thin radio component in our broadband study of Mrk 926.

While this component is consistent with an optically-thin radio jet, future multi-wavelength observations including high spatial resolution radio observations at multiple frequencies are required to probe the origin of the radio emission in more detail.

sources.summary: The paper reports X-ray spectral changes in the bare Seyfert-1 galaxy, linking a vanished Compton hump, stable continuum, and FeK/soft-excess variability to changing reprocessing media.

quotes.quote: A weakening Compton hump and Soft X-ray excess detected in the Seyfert-1 galaxy MCG --02--58--22 0000-0003-2714-0487 Sibasish Laha Center for Space Science and Technology, University of Maryland Baltimore County, 1000 Hilltop Circle, Baltimore, MD 21250, USA.

We have carried out an extensive X-ray spectral study of the bare Seyfert-1 galaxy to ascertain the nature of the X-ray reprocessing media, using observations from (2009) and simultaneous observations from and (2016) . The most significant results of our investigation are: 1. The primary X-ray emission from the corona is constant in these observations, both in terms of the power law slope ( =1.80 ) and luminosity ( L_ 2-10 = 2.55 10^ 44 ). 2. The soft excess flux decreased by a factor of two in 2016, the Compton hump weakened/vanished in 2016, and the narrow FeK emission line became marginally broad ( =0.35 0.08 ) and its flux doubled in 2016.
Since the primary X-ray continuum does not change, we presume that any changes in the reprocessed emission must arise due to changes in the reprocessing media.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 0926 as the Kite's nearby QSO companion in an active binary system, assessing whether interactions with it helped form the tail or fuel nuclear activity.

quotes.quote: It has a companion galaxy, projected only 57 kpc away at a comparable redshift (z = 0.0470) , that is a QSO (Mrk 0926, or alternately PGC 070409). This pair had attracted previous attention because the Kite galaxy also shows signs of nuclear activity identified spectroscopically as ``composite" by in a study of binary active galaxies liu , although neither liu nor a subsequent study weston identified clear signs of an interaction between the galaxies.

A close interaction between the Kite and Mrk 0926, which would be required to generate a long tidal tail, would also tend to form a bridge between the galaxies and disturb the morphology of the interacting galaxies toomre,barnes , neither of which is observed. Additionally, the interaction typically generates a second long tail from the other galaxy, but none is observed to emanate from Mrk 0926.
Finally, a close interaction, which is needed to provide a sufficient kick to Kite A, might also be responsible for fuelling the central black holes in the Kite and Mrk 0926 liu,weston .

sources.summary: The paper highlights SDSS J230443.47-084108.6 as a two-epoch Balmer-profile case with changed line shape and a clear red-wing inflection in SDSS H and H spectra.

quotes.quote: In Figure 16 we show an interesting case, SDSS J230443.47-084108.6, for which we have available recorded spectra at two different epochs, about eight years apart. The top panel shows the spectrum of the H region observed in May 1993 Marziani03a and the bottom panels show the H and H spectral regions of the same source, provided by SDSS, observed in December of 2001. Not only do we see a significant change in the functional shape of the Balmer profiles, but we note the dramatic change on the red side of these lines. In the SDSS spectrum we see a clear inflection on the red wing of both H and H (see also the detailed monitoring study and complex behavior of NGC 4151 as presented in Shapovalova09 ).

sources.summary: The atlas gives Mrk 926's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: Mrk 926 is a low-inclination early-type spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0469 ( D 194 Mpc) with a Sy 1.5 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , belonging to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. It possesses a compact radio core with very weak extension in east-west direction of one arcsecond ( 0.85 kpc) scale. mundell_parsec-scale_2000 .

gmw_00000148.name: Mrk 937
gmw_00000148.overview: Mrk 937 is a face-on spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.0295, corresponding to a distance of approximately 119 Mpc [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus classified optically as a Seyfert 1 [arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The atlas gives Mrk 937's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: Mrk 937 is a face-on spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0295 ( D 119 Mpc) with an AGN with optical Sy 1 classification veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . This object remained undetected with at 12 m, and no observations are available. We observed Mrk 937 in two narrow N band filters in 2006 but did not detect any MIR emission either.

gmw_00000149.name: Mrk 1014
gmw_00000149.overview: Mrk 1014 is a radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:2308.16780, arXiv:astro-ph/0603031] hosted by a disturbed face-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:1612.04661, arXiv:1310.2770] in the late stages of a merger [arXiv:2308.16780, arXiv:2210.16764]. The system features tidal tails [arXiv:2308.16780, arXiv:astro-ph/0603031], dense star formation restricted within its central 2 kpc region [arXiv:2210.16764], and a radio jet that interacts with the interstellar medium [arXiv:astro-ph/0606540].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 1014 as a merging QSO host with extended [O III] emission, unusual gas velocities, and evidence for jet-cloud interactions.
