gmw_00000155.name: NGC 454E
gmw_00000155.overview: NGC 454E is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770] that hosts a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1201.2169, arXiv:1310.2770]. It is in the early stages of a strong interaction with a western starburst companion [arXiv:1201.2169, arXiv:1310.2770], and it exhibits a varying X-ray absorbing column density [arXiv:1201.2169, arXiv:1303.0009].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC454E as the Seyfert 2 eastern component of NGC454 and argues its X-ray absorber varied strongly, making it a changing-look AGN.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed analysis of the X-ray spectrum of the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC454E, belonging to the interacting system NGC454. Observations performed with , \ and \ allowed us to detect a dramatic change in the curvature of the 2--10 keV spectrum, revealing a significant variation of the absorbing column density along the line of sight (from 1 10^ 24 \ to 1 10^ 23 ). Consequently, we propose this source as a new member of the class of ``changing look'' AGN, i.e. AGN that have been observed both in Compton-thin ( = 10^ 23 cm^ 2 ) and reflection dominated states (Compton-thick, >10^ 24 cm^ 2 ).

Optical studies ( Arp87,Johansson88,Stiavelli98 ) of the interacting system NGC454 (see Figure fig:imaging , right panel) describe it as a pair of emission line galaxies consisting of a red elliptical galaxy (eastern component, hereafter NGC454E) and a blue irregular galaxy (western component, hereafter NGC454W), at redshift z=0.0122. The distorted morphology of both these galaxies, together with the spectroscopic and photometric evidence of a young stellar population, is a clear sign of the interacting nature of this system. Furthermore, three very blue knots (discussed in section other_src ), probably Strongren spheres surrounding clusters of very hot newly formed stars, are located (and likely related) to the south of NGC454W. HST observations of the system, performed with the Wide Field Planetary Camera 2, confirmed that NGC454 is in the early stages of interaction Stiavelli98 . The above authors stated also that an important fraction of gas has drifted to the center of the eastern component, but it has yet not produced any significant visible star formation activity; a population of young star clusters has formed around the western component. The optical spectrum of NGC454E is consistent with that of a Seyfert 2 galaxy (although none of the high excitation lines, e.g. HeII lines, can be seen) while no optical evidence of an AGN is present in the spectrum of NGC454W which is fully consistent with that of a star-forming galaxy Johansson88 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 454E as the AGN-bearing eastern component of NGC 454 and models its X-ray spectrum, absorption variability, and intrinsic luminosity.

quotes.quote: Analysis of Chandra images for NGC 454 (Mushotzky and Koss, private communication) also shows that the hard emission (above 2 keV) is centred on the eastern source, confirming its AGN nature. We henceforth refer to this object as NGC 454E for clarity, to emphasise that this is the source responsible for the AGN emission of interest here. The best-fit model is an absorbed double power-law with coupled photon indices (partial covering absorption) with an Iron line at 6.4 keV.

There are some prominent residuals at low energies ( 1 keV) which could be identified as emission from hot, diffuse gas indicative of star formation contamination to the AGN spectrum from the western component. The fit is marginally improved by inclusion of a mekal model to model these low-energy residuals (an improvement of ^ 2 7 ). There are also unmodelled residuals near the Iron K-alpha line ( 5-7 keV) which have been attributed to an ionised absorber in (see also effeddparadigm ), but we do not attempt such a detailed fit here. W09 find a column of 15.9 10^ 23 cm^ 2 from XRT spectra, in comparison to 36.8^ +2.6 _ 2.3 10^ 22 cm^ 2 found here using XMM. The variation of a factor of 2 suggest this is a `changing-look' AGN (e.g. see 2002ApJ...571..234R ), a scenario discussed extensively in by comparing the XMM-Newton dataset used here with Suzaku and Swift/XRT data.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 454E's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 454 is a strongly interacting system of two galaxies (nuclei separation 28 7 kpc; PA 70 ; johansson_activity_1988 ) at a redshift of z= 0.0122 ( D 52.3 Mpc). The eastern component, NGC 454E, is elliptical, more regular and brighter than the western starburst component. NGC 454E hosts a Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 that belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample.

gmw_00000156.name: NGC 526A
gmw_00000156.overview: NGC 526A is the western elliptical component of a strongly interacting pair of galaxies [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a changing-state active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2211.04478] that has been classified as a Seyfert 1.9 [arXiv:1310.2770] and a Seyfert 1.5 [arXiv:1310.2770], displaying a broad iron-K line feature in its X-ray spectrum [arXiv:1606.04941].

sources.summary: The paper reports a significant disk-reflection fit improvement for NGC 526A, finds the broad iron-K line important, and notes XMM-Newton work found relativistic reflection statistically indistinguishable from absorption models.

quotes.quote: Adding the disk-reflection component to the best-fit models obtained in Section sec:base_model , we find that fits are significantly improved at > 99 confidence level in four sources (2MASX J1200+0648, Fairall 49, NGC 526A, and NGC 6300). Their unfolded spectra that give a smaller ^2 value between the assumptions of =10 or 100 are shown in Figure fig:disk . Except for NGC 6300, the disk-reflection component better improves the fit at energies below 10 keV than above 10 keV. That is, a broad iron-K line feature is more essential to reproduce the spectra than the reflection hump at 30 keV in the first three objects.
analyzed the XMM-Newton spectra of NGC 526A and found that the relativistic reflection can well reproduce the spectra but is indistinguishable from absorption models in terms of statistics.

sources.summary: The paper reports that 526A has contrasting fitted column-density behavior, fixes parameters to constrain the fit, and finds agreement with previous work.

quotes.quote: 526A In tab-fit \ is \ but \ clearly . To constrain , we need to fix A_S=1 . We also fix \ to 100 \ as its lower limit tends to zero. The laha2020 \ reported for this obsID is in reasonable agreement with our \ \ value. 1.8 for this obsID and a leaking power-law scattered continuum contributes at a relative normalization of 30 .

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 526A's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 526A is the western elliptical component of an strongly interacting pair of galaxies at a redshift of z= 0.0191 ( D 82.8 Mpc) with a Sy 1.9 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , belonging to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. Note that the nucleus has formally be classified as a Sy 1.5 veron-cetty_catalogue_1996 . It possesses a very extended NLR (several kpc diameter; PA 130 ; mulchaey_emission-line_1996,bennert_size_2006 ), coinciding with the extended radio morphology nagar_radio_1999 .

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 526A changing from narrow-line type 2 behavior to broad-line turn-on changing-state AGN behavior, with a contemporaneous BAT flux increase.

quotes.quote: NGC 526A NGC 526A (BAT 72) was observed as a type 2 source with only narrow Balmer emission lines in 2009 July. In early 2010, an increase was seen in the BAT flux by around a factor of two. In 2016 and 2018, the source is observed with a broad component in the \ emission line and so we consider it to be a `turn-on' CS AGN, although we note that no corresponding change is detected in .

sources.summary: The paper reports that its BeppoSAX fit agrees with Landi et al., confirming a relatively flat intrinsic continuum with photon index near 1.54.

quotes.quote: NGC 526a: The same set of BeppoSAX data on this source have been analyzed by Landi et al. (2001). The results obtained by these authors are in agreement with ours, within the errors. In particular, it is confirmed the relatively flat continuum spectrum: the best fit values for the photon index of the intrinsic power law are in the 1.47-1.6 range, in agreement with our result, =1.54^ +0.05 _ 0.04 .

gmw_00000157.name: NGC 612
gmw_00000157.overview: NGC 612 is an S0a radio-loud galaxy [arXiv:1811.06266, arXiv:2304.06117] hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770] and a hybrid radio morphology [arXiv:1801.08237, arXiv:1811.06266]. It features a warped, star-forming disk [arXiv:1605.07565] and is connected to its neighbor NGC 619 by a 400 kpc neutral hydrogen bridge [arXiv:1801.08237, arXiv:1811.06266].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 612 as a dusty radio galaxy with a disk-like mid-infrared profile, star-forming colors, a higher inferred star-formation rate, and smoother accretion than NGC 1316.

quotes.quote: The two galaxies differ markedly in their mid-IR properties. The 3.4 m brightness distribution can be well represented by the superposition of two S ersic models in NGC 1316 and by a S ersic model and an exponential disk in NGC 612. The colors of NGC 1316 are typical of those of early-type galaxies; those of NGC 612 are in the range found for star-forming galaxies.

From the 22 m luminosity, we infer a star-formation rate of 0.7 M_ yr ^ 1 in NGC 1316 and 7 M_ yr ^ 1 in NGC 612. spectroscopy shows that the 7.7-to-11.3 m PAH line ratio is significantly lower in NGC 1316 than in NGC 612. The images reveal resolved emission from dust in the central 1 ' -2 ' of the galaxies. In NGC 1316, the extra-nuclear emission coincides with two dusty regions NW and SE of the nucleus seen in extinction in optical images and where molecular gas is known to reside. In NGC 612 it comes from a warped disk.

sources.summary: The paper presents broadband polarimetry of radio galaxy NGC 612, describing its suitability for Faraday-rotation studies, hybrid radio morphology, group interaction signatures, and H I bridge.

quotes.quote: Motivated by the recent improvement in continuous frequency coverage, we observe NGC 612 (PKS B0131-367) in an attempt to conclusively determine the physical properties of all Faraday rotating components that contributes to the observed polarisation. NGC 612 has been studied at multiple wavelengths and is an ideal candidate with which to carry these polarisation studies. Due to its relatively low redshift ( z = 0.0297 , deVaucouleurs1991 ), the two, large-scale lobes Ekers1978 span a relatively large angular scale ( 18 arcseconds) and is well-resolved by the ATCA. Resolving NGC 612 is particularly interesting due to the marked difference in morphology between the two radio lobes. NGC 612 has been classified as having a hybrid radio source morphology GopalKrishna2000 with the eastern lobe exhibiting strong FR- II FanaroffRiley1974 characteristics, marked by a hot spot (`HS', RA(J2000) = 01:34:17, Dec(J2000) = -36:30:39), whereas the western lobe more closely matches a FR-I classification with a visible jet.

The total radio power of NGC 612 is P_ 4.8 GHz 0.8 10^ 25 W Hz ^ 1 Morganti1993 , which falls between typical FR-I and FR-II values OwenLaing1989, OwenWhite1991 . NGC 612 is a member of a galaxy group with 7 members Ramella2002 in which there is evidence of a recent interaction. This is supported by existence of the dust lane in the optical counterpart Ekers1978, KotanyiEkers1979 in addition to a tenuous H i bridge reaching 400 kpc from the disk of NGC 612 towards its nearest neighbour, NGC 619 Emonts2008 .

sources.summary: NGC 612 is presented as a low-redshift S0 radio-loud AGN/HYMORS with asymmetric radio lobes, plus an H I bridge and X-ray emission toward its eastern lobe.

quotes.quote: NGC 612 is a low redshift radio-loud AGN that allows for a detailed study of the environmental impact of radio-loud jets and lobes, which may be of additional use in interpreting the observed properties of high redshift systems. NGC 612 is a typical S0 galaxy at z = 0.0297 Westerlund1966,Goss1980,Veron2001 hosting a radio-loud AGN PKS 0131-36 Ekers1978 . PKS 0131-36 has been classified as a HYbrid MOrphology Radio Source HYMORS; Gopal2000 . The eastern radio lobe contains a hotspot near the furthest edge of the radio lobe typical of Fanaroff-Riley type II ( ) radio sources Fanaroff1974 . The western lobe shows no indication of a hotspot and is more diffuse, resembling FR type I ( ) radio sources.

observations by Emonts2008 reveal a faint bridge extending 400 kpc to nearby galaxy NGC 619 with no detectable optical counterpart related to the bridge or tails. The mass distribution is mainly found in disks around NGC 619 ( M_ HI = 8.9 10^ 9 ) and NGC 612 ( M_ HI = 1.8 10^9 ), with M_ HI = 2.9 10^8 located in the bridge connecting the two galaxies see their Fig. 5 Emonts2008 . X-Ray observations by Tashiro2000 detect diffuse emission at 0.7 -- 3 keV extending away from NGC 612 towards the eastern radio lobe and no emission near the western lobe.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 612 with multiepoch X-ray torus models, finding consistent torus parameters but ambiguous variability: borus02 favors absorption changes, while UXCLUMPY favors intrinsic flux changes.

quotes.quote: Analysis of results: All models fit this source well, and our results are compatible with those derived by Zhao2020 . The best-fit values for the torus parameters are in good agreement, within errors, for all models. However, that is not the case when it comes to the variability determination.

While all models require some form of variability (T >10 for the non-variability scenario), MYTorus is not able to discern between a pure N_ H,los variability scenario and a pure flux variability with enough significance. borus02 , on the other hand, clearly favors an N_ H,los variable scenario We note that, while MYTorus and borus02 give practucally identical best-fit parameters, the errors of MYTorus are much larger. This results in the source being compatible with a non N_ H,los variability scenario . And finally, UXCLUMPY favors a scenario in which the spectral variability is predominantly caused by intrinsic flux changes, rather than absorption. We thus classify this source as `Undetermined'.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 612 as a powerful radio-source S0 galaxy with a huge rotating H I disc, young stars, a massive halo, and interaction or merger signatures.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage 2007 We present the detection of an enormous disc of cool neutral hydrogen (H I ) gas surrounding the S0 galaxy NGC 612, which hosts one of the nearest powerful radio sources (PKS 0131-36). Using the Australia Telescope Compact Array, we detect M_ HI = 1.8 10^ 9 M_ of H I emission-line gas that is distributed in a 140 kpc wide disc-like structure along the optical disc and dust-lane of NGC 612. The bulk of the gas in the disc appears to be settled in regular rotation with a total velocity range of 850 , although asymmetries in this disc indicate that perturbations are being exerted on part of the gas, possibly by a number of nearby companions. The H I disc in NGC 612 suggests that the total mass enclosed by the system is M_ enc 2.9 10^ 12 \ sin ^ 2 i \ M_ , implying that this early-type galaxy contains a massive dark matter halo. We also discuss an earlier study by Holt et al. that revealed the presence of a prominent young stellar population at various locations throughout the disc of NGC 612, indicating that this is a rare example of an extended radio source that is hosted by a galaxy with a large-scale star-forming disc.

In addition, we map a faint H I bridge along a distance of 400 kpc in between NGC 612 and the gas-rich ( M_ HI = 8.9 10^ 9 M_ ) barred galaxy NGC 619, indicating that likely an interaction between both systems occurred. From the unusual amounts of \ gas and young stars in this early-type galaxy, in combination with the detection of a faint optical shell and the system's high infra-red luminosity, we argue that either ongoing or past galaxy interactions or a major merger event are a likely mechanism for the triggering of the radio source in NGC 612.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 612 as exceptional among the sample, with a large, massive CO disc, central gap, residuals, and likely bar-related non-circular motions from externally accreted gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 612 NGC 612 is exceptional among our sources: we detect a large CO(2-1) disc extending 10 kpc along the major axis, with an estimated molecular gas mass of 2.0 10^ 10 M _ (about two order of magnitude larger than in the other CO detections; see Paper I). In this case, our initial model leaves large residuals in the inner part of the disc (peak amplitudes 80 km s ^ 1 ). Furthermore, the sharp edges of the velocity curve (Fig. fig:ngc612_hole_PVD ) cannot be well reproduced.

We find that the CO in NGC 612 is distributed in a disc-like structure with a central gap. The central gas deficiency, along with the sharp edges in the velocity curve, lead us to suppose the presence of a stellar bar in this object. This is also consistent with the high residuals ( 60 km s ^ 1 ) visible in the velocity map at the centre of the CO disc, indicating the presence of non-circular motions, expected in presence of non-axisymmetric potential due to a bar.

sources.summary: The paper models ALMA CO in NGC 0612, finding an extended rotating molecular disc, a central CO hole, and only a conservative SMBH mass upper limit.

quotes.quote: NGC 0612 is optically classified as an S0a galaxy viewed close to edge-on, characterised by an extensive and strongly warped dust distribution in the equatorial plane and by a massive, regularly-rotating stellar disc ( West66,Fasano96,Holt07 ; Figure fig:NGC612_optical , left panel). Such disc has a star formation rate of 7 M _ yr ^ 1 Asabere16 , suggesting that the galaxy may be a misclassified late-type galaxy, rather than an ETG. NGC 0612 resides in a poor group of seven galaxies Ramella02 , and forms a pair with the barred spiral galaxy NGC 0619 Emonts08 .

In NGC 0612, we detect a nearly edge-on, clumpy disc of molecular gas, extending up to 10 kpc along its major axis. At the centre of this gas distribution a clear gap is observed with an estimated radius R_ hole 0.94 , thus likely much larger than the predicted R_ SOI ( 0.13 ).
After testing different arbitrarily-chosen parametric functions, we were able to acceptably reproduce the observed velocity and set a conservative upper limit of M_ BH 3.2 10^ 9 M _ .

sources.summary: The paper reviews NGC 612 broadband radio polarimetry, linking its lobe-circumgalactic-gas interaction, Faraday rotation, depolarization, and hotspot RM enhancement to turbulent intragroup magnetic material.

quotes.quote: Banfield et al. showed broad-band radio polarization observations of NGC 612 taken with the ATCA from 1 to 3 GHz. They examined the linear polarization, Faraday rotation, and depolarization distributions resulting from the interaction between the lobes of the radio galaxy NGC 612 and the circumgalactic gas. They found that the polarized properties are tracing a behavior, likely due to an external Faraday rotation characterized by a turbulent magnetic field in the intra-group medium. Evidence of a correlation between the Faraday structure and the radio morphology was found. In particular, the increase in the RM and the depolarization at the hotspot is most likely explained by the compression of the ambient gas due to the bow-shock region on to the lobe.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 612 as an FR-II radio galaxy detected by HIPASS in broad H I emission and narrow absorption, with a large H I measurement including neighboring galaxies.

quotes.quote: HIPASS J0133--36 : wide \ emission and narrow \ absorption is also detected towards the FR-II radio galaxy NGC 612 (PKS 0131--36; \ = 8925 ). The mid-point of the \ emission agrees with that of the narrow \ absorption ( 8789 ).
We note a 100 \ offset to their \ absorption line measurement. The HIPASS \ measurement, comprising NGC 612 and neighbouring galaxies, is 13 Jy , corresponding to 4.7 10^ 10 \ (assuming D = 125 Mpc).

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 612's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 612 is a highly inclined, radio-loud early-type galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0298 ( D 132 Mpc) with hybrid radio morphology gopal-krishna_extragalactic_2000 and a Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 that belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample. After , NGC 612 was observed with /IRS in LR staring mode. The spectrum indicates dominant and intense star formation with strong PAH features, silicate 10 m absorption and a slightly red spectral slope in F_ space.

sources.summary: The paper notes a patchy ALMA structure around NGC 612's bright nuclear component and says comparable radio data are insufficient to interpret it.

quotes.quote: 05: NGC 612 The ALMA image of this object was taken at a resolution of 0 05, revealing a patchy structure around the bright nuclear component within a radius of 0 2. Based on a VLA observation at 5 GHz, reported an unresolved component whose diameter size was constrained to be < 0 3. This resolution would be almost the highest ever achieved for this object, except for that of the ALMA observation, and thus there seem to be no data that help us to better understand the patchy structure.

gmw_00000158.name: NGC 613
gmw_00000158.overview: NGC 613 is a nearby barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:0710.4036, arXiv:2405.19709] that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1508.05065, arXiv:1905.01979] and a circumnuclear star-forming ring [arXiv:0709.0353, arXiv:2010.01974]. High-resolution observations reveal a trailing nuclear spiral [arXiv:1811.00984, arXiv:1908.03149] and a molecular outflow aligned with its central radio jet [arXiv:1911.00707, arXiv:2311.03587].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 613 as a barred Sbc spiral with a nuclear star-forming ring, quiescent NIR nucleus, possible AGN outflow, and jet-disturbed ring morphology.

quotes.quote: NGC 613 NGC 613 is a large, relatively nearby ( d = 17.5 ), strongly barred Sbc spiral. It is classified as a Seyfert galaxy in the NED database, although without a type designation, and in fact, it is somewhat unclear on what basis this classification was made. The optical emission line spectrum of the NGC 613 nucleus is classified as ``composite'' by . Furthermore, NGC 613 emits neither detectable X-ray emission in the 2-10 band, nor any high excitation MIR lines such as [ OIV ] or [ SiII ] that are usually found in AGN sat04 .

Emission line morphology - evidence for an AGN outflow The \ emission shows a very clearly defined ring structure, composed of seven almost regularly spaced bright clumps that are the sites of current massive star formation. These ``hot spots'' are brightest along the southern half of the ring, while the northern half shows a well-defined ``gap'' at PA 30 which is also evident in the \ emission map. This direction agrees well with that of the radio jet found by and studied further by . It thus appears that a mechanical outflow from the AGN has disturbed the ring morphology.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 613's inner 700 pc, finding AGN and shock excitation, star-forming ring hot spots, disturbed ring morphology, a central molecular-gas reservoir, and an impending nuclear starburst.

quotes.quote: We present near-infrared ( H and K band) integral-field observations of the inner 700 pc of the active spiral galaxy NGC 613, obtained with SINFONI on the Very Large Telescope. We use emission-line ratios to determine the dominant excitation mechanisms in different regions within our field-of-view, in particular the active nucleus and the star-forming circum-nuclear ring. Diagnostic diagrams involving \ and \ fluxes indicate that the gas is not only photoionized by the AGN in the nucleus of NGC 613, but also shock-heated. On the other hand, the emission line ratios measured in the ``hot spots'' along the ring are fully consistent with them being young star forming regions.

The ring morphology appears disturbed by a radial outflow of material from the AGN, which is confirmed by the existence of a weak jet in archival radio maps. However, this jet does not seem to have any significant effect on the morphology of the large ( 8 10 ^7 ) reservoir of molecular gas that has accumulated inside the central 100 pc. Such a concentration of molecular gas around an AGN is unusual, and supports a scenario in which star formation is recurrent and episodic in spiral galaxies. In this context, NGC 613 appears to be in final stages of the gas accumulation phase, and is likely to undergo a nuclear starburst in the near future.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA [C I] and CO observations of NGC 613, resolving its central disk and star-forming ring and interpreting minor-axis [C I] kinematics as a tentative AGN-driven molecular outflow.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The phase reference center of ( _ J2000.0 , _ J2000.0 )= ( 1h34m18.19s , 29D25'06.60'' ) was adopted (Miyamoto et al. 2017).

quotes.quote: We report ALMA observations of [ C I ] ( ^3P_1-^3P_0 ), C 13 O, and C O 18 ( J=1-0 ) toward the central region of a nearby Seyfert galaxy NGC 613. The very high resolutions of 0 26 0 23 (= 22 20 pc) for [ C I ] and 0 42 0 35 (= 36 30 pc) for C 13 O, and C O 18 resolve the circum-nuclear disk (CND) and star-forming ring. The distribution of [ C I ] in the ring resembles that of the CO emission, although [ C I ] is prominent in the CND. This can be caused by the low intensities of the CO isotopes due to the low optical depths under the high temperature in the CND. We found that the intensity ratios of [ C I ] to C 12 O(3--2) ( R_ C I /CO ) and to C 13 O(1--0) ( R_ C I / C 13 O ) are high at several positions around the edge of the ring.

The [ C I ] kinematics along the minor axis of NGC 613 could be interpreted as a bubbly molecular outflow. The outflow rate of molecular gas is higher than star formation rate in the CND. The flow could be mainly boosted by the AGN through its radio jets.

sources.summary: The paper uses ALMA CO observations to show NGC 613 has a nuclear spiral/ring system, an AGN or radio-jet-driven fossil molecular outflow, and negative-torque gas inflow feeding its central black hole.

quotes.quote: The morphology of CO(3-2) line emission reveals a 2-arm trailing nuclear spiral at 100 pc and a circumnuclear ring at 350 pc radius, that is coincident with the star-forming ring seen in the optical images. Also, we find evidence of a filamentary structure connecting the ring and the nuclear spiral. The ring reveals two breaks into two winding spiral arms corresponding to the dust lanes in the optical images.

The outflow is mainly boosted by the AGN through entrainment by the radio jet, but given the weak nuclear activity of NGC 613, we might be witnessing a fossil outflow , resulted from a strong past AGN that now has already faded. Furthermore, the nuclear trailing spiral observed in CO emission is inside the inner Lindblad resonance (ILR) ring of the bar. We compute the gravitational torques exerted in the gas to estimate the efficiency of the angular momentum exchange. The gravity torques are negative from 25 to 100 pc and the gas loses its angular momentum in a rotation period, providing evidence of a highly efficient inflow towards the center. This phenomenon shows that the massive central black hole has a significant dynamical influence on the gas, triggering the inflowing of molecular gas to feed the black hole.

sources.summary: The paper uses BVRI imaging of NGC 613 to measure bar corotation, identify an inner phase crossing, and connect nuclear structure to a star-forming ring and disk.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present BVRI imaging data of NGC 613. We use these data to determine the corotation radius of the bar, using the photometric phase crossing method. This method uses the phase angle of the spiral structure in several wavebands, and looks for a crossing between the blue ( B ) light and the redder wavebands (e.g., R or I ). For NGC 613, we find two phase crossings, an outer phase crossing at 136 8 arcsec and an inner phase crossing at 16 8 arcsec. We argue that the outer phase crossing is due to the bar corotation radius, and from the bar length of R_ bar =90.0 4.0 arcsec we go on to calculate a relative bar pattern speed of R =1.5 0.1 , which is consistent with the results of previous methods described in the literature.

All of our structure maps and our color map highlight a nuclear ring of star formation at a radius of 4 arcsec, which had also been observed recently using ALMA. Furthermore, the radius of our inner phase crossing appears to be consistent with the size of a nuclear disk of star formation that has been recently detected and described in the literature.

sources.summary: ALMA CO maps show NGC 613 has an ILR nuclear ring, a two-arm circumnuclear spiral, and a compact rotating molecular torus with kinematic decoupling and shock-dominated ionized gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 613 shows an incomplete ring with high-density contrast, as seen in our large-scale map of CO(3-2) (Fig. fig:613-1326 ), and emission from a clear trailing two-arm spiral structure in the circumnuclear disk (CND). This galaxy has a typical ILR nuclear ring, of radius 3.5 arcsec (or 300 pc), just inside the two characteristic leading dust lanes of the bar, which are tangent to the ring. Inside the ring, there is a central molecular component, or CND of radius 1 = 83 pc. Water masers have been detected in the nucleus Kondratko2006 .

Inside the nuclear spiral structure, there is a very dense and compact (radius 14 pc) rotating component, which might be interpreted as the molecular torus. The torus is also distinguished by a kinematic decoupling, with a major axis PA=0 ^ , (better seen in HCO ^+ (4-3)), while the outside axis is PA=105 ^ . The excitation of the ionized gas in the torus is dominated by shocks Davies2017 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 613 as showing simultaneous AGN fueling and feedback, with a resolved nuclear spiral, decoupled 14 pc torus, rapid angular-momentum loss, and molecular outflow.

quotes.quote: The case of NGC 613 is even more interesting, revealing both fueling due to a trailing nuclear spiral, but in simultaneity, a molecular outflow due to the AGN feedback Audibert2019 . With 0.09'' x 0.06'' resolution ( 5pc), the nuclear spiral is well resolved, with a radius of 60pc, inside the ILR ring of R=300pc. Inside the spiral, a torus, or circum-nuclar disk of radius 14pc is well decoupled and contrasted both in its morphology and kinematics, in particular in the dense tracers Combes2019 . Figure fig4 shows that the gas inside 100pc is losing quickly its angular momentum. The position-velocity diagrams along the major and minor axes reveal a molecular outflow Audibert2019 .

The mass involved in the outflow is 2 10 ^6 M _ , and the mass outflow rate is 27 M _ /yr. This outflow could be entrained by a radio jet, but given the weak radio activity of NGC 613, it might be a fossil outflow, reflecting its past activity.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 613 as showing a trailing nuclear spiral, negative molecular-gas torques, rapid angular-momentum loss, and a compact molecular outflow aligned with the radio jet.

quotes.quote: Such trailing nuclear spirals have been found also in NGC 613 Audibert2019 and in NGC 1808 (Audibert et al. 2020, in prep.), as can be seen in Fig. fig1 . The nuclear spiral is conspicuous in the CO(3-2) emission line, but also in the dense gas tracers like HCN, HCO ^+ or CS. The rotation curves of these galaxies, derived both from the stellar potential (traced by near-infrared images) and the observed kinematics of the gas, confirm that the nuclear gas is falling within the sphere of influence of the black holes. The gravity torques have been measured on the deprojected images of the molecular gas, with the method described in , and the torques are indeed negative Audibert2019 .

This is the case of NGC 613, where a very short (23pc) and small velocity (300km/s) outflow is detected on the minor axis, in the same direction of the radio jet, mapped at cm wavelength with the VLA Audibert2019 . This was possible thanks to the ALMA resolution of 60 mas (5pc). The coincidence with the radio jet strongly suggests that the outflow is AGN feedback in the radio mode.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 613 central kinematics and stellar archaeology, describing its bar, obscured LINER-like AGN, circumnuclear star-forming ring, inner intermediate-age ring, and stellar rotation.

quotes.quote: This work is a continuation of paty , hereafter Paper I, and is focused on the study of the stellar and gas kinematics and of the stellar archaeology of the central region of NGC 613, with the aim of determining a dynamic and evolutionary scenario to this galaxy nucleus. NGC 613 is an SB(rs)bc galaxy, located at 26 5 Mpc distancia . One of its most outstanding morphological features is the bar, whose size is 139 arcsec ( 17 kpc; kormendy ). The bar interacts with the nuclear region bringing gas from the outer regions to the centre through a nuclear spiral ( audibert ; Paper I) interfering in the star formation, which seems to take place in bursts and not in a continuous form alloin .

As discussed in Paper I and by audibert , the AGN of NGC 613 has emission-line ratios compatible with the ones of low-ionization nuclear emission-line regions (LINERs) and previous works indicate that it is considerably obscured castangia2013,ASMUS2015 . The bolometric luminosity of the AGN estimated by daviesbecca is 1.6 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 . As mentioned in Paper I and in previous works ( hummel ; BOKERIAU,boker ; falconIAU,falcon613 ; Paper I), NGC 613 has a star-forming ring that contains eight H ii regions.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 613 as a Seyfert/nuclear starburst galaxy with a trailing CO nuclear spiral, circumnuclear ring, skewed nuclear kinematics, and an AGN-boosted molecular outflow.

quotes.quote: In the Seyfert/nuclear starburst galaxy NGC 613, we have combined ALMA Cycles 3 and 4 observations at a spatial resolution of 17 pc ane19 . The morphology of CO(3-2) line emission reveals a 2-arm trailing nuclear spiral at 100 pc and a circumnuclear ring at 350 pc radius, that is coincident with the star-forming ring seen in the optical images. The molecular gas in the galaxy disk is in a remarkably regular rotation, however, the kinematics in the nuclear region is very skewed. We find broad wings in the nuclear spectra of CO and dense gas tracers, with velocities reaching up to 300 km/s, associated with a molecular outflow emanating from the nucleus ( 25 pc, Fig. out ). We derive a molecular outflow mass M_ out =2 10 ^6 M _ and a mass outflow rate of M _ out = 27 M_ yr^ 1 .

The outflow is mainly boosted by the AGN through entrainment by the radio jet, but given the weak nuclear activity of NGC 613, we proposed that we might be witnessing a fossil outflow , resulted from a strong past AGN that now has already faded. From 25 to 100 pc, the nuclear trailing spiral observed in CO emission inside the Inner Lindblad Resonance (ILR) ring is efficiently driving gas towards the center.

sources.summary: The paper uses SINFONI and HST observations of NGC 613's circumnuclear star-forming ring to test star-formation models, identifying ODRs and a hotspot age sequence.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present our efforts to understand the preferred model of star formation along the rings, based on SINFONI integral-field observations, of circumnuclear star-forming rings in five nearby spiral galaxies (NGC 613, NGC 1079, NGC 1300, NGC 5248, IC 1438).

In Figure 3 we put these two models to the test by showing the observations for NGC 613, one of the five galaxies in our sample. In the left panel we display a F666W HST image of the nuclear regions. The ring is oulined with an ellipse, and two star symbols mark the position of the ODRs (based on the regions with highest dust extinction in the ring). In the right panel we present a false-color image constructed from the SINFONI emission line maps of He I , Br , and [ Fe II ] assigned to the blue, green, and red color channels, respectively. The bottom half of the ring shows the trends expected under the 'Pearls on a string' picture: an age sequence (blue-green-red) in the different hotspots.

sources.summary: The paper reports a 22 GHz water megamaser in NGC 613, describing the barred spiral's composite spectrum with a Seyfert-like contribution, inner radio structure, CO content, and AGN-favored association.

quotes.quote: NGC 613 is a barred spiral galaxy RC3 , located at a distance of 20 Mpc (1 100 pc, for H_0 =75 kms Mpc ^ 1 ). Optical spectroscopy revealed that this galaxy has a composite spectrum, with an region and a Seyfert-like contribution simultaneously present veron . High resolution radio continuum maps of the inner kpcs of NGC 613 show a linear structure, consisting of three distinct blobs, oriented perpendicular to a ring-like feature of radius 350 pc hummel . A large amount of CO has been detected n613co but high angular resolution images of molecular emission have, so far, not yet been reported.

Two detections are reported: a megamaser with an isotropic luminosity, , of 35 in the Seyfert/ galaxy NGC 613 and a kilomaser with 1 \ in the merger system NGC 520. The high luminosity and the presence of a Seyfert nucleus favor an association for NGC 613 with an active galactic nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper decomposes NGC 613's central emission into star formation, AGN, and shock components, showing its barred spiral nucleus contains an ionization cone, radio jet, and shocked gas.

quotes.quote: In this paper we isolate the contributions of star formation, shock excitation and active galactic nucleus (AGN) activity to the emission line luminosities of individual spatially resolved regions across the central 3 3 kpc ^2 region of the active barred spiral galaxy NGC 613. The star formation rate and AGN luminosity calculated from the decomposed emission line maps are in close agreement with independent estimates from data at other wavelengths. The star formation component traces the B-band stellar continuum emission, and the AGN component forms an ionization cone which is aligned with the nuclear radio jet.

This paper is based on integral field data for NGC 613, a nearby ( D = 26.4 Mpc, assuming H_0 = 73.0 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ; Tully09, Nasonova11 ) SBbc galaxy deVaucouleurs91 with clear evidence for current star formation, shock excitation FalconBarroso14 , and AGN activity Goulding09 . The kiloparsec-scale radio structure of the galaxy (as imaged by the NRAO VLA Sky Survey at 1.4 GHZ) reveals enhanced star formation activity at the ends of the bar, as well as enhanced nuclear emission.

Previous optical, infrared and radio studies of NGC 613 have revealed extended star formation which is enhanced at the ends of the bar, enhanced nuclear emission and shocked gas in the circumnuclear regions.

sources.summary: The paper studies GMCs in NGC 613, a barred spiral with a nuclear ring, linking enhanced linewidths to feedback, accretion, collisions, and bar-driven inflow.

quotes.quote: NGC 613 is a nearby barred spiral galaxy with a nuclear ring. Exploiting high spatial resolution ( 20 pc) Atacama Large Millimeter/sub-millimeter Array \ observations, we study the giant molecular clouds (GMCs) in the nuclear ring and its vicinity, identifying 158 spatially- and spectrally-resolved GMCs. We discuss the possible origins of the steep size -- linewidth relation and enhanced observed linewidths of the clouds and suggest that a combination of mechanisms such as stellar feedback, gas accretion and cloud-cloud collisions, as well as the gas inflows driven by the large-scale bar, may play a role.

NGC 613 has a large-scale bar, an inner star-forming ring encircling the bar and weak spiral arms protruding from the bar, at a distance D=17.5 Mpc tully1988_distance . In the central regions (i.e. \ well within a diameter of 1 kpc), NGC 613 has a bright core and a well-studied star-forming nuclear ring hummel1992, boker2007, boker2008, falcon-barroso2008, falcon-barroso2014, miyamoto2017_ngc613, miyamoto2018_ngc613 , that are prominent in both optical continuum and molecular gas emission. NGC 613 also harbours a nuclear spiral audibert2019 , a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus (LL-AGN; veron-cetty_1986,hummel1987,goulding2009 ), prominent radio jets miyamoto2017_ngc613 , shock excitation falcon-barroso2014 and a shock co-spatial with the peak of the radio jet davies2019_ngc613_shock .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 613 as a bright barred multi-armed spiral with an active nucleus, aligned radio sources, and paired high-redshift quasars interpreted as ejection-related.

quotes.quote: NGC 613 (AM 0132-294) is a multi armed spiral with an active nucleus. Radio emission is aligned along the narrow inner bar of the spiral and appears to include inner and outer pairs of radio sources which define a line of ejection from this nucleus. Four high redshift quasars from the 2dF survey fall further out along this same line.

The galaxy NGC 613 appears as a striking example of the class of multi-armed spirals (Fig. 1). NGC 613 (AM0132-294) is bright ( B_T= 10.75 ), barred (SBb(rs)II) and has a redshift of z = .005. High resolution images show a narrow, high brightness bar extending from the nucleus out on either side, roughly NW and SE.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 613 as SN 2016gkg's host, adopts a Tully-Fisher distance, analyzes archival HST imaging, and measures a slightly sub-solar host-galaxy metallicity from emission-line ratios.

quotes.quote: Throughout this paper, we assume a Tully-Fisher distance to NGC 613 of 26.4 5.3 Mpc , with a corresponding distance modulus of 32.11 0.44 nasonova+11 , and Milky Way extinction of A_ V = 0.053 schlafly+11 . Archival Data We obtained archival imaging of NGC 613 from the \ Legacy Archive https://hla.stsci.edu/hla_faq.html from 21 August 2001 (Cycle 10, Proposal ID 9042, PI Stephen Smartt).

As we mention in sec:observations , we aligned the slit of the Goodman Spectrograph to obtain a spectrum of NGC 613 simultaneously with SN 2016gkg. Analysis of the host galaxy emission line ratio R_ 23 = I_ [ O ] 3727 + I_ [ O ] 4959 + I_ [ O ] 5007 I_ H using the calibration in kobulnicky+04 suggests that NGC 613 has 12 + ( O / H ) = 8.61 0.15 with an implied metallicity of Z = 0.012 0.004 . This value is slightly sub-solar ( 12 + ( O / H )_ = 8.7 ), although it agrees with the solar value to within our error bars.

sources.summary: The review presents NGC 613 as a strong barred galaxy whose high-resolution ALMA data reveal an internal trailing nuclear spiral, molecular torus, rapid angular-momentum loss, and molecular outflow.

quotes.quote: NGC 613 is the academic case of a strong barred galaxy, with a star forming gas ring at ILR. The first ALMA observations, with moderate spatial resolution, could see only the ring, without resolving the internal structure Miyamoto2017 . With a beam of 60mas, it was possible to clearly see an internal trailing nuclear spiral, and inside the spiral a molecular torus (cf Fig. fig8 ). The computation of the torques indicates that the gas can lose all its angular momentum in only one rotation, when inside 50 pc radius Audibert2019 . In addition, a molecular outflow is detected along the minor axis, parallel to the cm-wave detected radio jet.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 613 as a barred ringed spiral whose model fit is weak, reproducing only the inner ring and small portions of arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 613 : this SBbc ( T=4 ) galaxy has a bar, inner ring and several spiral arms emerging from the ring. The Fourier phase angle method gives too high bar radius, well in the area of spiral arms. The fit, which sets R 1.6 , is not very good (see Fig. modsel1 ): only the inner ring and small parts of the arms are reproduced. The inner ring would allow also a bit higher pattern speed, which would set the galaxy clearly to the domain of fast bars.

sources.summary: The paper adds NGC 613 to the spiral black-hole sample and identifies it as a barred-galaxy outlier whose maximum rotational velocity may be anomalous.

quotes.quote: Here, we use the same sample, plus four new galaxies (NGC 613, NGC 1365, NGC 1566, and NGC 1672) from Combes:2019 , making our total sample of spiral galaxies with directly measured black hole masses twice the size of recent studies e.g., Sabra:2015 .

Except for NGC 4395, it is apparent from inspection of Figure fig:v-sigma that five galaxies (Circinus, NGC 613, NGC 1300, NGC 4151, and NGC 5055) are mild outliers, noticeably outside the 1 ( 1 _ rms ) scatter band. Of these five galaxies, two galaxies (NGC 1300 and NGC 5055) are >2 outliers. Davis:2018 note that NGC 1300 is an outlier in the M_ BH - M_ *,tot diagram for spiral galaxies, with either an overmassive black hole or a low total stellar mass, which is matched here by either a low v_ max or a high _0 . Davis:2017 pointed out that NGC 5055 is an outlier in the M_ BH - _0 diagram for spiral galaxies, with either an overmassive black hole or a low central stellar velocity dispersion. Our ( _0, v_ max ) dataset can be described by a Pearson correlation coefficient = 0.41 , and a p value probability equal to 9.35 10^ 3 that the null hypothesis is true. The Spearman rank-order correlation coefficient r_s=0.40 , with p_s=1.07 10^ 2 that the null hypothesis is true. However, the slope of the v_ max - _0 relation is inconsistent with a value of 1 at the 3.5 level. That is, v_ max / _0 is not a constant ratio. We do, however, acknowledge that the v_ max - _0 relation may be bent or curved, but we require more data with _0<100 km s^ 1 to see this. We find that the slope of our v_ max - _0 relation is shallower than but consistent with the v_ rot - _0 slope ( 0.84 0.09 ) from Ferrarese:2002 for 38 spiral galaxies; the slope ( 0.74 0.07 ) from Pizzella:2005 for 40 high surface brightness disk galaxies, eight giant low surface brightness galaxies, and 24 elliptical galaxies; and the slope ( 0.90 0.15 ) from Kormendy:Bender:2011 for 30 spiral galaxies, which are a subset of the Ferrarese:2002 sample; at the levels of 1.00 , 0.53 , and 1.00 , respectively. Our sample selection of only galaxies with dynamically estimated black hole masses does appear to have resulted in a selection bias, artificially truncating data such that we have a deficit of galaxies with _0 100 km s^ 1 (as seen in Figure fig:v-sigma ). their Figure 10 Lynden-Bell:1988 discuss this problem and present a solution by modifying the type of linear regression. By using a regression of v_ max on _0 , i.e., the bces (Y | X) routine that minimizes the residuals in the Y-direction, we can generate a relation that is less strongly affected by this bias. Doing so, we find rCl v_ max km s^ 1 & = & (0.30 0.11) _0 141 km s^ 1 && + (2.33 0.01), with _ rms = 0.07 dex and =0.06 dex, both in the v_ max direction (see Figure fig:v-sigma ). Indeed, it is prudent to show concern for possible selection bias. Batcheldor:2010 pointed out that a false M_ BH - _0 relation can be obtained from galaxies with randomly assigned black hole masses by imposing a spatial resolution cutoff to censor unresolvable spheres of influence. Thus, it is important to be cautious about unobserved data in samples of spiral galaxies with dynamical black hole mass estimates. However, it is also possible that the v_ max - _0 relation breaks down for small galaxies. For example, see their Figure 1 Courteau:2007 show that the v_ max - _0 relation only holds for galaxies with _0 80 km s ^ 1 and v_ max 200 km s ^ 1 , and they attribute the breakdown for smaller galaxies to less certain rotational and dispersion estimates due to amplified gas turbulence, velocity anisotropy, and measurement errors. Pseudobulges Galaxies that allegedly contain pseudobulges See Fisher:Drory:2016 for a review, summarizing the observed properties of pseudobulges and classical bulges, and see Graham:2015 for concerns with identifying pseudobulges, splitting what may be a bulge continuum, and galaxies hosting both types of bulge see for semianalytical models of galaxy formation yielding composite bulges Izquierdo-Villalba:2019 . Equally concerning, Costantin:2018 assess that bulge classification is difficult with any observational diagnostic other than their intrinsic three-dimensional shape; classical bulges appear as thick oblate spheroids, with pseudobulges distinguishable by their relative disk-like flattening. constitute the bulk of our sample i.e., 36 out of 42; Davis:2017 . For local ( z 0 ) galaxies, the central stellar velocity dispersion is primarily a measurement of the kinematics of the bulge; however, for those galaxies with only pseudobulges or small bulges, the kinematics of the bar and disk will contribute in a higher proportion than the weak contribution in galaxies with large classical bulges Courteau:2007 . Similarly, the use of _e (the velocity dispersion within the effective half-light radius of a galaxy) is not desirable because it is heavily biased by any bar or disk in the galaxy. Hartmann:2014 show that the central _0 is not only _0( bulge ) but rather _0( bulge + disk ) due to contamination by the disk, which increases with disk inclination. When a classical bulge does not dominate, it is important to take into account the disk, the effect of gas (whereas the gas is negligible in massive galaxies and ellipticals), the bar orientation, Bars, when viewed from an end-on orientation, may exhibit a small secondary biasing effect. Sight-lines down the major axis of a bar should elevate the observed _0 , and thus more edge-on disks would have higher _0 values if the bar were additionally positioned in the right way within the disk plane for this sight-line to occur. the spectral line used for the measurement, Harris:2012 point out that the spectral line used for the measurement of _0 can bias the result by as much as 8 . the rotation, Spectroscopic measurements of velocity dispersion through a fixed aperture do not yield reliable estimates for the true _0 of a galaxy due to contamination from galactic rotation Taylor:2010,Bezanson:2011,Uitert:2013,Hasan:2019 . Kang:2013 address the problem of rotation contaminating the _0 measurement and try to remove the rotation and isolate the true _0 with small apertures. They report consistent _0 values from the optical and near-infrared absorption lines, but find that a rotation component can cause _0 to vary by up to 20 in a single-aperture spectrum. and the inclination. These complications are beyond the scope of this project; however, we might suspect a dependency on inclination for our sample, especially for the galaxies with the lowest observed _0 values, where disk star contamination ( if present) will be fractionally more noteworthy. An additional investigation of the bar strength is beyond the desired scope of this work; however, a significant follow-up study might garner further insight. For example, NGC 613 and NGC 1300 (both classical barred galaxies) are prominent outliers in Figure fig:v-sigma , suggesting that their bars might have significantly altered the kinematics of these galaxies. Graham:2011 demonstrate that the scatter in the M_ BH - _0 diagram can be mitigated by fitting an offset relation for barred galaxies. For pseudobulges built from bars, the ``bulge'' is very flattened, and the velocity dispersion is anisotropic, much larger in the horizontal direction than in the vertical direction. Bellovary:2014 specifically treat this anisotropic issue, and show that increased inclination not only produces an elevated scatter at the low-mass end of the M_ BH - _0 relation, but also introduces a bias, to increase globally both the average and the median value above the true _0 , which tends to show an offset M_ BH - _0 relation for disky galaxies. This bias applies not just to barred galaxies but all to spiral galaxies because all spiral galaxies will likely possess a bar at some point in their life. Sheth:2008 describe the development of a bar as an indicator of maturity, although bars may be recurrent and therefore not a sign of either maturity or immaturity. In consideration of the effects of inclination, we have investigated _0 vs. \ (i) and found no correlation. We do, however, note that some galaxies have uncharacteristically low _0 values. For instance, NGC 253 (the Sculptor galaxy) has _0=(96 18) km s^ 1 , but it is a bulgy, massive, and inclined ( i=75 3 ) galaxy. Perhaps some explanation for this galaxy can be obtained by considering that NGC 253 is a prototypical starburst galaxy Watson:1996,Kornei:2009,Leroy:2018,Mangum:2019,Martin:2019,Takano:2019 . As a result, _0 may depend on the age of the stars: when there is gas, young stars can form from the gas, strongly weight the optical spectrum, and ``cool'' the stellar component. This is contrary to a collision-less simulation, where the stellar component only heats up. This process in the disk can contribute to the measurement of _0 , leading to a small _0 drop at the center of a galaxy and could explain why _0 is low for NGC 253 and other star-forming galaxies. Indeed, other prominent starburst galaxies display this behavior. The v_ max / _0 ratios of NGC 253 and three other prominent star-forming galaxies, IC 342, NGC 2146, and NGC 6946 see and references therein Gorski:2018 , are all elevated with v_ max / _0=2.04 0.38 , 3.11 0.49 , 2.33 0.16 , and 5.66 0.98 (all from HyperLeda), respectively. Moreover, the bar in NGC 253 is not end-on but inclined (if not completely side-on), reducing its effect on _0 . The widespread presence of low- to medium-luminosity, active galactic nuclei (AGNs) in spiral galaxies e.g., Beifiori:2009,Jiang:2011b,Xiao:2011 indicates that the efficient growth of SMBHs through the accretion of gas is prevalent. It is evident that secular processes in disk galaxies play a key role in the development of SMBHs Cisternas:2011,Schawinski:2011,Schawinski:2012,Salvo:2012,Kocevski:2012,Simmons:2012,Treister:2012,Debattista:2013,Zubovas:2019 . Nuclear bars have been theorized to drive gas inflows into the center, feeding black holes e.g., Shlosman:1989,Shlosman:1990,Hopkins:2010 . see also Valluri:2016 Du:2017 considered the effect of an SMBH on a nuclear bar, showing that nuclear bars are destroyed at lower SMBH masses than the large-scale bars in their simulations. Specifically, they found that embedded bars are quickly dissolved when M_ BH 0.2 M_ *,tot , halting the inner-bar-driven gas-feeding mechanism. For comparison, we find that for the subsample of 35 pseudobulge spiral galaxies in Davis:2018 , the median ratio is is approximately an order of magnitude lower, M_ BH =(0.016 0.011 ) M_ *,tot . The central velocity dispersion is enhanced by the presence of a bar, ultimately leading to slightly higher values of _0 in barred galaxies than in unbarred galaxies. Hartmann:2014 conducted simulations, showing that bar growth heats the disk, forms the bulge, and concentrates the mass, The bar strength is not necessarily a clear indicator of mass concentration, as a galaxy could have already passed its maturity, concentrated its mass, and its once strong bar could now appear weakened or have disappeared. therefore the mass evolution increases the central velocity dispersion, The simulations of Hartmann:2014 show that the formation of a bar yields an elevated _0 even when viewing the disk face-on. This suggests a connection with out-of-plane buckling that leads to peanut shell-shaped structures Burbidge:1959,Vaucouleurs:1974,Illingworth:1983,Jarvis:1986,Combes:1990,Bureau:1999,Lutticke:2000,Lutticke:2000b,Patsis:2002,Buta:2007,Athanassoula:2016,Ciambur:2016 that are more readily visible in edge-on spiral galaxies, but are also recognizable in more face-on systems Laurikainen:2011,Laurikainen:2013,Laurikainen:2014,Laurikainen:2015,Laurikainen:2018,Erwin:2013,Athanassoula:2015,Buta:2015,Laurikainen:2016,Laurikainen:2017,Herrera-Endoqui:2017,Salo:2017,Salo:2017b,Saha:2018 . The bar or peanut shell shape may also increase the vertical dispersion of the disk, in addition to the bar, but this is a small effect considering the great streaming in the disk. Therefore, if Hartmann:2014 see an effect for i=0 , it is more likely due to the concentration of the mass in the center and the global heating than due to the peanut shell shape. Moreover, when gas is present, there is a diminished box or peanut shell-shape effect; the presence of a large gas fraction tends to weaken or destroy the peanut shell shape. which causes barred galaxies to move to higher velocity dispersions in the M_ BH - _0 diagram. However, their simulations did not include gas see Seo:2019 , which meant that gas was not present to possibly form new stars (cooling the stellar component and decreasing _0 ) or fuel SMBHs in their galaxies (increase M_ BH ). From complementary simulations, Brown:2013 found that the growth of an SMBH also affects the nuclear stellar kinematics of a galaxy, increasing _0 . Hartmann:2014 remark that the increase in _0 due to SMBH growth within a bar, seen by Brown:2013 , is only a small effect ( 7 ) compared to that resulting from the formation and evolution of a bar. The M_ DM - _0 Relation As mentioned in Section sec:intro , some theoretical models suggest that dark matter halos should be related to the mass of their SMBHs. Additionally, lambda cold dark matter ( CDM) disk--bulge--halo models indicate that the disks and bulges are formed from the cooling of baryons inside dark matter halos, implying that the disk and bulge of a galaxy should also be partially determined by the properties of its dark matter halo e.g., Haehnelt:1998,Bosch:2000,Zhang:2000 . As a consequence of this bulge--halo connection and the known M_ BH - _0 relation, there should be an M_ DM - _0 correlation. Combining our v_ max - _0 relation (Equation ( eqn:log_v-log_sigma )) with Equation ( eqn:conversion ) yields rCl M_ DM M_ & = & (1.58 0.29) _0 141 km s^ 1 && + (12.03 0.12). Additional uncertainty is introduced through the propagation of errors Error propagation calculations were performed with the python package uncertainties ( http://pythonhosted.org/uncertainties/ ). when Equations ( eqn:log_v-log_sigma ) and ( eqn:conversion ) are combined, however, Equation ( eqn:M_DM-sigma ) provides a useful method of quickly estimating M_ DM in spiral galaxies with bulges when only _0 measurements are available. Zahid:2016 examine the _0 - M_ *,tot relation for massive ( M_ *,tot 10^ 10.3 M_ ) quiescent galaxies at z<0.7 . They find that _0 M_ *,tot ^ 0.3 , which is the same as the scaling relation between the dark matter halo velocity dispersion and halo mass (i.e., _ DM M_ DM ^ 0.3 ) obtained by N body simulations Evrard:2008,Posti:2014 . We can compare our result by rearranging Equation ( eqn:M_DM-sigma ) to show that _0 M_ DM ^ 0.63 0.11 for late-type galaxies has twice the slope derived by Zahid:2016 for early-type galaxies. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first time that the relationship between the mass of the dark matter halo and the central stellar velocity dispersion has been derived or presented for spiral galaxies, although we caution against its extrapolation to late-type ( Sc) spiral galaxies with _0 100 km s^ 1 that potentially are overinfluenced by the disk kinematics. Alternatively, combining Equation ( eqn:log_v-log_sigma_alt ) with Equation ( eqn:conversion ) yields rCl M_ DM M_ & = & (0.74 0.26) _0 141 km s^ 1 && + (12.03 0.12). The M_ BH - v_ max Relation Here, we derive the M_ BH - v_ max scaling relation for the largest sample of spiral galaxies with directly measured black hole masses to date. Our (v_ max , M_ BH ) dataset, consisting of 42 galaxies, can be described by = 0.74 , p=2.24 10^ 8 , r_s=0.72 , and p_s=6.70 10^ 8 . The bces bisector regression yields the relation rCl M_ BH M_ & = & (10.62 1.37) v_ max 210 km s^ 1 && + (7.22 0.09), with _ rms = 0.58 dex and =0.45 dex , both in the M_ BH direction (see Figure fig:M_BH-log_v ). These levels of scatter place the M_ BH - v_ max relation on par with the scatter ( _ rms = 0.63 dex and =0.58 dex ) about the M_ BH - _0 relation for spiral galaxies Davis:2017 . The slope for the M_ BH - v_ max relation is significantly steeper than the slopes presented in previous works. However, the steep slope is in good agreement with the prediction from Equation ( eqn:pred ), consistent at the level of 0.46 , which both confirms that our M_ BH - v_ max relation is in agreement with the Tully--Fisher relation and that the M_ BH - M_ *,tot relation must have the steep slope of 3 reported by Davis:2018 . For reference, the M_ BH -(stellar bulge mass) relation for spiral galaxies has a slope of 2.44_ 0.31 ^ +0.35 Davis:2019 . We do not find barred galaxies to be offset in our M_ BH - v_ max relation (see Figure fig:M_BH-log_v ); the bulk of our galaxies have barred morphologies (35 out of 42). Courteau:2003 found that barred galaxies are not offset in the Tully--Fisher relation, reinforcing our finding that v_ max appears to predict black hole masses equally well for barred and nonbarred galaxies. In a highly influential work, Ostriker:1973 concluded from their simulations of galaxies that massive halos would act to stabilize disks and suppress bar instabilities; thus, bars would preferentially form in galaxies with low-concentration halos. However, we now know that this is wrong. Bosma:1996 remark on the stabilizing influence of a bulge, and Sellwood:2001 find that high central rates of shear can stabilize a disk regardless of the dark matter halo density. In fact, the halo does not stabilize the disk, and even in some cases destabilizes it by increasing the bar instability Athanassoula:2002 . That is, a single M_ *,tot - v_ max Tully--Fisher relation for spiral galaxies combined with a single M_ BH - M_ *,tot relation for spiral galaxies yields a single M_ BH - v_ max relation for spiral galaxies. Based on the behavior of four spiral galaxies, Ferrarese:2002 postulated that halos with M_ DM 5 10^ 11 M_ , i.e., (M_ DM /M_ ) 11.70 , are progressively less efficient or perhaps incapable of forming SMBHs. Alternatively, Wassenhove:2010 find that a few percent of their simulated galaxies with massive black holes encounter stripping during their evolution, which leaves them ``naked,'' having lost most of their surrounding dark matter halos. As can be seen in Figure fig:M_BH-log_v , three of our galaxies (Circinus, NGC 1566, and NGC 4395, two of which were outliers in Figure fig:v-sigma ) have (M_ DM /M_ ) < 11.70 (i.e., v_ max < 159 km s ^ 1 from Equation ( eqn:conversion )). Our results show that 5 10^ 11 M_ is not a hard-and-fast threshold; however, given that our uncertainties on M_ DM are greater than 0.1 dex (see Table table:Sample , Column (9)), our smallest dark matter halos are only marginally below this limit. For example, based on v_ max =98 7 km s^ 1 , we predict that NGC 1566 has a quite low (M_ DM /M_ )=11.67 0.15 . However, because NGC 1566 is a Type I Seyfert galaxy Alloin:1985,Oknyansky:2019 , it is hard to imagine it not possessing an SMBH. If we track the location of the six outliers from Figure fig:v-sigma to Figure fig:M_BH-log_v , we find that the locations of NGC 613, NGC 1300, and NGC 4151 suggest that their maximum rotational velocities are anomalous, rather than their central stellar velocity dispersions or black hole masses.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 0613 as an optically unidentified mid-infrared AGN whose line ratios and face-on host suggest little host extinction.

quotes.quote: Ten of our mid-IR classified AGNs are not unambiguously identified as AGNs at optical wavelengths: five are classified as HII galaxies (NGC 0613, NGC 1448, NGC 1792, NGC 4945, and NGC 5128), four are classified as LINERS (NGC 0660, NGC 3627, NGC 3628, and NGC 5195), and one does not have good-quality optical spectroscopic data (ESO121-G006). In Fig. fig_oiii_nev a, we show that three of the optically unidentified AGNs (NGC 0613, NGC 3627 and NGC 5195) are within the intrinsic scatter of the [ OIII ] [ NeV ] relation (shaded region), and consequently do not appear to be deficient in [ OIII ] flux. Furthermore, three of the optically unidentified AGNs are found to be hosted in relatively face-on galaxies (NGC 0613, NGC 3627 and NGC 5195), similar to that of the optically identified AGNs, suggesting there is little extinction from the host galaxy towards these AGNs.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 613's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 613 is a late-type spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 26.6 5.3 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with an active nucleus classified as an AGN/starburst composite veron_agns_1997 and a surrounding elliptical starburst ring (major axis 7 900 pc; PA 110 ; hummel_central_1992 ). It shows a complex and radio morphology, possibly caused by an AGN ( hummel_central_1987 ; see also boker_sinfoni_2008 ). In addition, nuclear water maser emission was detected kondratko_discovery_2006 .

sources.summary: The paper favors an AGN in NGC 613, citing a heavily obscured Compton-thin X-ray spectrum, MIR-X-ray agreement, and compact MIR morphology despite circumnuclear star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 613 The AGN candidate in NGC 613 has been observed with and shows a heavily obscured but Compton-thin X-ray spectrum with an intrinsic luminosity of = 41.55 . The predicted value from the MIR--X-ray correlation is in good agreement, as well as the predicted X-ray column density. Together with the compact MIR morphology at subarcsecond scales, this strongly favours the presence of an AGN in NGC 613 in addition to the circumnuclear star formation.

gmw_00000159.name: NGC 676
gmw_00000159.overview: NGC 676 is an edge-on spiral galaxy located at a distance of approximately 19.5 Mpc [arXiv:1310.2770]. The galaxy hosts a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus, which remains undetected at radio wavelengths [arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 676's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 676 is an edge-on spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 19.5 8.7 Mpc tully_nearby_1988 with a Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , which is undetected at radio wavelengths ho_detection_2001 . No observations are available for NGC 676. In the images, it appears elliptically elongated along the galaxy major axis.

gmw_00000160.name: NGC 788
gmw_00000160.overview: NGC 788 is a spiral galaxy hosting a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2103.08736, arXiv:1310.2770] that displays a compact, core-dominated radio morphology [arXiv:2005.08569] and a highly absorbed X-ray spectrum [arXiv:2111.14925]. Its central region is dominated by intermediate-age stars [arXiv:2203.07484], while its ionized gas traces a bipolar outflow [arXiv:2103.08736].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 788 as a Seyfert 2 spiral whose ionized gas traces the AGN ionization structure, with extended NE-SW emission and compact coronal emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 788 NGC 788 is a spiral galaxy (SA0/a?(s)) located at 56 Mpc and hosts a Sy 2 nucleus. The stellar velocity field is described by a rotation pattern, with the orientation of the line of the nodes _0 120^ and smaller ( 50-80 km s ^ 1 ) stellar velocity dispersion patches are observed at 250 pc, probably due to intermediate age stellar populations rogemar_stellar . The radial profile for the ionised gas mass is more concentrated in the centre ( 100 pc) and has a steeper distribution than that of the hot molecular gas rogemar_sample . By mapping the gas distribution, astor19 show that Pa and 1.2570 m emission are extended along the northeast--southwest direction and present higher ( 150 km s ^ 1 ) velocity amplitudes and regions of 150 km s ^ 1 \ in the same direction, interpreted as a bipolar outflow.

The ionised gas emission is likely tracing the AGN ionisation structure. We find that the Pa emission is extended mostly along PA=52^ 4^ , which is displaced from stellar kinematic PA 120^ 3^ ; rogemar_stellar by 68^ . The highest intensity levels in the H _2 flux map are more extended along the N--S direction, while at lower flux levels, the H _2 emission spreads over the whole NIFS FoV. The \ is compact and slightly elongated along the AGN ionisation axis, as traced by the orientation of the Pa emission.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 788 with added soft-emission Gaussian lines and finds degenerate torus solutions; despite an upward N_H trend, it classifies the source as non-variable in N_H.

quotes.quote: NGC 788 Data reduction/fitting: Three Gaussian lines ( zgauss in xspec ) were added to model the source soft emission. The reduced ^2 showed significant improvement for all models, justifying this decision (1.24 to 1.13 for , 1.27 to 1.13 for and 1.29 to 1.17 for ).

For this source we present two borus02 configurations that can explain the data with the same goodness of fit. The two configurations can be described as a low- N_ H,av scenario and a high- N_ H,av one. The former is statistically preferred by MYTorus , which cannot reproduce the latter without forcing N_ H,av to stay at a very high value. UXCLUMPY , while not directly comparable (it does not provide a value for N_ H,av ), results in values of N_ H,los that are more similar to those of the high- N_ H,av borus02 option. Given how the first configuration is practically identical to the MYTorus results, we opt to show the second borus02 configuration (the high- N_ H,av scenario) in all plots regarding the source. The degeneracy between the reflection and line-of-sight component modeling results in different estimates for N_ H,los for each model, although the upwards trend of N_ H,los vs time is maintained (see Fig. fig:nhvstime1 ).
However, given how the ^2_ red comparison doesn't show a significant preference for N_ H,los variability over intrinsic flux variability, we classify this source as `Non-variable in N_ H,los '

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 788 as a bright Seyfert 2 test case, describing its high-S/N, absorbed, reflection-rich X-ray spectrum and showing the Bayesian analysis agrees with full-spectrum and literature fits.

quotes.quote: The second spectrum, from the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 788 (3XLSS J020106.4-064859, exposure time 10.0 ks), has a much higher S/N (41) and shows many more spectral features, for example, a highly absorbed primary power law and a strong reflection with an iron emission line, as shown in the bottom panel of Fig. fig:realfit . This object is the brightest point source in the XXL north field Chiappetti2018 , with a 2--10 keV flux of 1.4 10^ 12 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 . For this source, it is necessary to apply the full model (Sect. full model ), and each free parameter can be well fit, with the exception of the soft excess, which is completely absorbed This object even requires two apec at different temperatures because its S/N is very high. .

Ricci2017 used a model (B3) very similar to our full model to fit the spectra of NGC 788. They found in general comparable results with our fitted values, for instance, (N_ H )=23.77 0.05 , which agrees in general with our results. On the other hand, they find =1.64 0.26 , which is slightly lower than our fitted values. However, this difference can be explained by the fact that has been determined using a fit that also makes use of Swift/BAT data and a free cutoff energy that is found to be around 70 keV.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 788 has rotating stellar kinematics and low-sigma rings or partial rings, interpreted as young or intermediate-age stellar populations.

quotes.quote: A rotating disk pattern is recognized in the LOS velocity field for all galaxies, with a straight zero velocity line for most of them. For two galaxies -- Mrk 1066 m1066_kin and NGC 5899 (Fig. n5899 ) -- an S shape zero velocity line is observed, which is a signature of the presence of a nuclear bar or spiral arms in these galaxies e.g. combes95,emsellem06 . For most galaxies, the maximum amplitude of the rotation curve is expected to be observed outside of the NIFS FoV. The rotation disk signature is clearly seen in the one one-dimensional plots shown in Fig. vcuts , obtained by averaging the velocity and values within a pseudo-slit with width of 0 25 oriented along the major axis of the galaxy. In addition, distinct structures of low- values ( 50-80 ) are seen in the the maps: low rings or partial rings are seen for Mrk 1066, Mrk 1157, NGC 5929 and NGC 788.

The velocity dispersion maps show low- ( 50-80 ) rings for 4 galaxies (Mrk 1066, Mrk 1157, NGC 5929 and NGC 788) or ``patches" of low- structures (for Mrk 607, NGC 2110, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 4235 and NGC 5899) at typical distances of 200 pc, interpreted as being originated in young/intermediate age stellar populations.

sources.summary: The paper maps ionized and molecular gas in NGC 788, finding extended emission, atypically perpendicular H2 structure, smaller H2 rotation amplitude, and east-west outflow signatures.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We have used the Gemini Near-Infrared Integral Field Spectrograph (NIFS) in the J and K bands to map the distribution, excitation and kinematics of the ionized H ii and warm molecular gas H _2 , in the inner few 100 pc of 6 nearby active galaxies: NGC 788, Mrk 607, NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 5506, NGC 5899.

The flux distributions for the selected emission lines cover most of the FOV, corresponding to maximum distances from the nucleus varying from 100 pc for NGC 3227 to 410 pc for NGC 788. The direction of the largest extent of the [ Fe ii ] 1.25 m and Pa flux distributions follows the orientation of the ionization axis, and which in many cases approximately coincides with the orientation of the major axis of the galaxy in our sample. Exceptions are the cases of NGC 788 where they are oriented at a small angle relative to the major axis, and NGC 5506, where the largest extent of the [ Fe ii ] emission is perpendicular to the major axis. Although also showing the highest emission levels following the major axis orientation, the H _2 2.12 m flux maps usually show emission more spread over all directions, being less collimated than the ionized gas emission. The only exception is NGC 788, for which the strongest H _2 emission is more extended perpendicularly to the major axis of the galaxy.

The H _2 velocity amplitudes is similar to those also seen in the [ Fe ii ] and Pa velocity fields, except in the case of NGC 788, for which the H _2 rotation amplitude is smaller. The rotation pattern can also be seen in the [ Fe ii ] and Pa velocity fields, but in these cases it is usually disturbed -- more in the case of [ Fe ii ] than in the case of Pa - indicating the presence of additional kinematic components, usually associated with enhanced values. Velocity dispersion maps These maps are shown in the third line of Figs. ngc788 - ngc5899 . The [ Fe ii ] maps usually show the highest values, reaching up to 150--300 km s ^ 1 , while the H _2 maps show the lowest values, in the range 40--90 km s ^ 1 . The Pa maps are similar to those of [ Fe ii ] although reaching somewhat overall lower values, in the range 40 km s ^ 1 to 250 km s ^ 1 . An exception is the case of NGC 5506 that shows regions of enhanced to 350 km s ^ 1 ) for all emission lines, extended perpendicularly to the major axis for [ Fe ii ] and Pa and in patches parallel to the major axis in H _2 ; such patches are also seen in [ Fe ii ] . h_3 and h_4 Gauss-Hermite moments The h_3 and h_4 maps show values ranging from --0.3 to 0.3 for all emission lines. An inverse correlation between the h_3 map and the velocity fields is observed for all emission-lines: positive values of h_3 are seen at the locations where negative velocities are observed in the velocity fields, while negative values are seen where positive velocities are observed. This means that red wings are observed in blueshifted emission lines, while blue wings are observed in redshifted emission lines. We also observe in some cases (e.g. for the three emission lines in NGC 3227 and some of the lines in the other galaxies) an inverse correlation between the h_4 and the velocity dispersion maps. Positive values and highest values of h_4 are seen at the locations showing the lowest velocity dispersion values, while values zero or most negative ones are seen in the regions with the highest velocity dispersion. Total gas mass and surface mass density distributions The measurement of gas masses and surface mass densities within the inner few hundred pc of the host galaxies of AGN can be used to evaluate the gas reservoir available to trigger and maintain the nuclear activity, as well as the formation of new stars in the circumnuclear region. The presence of recent star formation in the circumnuclear region of active galaxies has been evidenced via the observation of low stellar velocity dispersion structures (Riffel et al. 2017) and their association with young stars e.g. storchi2012 . We can also use gas mass estimates in the vicinity of AGN to look for a possible correlation between the amount of available gas and the power of the AGN. We have calculated the ionized gas masses, in units of solar masses (M _ ) using the following expression e.g. scoville,riffel08,storchi2009,astor2014,astor2017 : M_ HII 5.1 10^ 16 F_ Pa erg s^ 1 cm^ 2 D Mpc ^2 [ M_ ] , where F_ Pa is the flux in the Pa line and D is the distance to the galaxy. This equation was obtained from scoville assuming an electron temperature of T=10^4 K, electron density of N_e=100 cm ^ 3 and case B recombination osterbrock , values applicable to the inner kiloparsec of active galaxies. The flux of Br used in the original equation was replaced by the stronger Pa one assuming the theoretical ratio F_ Pa / F_ Br =5.88 osterbrock . For NGC 5506, we did use the flux of Br emission line instead of Pa , as the signal to noise in the J-band spectrum is worse than in the K-band one, particularly in the extranuclear regions. The mass of the warm molecular gas (in M _ ) was obtained using scoville : M_ H_2 5.0776 10^ 13 F_ H_ 2 2.1218 erg s^ 1 cm^ 2 D Mpc ^2 [ M_ ] , where F_ H_ 2 2.1218 is the flux for the corresponding emission line. In the derivation of the above equation by scoville it was assumed that the vibrational temperature is T=2000 K, indicating a thermalized gas which is valid for n_ H_2 >10^ 4.5 cm ^ 3 . Previous studies by members of our group have found that the assumption of thermal equilibrium is indeed applicable for nearby AGNs and the derived temperatures are very close to the above value storchi2009,diniz2015 . Uncertainties in the derived mass values were obtained considering that the mass derived from both Eq. mhii and mh2 are directly proportional to the line fluxes. All the mass uncertainties are thus lower than 30 , as we have masked out regions with flux uncertainties larger than this value.
