The actual uncertainties may be somewhat higher due to further uncertainties in the absolute flux calibration. In addition, we cannot derive precise physical parameters such as gas density and temperature, thus, we have to rely on the above assumptions even though they are just ``educated estimates" based on our previous similar studies. We show the surface mass density distributions of ionized and warm molecular gas -- _ HII and _ H_2 , respectively -- in the first two columns of panels of Fig. mass , and the corresponding azymuthally averaged profiles in the third column, in units of solar masses per parsec square. The surface mass densities were obtained by calculating the gas masses in each spaxel using the equations above and dividing them by the surface area of each spaxel, taking into account projection effects via the use of the disk inclinations quoted in riffel2018 . The resulting ionized gas surface mass density distributions are in some cases more compact when compared with that of the warm molecular gas, that seems to extend farther and more uniformly from the nucleus than the ionized gas. The azimuthaly-averaged profiles of _ HII and _ H_2 , shown in the last column of Fig. mass , reveal that typical ratios between the two surface mass densities within the inner 100 pc are of the order of 10 ^3 and that gradients are steeper for the ionized than for the molecular gas. In Table tabmass we show the integrated gas mass values (for the whole FOV) as well as the average surface mass densities (in units of M _ pc ^ 2 ) obtained as the ratio between the integrated masses and the area over which they are distributed, listed also in the Table. We also include in Table tabmass an estimate of the cold molecular gas mass. A number of studies have derived the ratio between the cold and warm H _2 gas masses by comparing the masses obtained using the cold CO molecular lines observed in millimetric wavelenghts with that of the warm H _2 observed in the near-IR. dale05 obtained ratios in the range 10^5 -10 ^7 , while ms2006 , using a larger sample of 16 luminous and ultra-luminous infrared galaxies, derived a ratio M _ cold /M _ warm = 1 - 5 10^6 . More recently, ma2013 compiled from the literature values of M _ cold derived from CO observations and H _2 (2.12 m) luminosities for a larger number of galaxies, covering a wider range of luminosities, morphological and nuclear activity types. From those data, they propose that an estimate of the cold H _2 gas mass can be obtained from the flux of the warm H _2 2.1218 line as: M_ H_2 cold 1174 L_ H_2 2.1218 L_ , where L_ H_2 2.1218 is the luminosity of the H _2 (2.12 m) line. The error associated to the factor = 1174 is 35 , according to ma2013 . Table tabmass shows that the integrated mass of warm H _2 gas (within typically 100 -- 400 pc from the nucleus) ranges from 106 M _ for Mrk 607 to 820 M _ for NGC 5506, while the estimated mass of cold molecular gas ranges from 8 10 ^7 M _ for Mrk 607 to 6 10 ^ 8 M _ for NGC 5506. The masses of ionized gas range from 1.8 10 ^5 M _ for NGC 3516 to 1.9 10 ^7 M _ for NGC 5506. We point out again that the uncertainties in the calculated masses, presented in Tab. tabmass , were obtained only by propagating the line flux uncertainties and are thus lower limits, considering that there are additional uncertainties in adopted physical properties -- e.g. on the M_ H_2 cold /M_ H_2 ratio, H _2 vibrational temperatures and electron densities. In this section we discuss individually each galaxy presenting a brief review of previous results from the literature, trying to relate them with ours . We also discuss and analyze the masses of ionized and molecular gas, as well as their surface mass density distributions and the global properties of the gas kinematics, leaving the kinematic modelling and the analysis of the gas kinematics and excitation to Paper B . NGC 788 NGC 788 (Fig. ngc788 ) was identified as a Seyfert galaxy by huchra1982 , and has been observed in the optical hamuy1987,wagner1988,kay1994,cruz1994 , radio ulvestad1989 , and millimetre heckman1989 wavebands.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 788 as a type 2 Seyfert with a VLA radio counterpart, a point-like hard X-ray source, and no detected inter-observation variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 788 This galaxy was optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert by . A radio counterpart was detected with VLA data nagar1999 . At X-rays, it was classified as a Compton thin candidate using ASCA data derosa2012 , and shows a point-like source in the 4.5-8 keV energy band (see Appendix multiimages ). It was observed once with Chandra in 2009 and once with XMM -Newton in 2010. Long term variability analyses of this source were not found in the literature.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 788 has young components mainly in the outer field, a central 500-700 Myr stellar population, older stars throughout, and nuclear hot dust.

quotes.quote: NGC 788: The light of this galaxy shows an important contribution of the two younger ( xy and xyo ) components with a maximum value of 40 percent. The radial profiles show that these two components only show up in the outer parts of the field of view (FoV). It is, however, worth mentioning that there is a ``stripe'' on the east side, that can be due to an instrumental fingerprint (not completely removed in the data reduction process), therefore this should just be taken as a trend. It is clear from these maps that the light of the central region of this galaxy is dominated by stars with ages in the range 500 Myr t 700 Myr ( xiy ) with contributions > 40 percent over the FoV. A fraction of 25 percent of older stars ( xio and xo stellar population component [ SPC ] ) are detected throughout the whole FoV.

sources.summary: The paper groups NGC 788 with Sy2 sources lacking absorption variability, favoring smooth gas and clumpy dust in a geometrically thick torus viewed at large inclination.

quotes.quote: Panel 6 of Figure fig:cartoon_Sy2 (e.g. ESO 138-G1, PKS 2356-61, and NGC 788): Sy2 sources without absorption variability, large covering factors Large covering factor is found in PKS 2356-61 if it is fitted to clumpy torus at mid-IR, although the smooth model is equally good from the statistical point of view. , and intermediate or high half-opening angles of both gas and dusty structures. The gas is smoothly distributed while the dust is located within clumps. A geometrically-thick torus of dust and gas is able to explain the observed characteristics of these three objects, where the dust is located in clumps while the gas is smoothly distributed. Interestingly these objects are seen at relatively large inclination angles but, in spite of that, the large value of the half opening angle of the torus prevents a direct view of the accretion disk.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 788's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 788 is a low-inclination spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0136 ( D 57.2 Mpc) with a Sy 2 nucleus with polarized broad emission lines kay_hidden_1998 . The AGN was discovered by and belongs to the nine-month BAT AGN sample.. At radio wavelengths the nucleus is slightly elongated (PA 62 ; nagar_radio_1999 ).

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC788 with earlier VLA and 22 GHz work and finds a compact, core-dominated morphology with a borderline flat-to-steep spectral index.

quotes.quote: NGC788 UlvestadWilson1989 studied it with VLA at 6 and 20 cm ( 1 arcsec resolution), reporting an unresolved morphology, fluxes of 1-2 and a borderline spectral index between steep and flat ( 0.47), see also NagarWilsonMulchaeyGallimore1999 . With 1" 22 GHz observation, Smith2020 observe an slightly extended morphology, interpreted as due to star formation, with flux density of 0.61 . Our 1 arcsec resolution maps reveal a compact, core-dominated morphology, with a 22 GHz flux density of 0.87 0.06, higher respect to Smith2020 , and a spectral index of = 0.4 0.2, compatible with that derived by Ulvestad Wilson at similar resolution.

gmw_00000161.name: NGC 985
gmw_00000161.overview: NGC 985 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1810.10732, arXiv:1511.07169] with a distinctive ring-shaped structure [arXiv:1511.07169, arXiv:2107.05676] and a double nucleus [arXiv:1810.10732, arXiv:1409.1244] resulting from a recent merger [arXiv:1511.07169, arXiv:2107.05676]. The active nucleus drives persistent outflows [arXiv:1511.07169, arXiv:2107.05676] and exhibits recurrent soft X-ray obscuration events [arXiv:1511.07169, arXiv:2107.05676].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 985 as a merging Seyfert with a double nucleus and models its compact mm excess to estimate coronal size, magnetic field, and electron index.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The emission center of the NGC 985 nucleus is located at = 02h34m37.84028s 0.00244^ , = -08^ 47 ^ 16.07808 ^ 0.00173^ (J2000) at 150 GHz.

quotes.quote: NGC 985 is a southern Seyfert 1 galaxy at z=0.0427 Fisher1995 whose black hole mass is 2.2 10^8 M_ kim2008 . NGC 985 is a peculiar galaxy with a prominent ring-shaped zone at several kiloparsecs from the nucleus deVaucouleurs1975 , suggesting that the galaxy is under- going a merging process. The galaxy contains a double nucleus, Seyfert nucleus, and non-AGN PerezGarcia1996 .

The right panel of Figure. fig:SED_radio shows the cm-mm radio spectrum of NGC 985 after subtracting extended galactic components, same as in IC 4329A. Again, the mm excess is clearly seen. We fit the spectrum with the same models for IC 4329A. For the X-ray coronal properties, we adopt the coronal temperature and the optical depth due to Thomson scattering of 29 keV and 3.5, respectively (see Section. app:xray ). The coronal properties of NGC 985 are estimated as (R, B, p) = (47 21 r_s, 4 2 G , 2.1 0.3) , setting =0.04 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 985 as a ring-galaxy merger with a double nucleus and AGN, plus an ISO-detected MIR dust arc possibly tracing an AGN-driven shock.

quotes.quote: The galaxy NGC 985 NGC 985 is a ring galaxy with a bright ``knot'' embedded in the outer ring (Rodrigues-Espinosa Stanga 1990). Optical and IR observations show that the ``knot'' is composed of two nuclei, a Seyfert-1 nucleus, and a second ``spiral bulge'' 3 arcsecs to the north-west of the Seyfert nucleus (Perez-Garcia Rodrigues-Espinosa 1996, Appleton Marcum 1993). This has led to the conclusion that NGC 985 consists of two galaxies undergoing a merger; the AGN lying at the center of the nucleus of one galaxy, and the second nucleus corresponding to the other. If one of the nuclei is the original nucleus of the ``target'' galaxy, then it must be significantly displaced from the ring center. This suggests that we are viewing the early stages of a highly off-center merging collision, since dynamical friction would quickly center the collision if we were witnessing the event at a later time.

In NGC 985, a dust arc is discovered in the MIR on the kpc--scale near the Seyfert 2 nucleus. This feature, which has not been seen at shorter wavelengths, may represent a shock wave driven into neutral or molecular gas by the AGN wind. The geometrical asymmetry of the "arc" might be expected on the grounds of asymmetric infall which is predicted in highly off-center ring galaxy collision models.

sources.summary: The paper monitors Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 985, finding transient soft X-ray obscuration, fast broad UV absorption, persistent outflows, and a ring-shaped structure from a recent merger.

quotes.quote: We observed the Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 985 on multiple occasions to search for variability in its UV and X-ray absorption features in order to establish their location and physical properties. We use to obtain X-ray spectra using the EPIC-pn camera, and the Cosmic Origins Spectrograph (COS) on the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) to obtain UV spectra. Our observations are simultaneous and span timescales of days to years. We find that soft X-ray obscuration that absorbed the low energy continuum of NGC 985 in August 2013 diminished greatly by January 2015.

NGC 985 (also known as Mrk 1048), may be another case of a recorded eclipsing event. It is a nearby z = 0.043 ( Fis95 ) Seyfert 1 galaxy which shows a distinctive ring-shaped structure, testimony of a recent merger event. NGC 985 also shows a persistent outflow in both the X-ray and the UV, revealed by a multi-component warm absorber (WA) in the X-ray, and blue-shifted absorption troughs in both wavebands ( Arav02a ; Kro05,Kro09 ).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 985 as a ring-shaped Seyfert 1 merger remnant with recurrent obscuration, multi-component warm absorbers, and outflows capable of AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: NGC 985, also known as Mrk 1048, is a nearby ( z = 0.043 , Fis95 ), bright ( F_ 0.3-10 keV = 2.2 10^ 11 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , Ebr16 ) Seyfert 1 galaxy with a distinctive ring shape, produced by a recent galactic merger. This source is known to host a WA in X-rays ( Kro05,Kro09 ) as well as an UV absorber ( Ara02 ).

NGC 985 experienced a low soft X-ray flux state in 2013 which was attributed to the passing by of an obscuring cloud of material across our line of sight ( Par14 ). The analysis of the EPIC-pn spectra of NGC 985 revealed that this obscuration event was possibly recurrent since there were signatures of obscuration in 2003, followed by an unobscured period in 2007-2008 (according to monitoring), before entering in the obscuration event of 2013. Interestingly, the analysis of the 2015 observations showed that some hints of obscuration were still present in the X-ray and UV spectra of NGC 985 ( Ebr16 ).

This would mean that this outflow by itself might be carrying enough mass and energy to significantly disrupt the ISM of NGC 985, as it is within the range 0.5 5 of the bolometric luminosity that is required by the models to be fed back into the medium to reproduce the observed M - relation (see HE10 , and references therein).

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 1048/NGC 985 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy with a ring-like merger morphology, warm absorption, and a gas-rich double nucleus that may feed AGN absorption.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1048 (also known as NGC 985) is a Seyfert 1 galaxy VeronCetty83 at redshift 0.0427. The galaxy itself has a clear ring structure, which suggests that it is going through a merger deVauc75 . The presence of warm absorption in the X-ray spectrum of this source is well known, and was first suggested based on ROSAT observations by Brandt94 .

Mrk 1048 has a very unusual morphology, with a prominent ring structure deVauc75 and a gas-rich double nucleus PerezGarcia96 . It is interesting to speculate that this remarkable morphology and recent merger may be responsible for absorption of the AGN, by allowing cold gas to fall in towards the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 985 as another Seyfert with a hard observed electron spectral index, uncertain radio components, and a need for further observations of its radio spectrum.

quotes.quote: For the other object, NGC 985, the observed electron spectral index is 2.11 0.28 Inoue2018 , which is hard considering the radiative cooling effect. Cascade components would have such a hard spectrum below the threshold energy see, e.g., 2003APh....19..525A . In addition, due to the quality of data at low frequencies, we could not precisely determine the other components such as free-free emission and synchrotron emission from star formation activity, and synchrotron emission from the jet. Those uncertainties may resulted in a less reliable measurement of the corona emission spectrum slope. Further observations are required to determine the radio spectral properties in NGC 985 precisely.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC985 as a Seyfert-nucleus ring galaxy whose ionized emission traces only the western ring segment, with no nearby companion identified.

quotes.quote: NGC985: The nucleus of this ring galaxy is known for its Seyfert activity rod90 , and is bright on both the \ and continuum images. The \ emission traces only the western segment of the ring. No companion can be identified within a few disk diameters of this galaxy.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 985's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 985 is a peculiar ring-like late-stage merger system, at a redshift of z= 0.0431 ( D 195 Mpc). The two nuclei have a separation of 3.5 ( 3 kpc; PA 110 ; appleton_infrared_1993 ). The eastern nucleus is classified a Sy 1.5 veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 , belonging to the nine-month BAT AGN sample.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 985 as a HILIGT spectral-variability example, showing its Seyfert I X-ray spectrum hardened in 2013 as soft flux diminished.

quotes.quote: In Fig.  fig:lc_ngc985 we show the light curve of the Seyfert I galaxy, NGC 985 in the 0.2--2 and 2--12 keV bands. It can be seen that the source spectrum hardened considerably in 2013, due to the soft flux diminishing while the hard flux remained stable at historical levels. This spectral change has been attributed to the development of a multi-component warm absorber around 2013 which preferentially absorbed the soft X-rays Ebrero16 .

sources.summary: The paper reports that MKN 1048 has a simple power-law GIS spectrum with an unusually low photon index for quasars, a marginal iron-line detection, and a measured 2-10 keV flux.

quotes.quote: MKN 1048: The GIS spectrum of MKN 1048 is well fitted by a simple powerlaw with photon index =1.60^ +0.02 _ 0.01 , a value significantly lower than the average photon index found in the X-ray spectra of quasars. The K _ iron line is marginally detected, at a 2 level (best fit equivalent width EW=96 _ 83 ^ +86 eV. The measured 2-10 keV flux is F=9.9 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 .

gmw_00000162.name: NGC 1052
gmw_00000162.overview: NGC 1052 is a massive elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1907.08035, arXiv:2111.10036] and prototypical LINER [arXiv:1610.06243, arXiv:2204.02416] that hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2105.01682, arXiv:2403.03974]. The galaxy features two-sided radio jets [arXiv:1904.00106, arXiv:2004.02305] and exhibits tidal interactions with nearby dwarf companions [arXiv:2109.09778, arXiv:2211.05993].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1052 with flux-binned Swift spectra, finding a partial-coverer fit, variable photon index, and harder-when-brighter behavior possibly tied to absorption.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052 Three flux-binned spectra could be produced from the available Swift data of NGC 1052. We found that a single partial coverer provided a very good fit to the spectra. A fairly good fit is obtained when all the parameters are tied ( ^2_R = 1.11 ). However, allowing , the absorbing column or the covering fraction to produces a fit which is statistically better in each case ( f = 9.5 and p=1.3 10^ 4 , f = 3.5 and p=3.3 10^ 2 , f = 8.0 and p=4.7 10^ 4 respectively). The best fit is obtained when only is left free ( ^2_R = 1.01 ).

For the best-fitting model, in which the absorber is constant and is free to vary between flux-binned spectra, is anticorrelated with the flux. Furthermore, the same anticorrelation is seen in our spectral fits when the absorbing column is allowed to vary as well as the photon index. The values of found are between those found by Weaver1999 and by Hernandez-Garcia2013 = 1.37-1.70 for similar models, though Hernandez-Garcia2013 attribute the primary cause of spectral changes in NGC 1052 to variations in the absorbing column, not the photon index.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC1052 as an E4 galaxy and prototypical LINER with broad optical/NIR components, H2O megamaser emission, radio jets, and absorbed hard X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: NGC1052 is morphologically classified as an elliptical (E4) galaxy and it is the brightest member of the Cetus I cluster. It has long been considered one of the prototypical LINERs and, after the detection of a faint broad component in the H , it was classified as a LINER1.9 by . Moreover, a broad component in the H emission line (FWHM 2100 ) was detected also in polarization by . Furthermore, presented a NIR spectrum of NGC 1052, with prominent Pa and [ Fe II ] emission lines.

NGC 1052 shows H _ 2 O megamaser emission claussen98 and variability in radio and X-rays ( vermeulen03 ; hernandezgarcia13 ). Radio observations of NGC 1052 revealed a double sided jet emerging from the nucleus kellermann98 . The X-ray spectrum is flat with a high absorbing column density guainazzi00 and there is a clear indication for the presence of an unresolved nuclear source in the hard bands ( satyapal05 ). suggested that this object might be more similar to a Seyfert than to a LINER galaxy, from the X-ray point of view.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1052 has a centrally peaked velocity-dispersion map and an h3 map anti-correlated with the velocity field.

quotes.quote: Patches of low- are seen for Mrk 607, NGC 2110, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 4235 and NGC 5899, while three objects (NGC 1052, NGC 3227 and NGC 4388) show a centrally peaked distribution. These rings and partial rings are located at distances from the nucleus ranging between 150 and 250 pc. Nuclear values for each galaxy are presented in the last column of Table tab_mod , obtained by fitting the nuclear spectrum integrated within a circular aperture with radius of 75 pc. Most galaxies show low h_ 3 values ( 0.10 <h_ 3 < 0.10), suggesting that their LOSVDs are well represented by a Gaussian velocity distribution. Exceptions are Mrk 607, Mrk1066 and NGC 4051 that show h_ 3 values larger than 0.15. For 8 galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 2110, NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 5506, Mrk 607 and Mrk 1066), the h_ 3 map is anti-correlated with the velocity field, with positive h_ 3 values seen at locations where rotation velocities are observed in blueshifts and negative h_ 3 values associated to regions where the rotation velocities are observed in redshifts.

The velocity dispersion maps show low- ( 50-80 ) rings for 4 galaxies (Mrk 1066, Mrk 1157, NGC 5929 and NGC 788) or ``patches" of low- structures (for Mrk 607, NGC 2110, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 4235 and NGC 5899) at typical distances of 200 pc, interpreted as being originated in young/intermediate age stellar populations. Centrally peaked maps are observed for three galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 3227 and NGC 4388). The h_ 3 moment is anti-correlated with the velocity field for 8 galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 2110, NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 5506, Mrk 607 and Mrk 1066) -- positive h_ 3 values seen at locations where the velocity field shows blueshifts and h_ 3 <0 for locations where the velocity field shows redshifts. The presence of these wings are attributed to the contribution of stars from the galaxy bulge that present lower rotation velocities. The observed velocity fields are well reproduced by a rotating disk model, with deprojected velocity amplitudes in the range 60--340 .

sources.summary: The paper measures NGC 1052's globular-cluster kinematics and infers a high dark matter fraction and typical large halo, while noting tidal gas, old stars, AGN jets, and interactions.

quotes.quote: Here we present radial velocities for a sample of 77 globular clusters associated with NGC 1052 obtained on the Keck telescope. Their mean velocity and velocity dispersion are consistent with that of the host galaxy. Using a simple tracer mass estimator, we infer the enclosed dynamical mass and dark matter fraction of NGC 1052. Extrapolating our measurements with an NFW mass profile we infer a total halo mass of 6.2 ( 0.2) 10 ^ 12 M _ . This mass is fully consistent with that expected from the stellar mass--halo mass relation, suggesting that NGC 1052 has a normal dark matter halo mass (i.e. it is not deficient in dark matter in contrast to two ultra diffuse galaxies in the group).

Here we present radial velocities for 77 globular clusters associated with the brightest group elliptical NGC 1052. Data were obtained using the DEIMOS spectrograph on the Keck II telescope. We measure a GC system mean velocity of 1503 km/s and a velocity dispersion of 132 km/s. These values are consistent with that of NGC 1052 itself. Using the GCs as tracer particles we estimate a total dynamical mass within 40 kpc of 4.14 10 ^ 11 M _ (compared to a stellar mass of 10 ^ 11 M _ ) and hence a high dark matter fraction of 75 . Extrapolating our measurements to the virial radius, assuming an NFW profile, we estimate a halo mass of 6.2 ( 0.2) 10 ^ 12 M _ . This mass is fully consistent with that expected from the stellar mass--halo mass relation, suggesting that NGC 1052 hosts a typical, large dark matter halo.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 1052 as a prototypical variable LLAGN, using XMM-Newton, Suzaku, NuSTAR, and Chandra data to model absorption, reflection, and spectral variability.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we make a multi-epoch analysis of NGC 1052, one of the prototypical LLAGN, using XMM Newton, Suzaku and NuSTAR observations, taken from 2001 to 2017. This is the first time that results from NuSTAR observations are reported for NGC 1052. We use ancillary Chandra data to decontaminate the nuclear spectrum from circumnuclear contributors.

NGC 1052 is a well known variable source at X-rays, however no consensus has been achieved about what is causing these variations. Two possible scenarios have been proposed: i) an obscuring structure along the line of sight Hernandez-13 and ii) a variable accretion disc Connolly-16 . In this work, we find for the first time, variations in both obscuration throughout the hydrogen column density, N_H , and the shape of the energy distribution of photons, i.e. the photon index, .

NGC 1052 (RA (J2000)= 02:41:04.8 and Dec (J2000) = -08:15:21) has been observed with most of the current and previous X-ray missions (i.e., BeppoSAX , XMM Newton, Chandra , Suzaku , NuSTAR , RXTE , and Swift ). In this work, we use data from XMM Newton, Chandra , Suzaku and NuSTAR .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1052's low-luminosity AGN torus with multi-epoch X-ray spectra, finding high covering factor, moderate column density, variable absorption, and possible density gradients.

quotes.quote: We present a novel approach to multi-epoch broadband X-ray spectroscopy, anchored in the long-term average spectrum in the hard X-ray band, applied to the nearby, X-ray bright AGN in the galaxy NGC 1052. From spectral features due to X-ray reprocessing in the circumnuclear material, based on a simple, uniform-density torus X-ray reprocessing model, we find a covering factor of 80--100 and a globally averaged column density in the range (1-2) 10^ 23 . This closely matches the independently measured variable line-of-sight column density range, leading to a straightforward and self-consistent picture of the obscuring torus in NGC 1052, similar to several other AGN in recent literature. Comparing this X-ray-constrained torus model with measurements of spatially resolved sub-parsec absorption from radio observations, we find that it may be possible to account for both X-ray and radio data with a torus model featuring a steep density gradient along the axis of the relativistic jets. This provides a valuable direction for the development of improved physical models for the circumnuclear environment in NGC 1052 and potentially in a wider class of AGN.

In this paper, we focus on the properties of the obscuring torus in the bright, nearby AGN at the center of the galaxy NGC 1052. It exhibits a type 2 optical spectrum with broad lines observed in polarized light barth+1999-ngc1052 . Studying its emission across the electromagnetic spectrum, fernandezOntiveros+2019-ngc1052 estimated that its bolometric luminosity ( L_ bol ) and Eddington ratio ( _ Edd =L_ bol /L_ Edd ) are around 7 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 and 4 10^ 4 , respectively. The relatively low accretion rate classifies NGC 1052 as a low-luminosity AGN (LL AGN; ho-2008 ), although it has previously been found to share some characteristics of typically more luminous Seyfert galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC1052 as the massive central elliptical whose tidal field likely perturbs DF2 and DF4; its potential, proximity, and future distance constraint anchor the tidal analysis.

quotes.quote: The group's central massive elliptical NGC1052 is the most likely source of these tidal disturbances. The observed distortions imply that the galaxies have a low total mass or are very close to NGC1052.

The group's central elliptical NGC1052 represents the most likely perturber due to its high mass and relative proximity to NGC1052-DF2 and NGC1052-DF4 2001MNRAS.327.1004B,2020ApJ...895L...4D,2021ApJ...914L..12S . We will show later that the morphologies of the galaxies are consistent with this assumption. The galaxies have very similar tidal distortions, even though they are not close to each other on the sky. This similarity, shown in Section Sec:Results , implies a similar underlying tidal field -- as expected when NGC1052 is the perturber.

The next clear observational step to further our work would be an improved NGC1052 distance constraint. Just as 2021ApJ...914L..12S was able to cancel out systematics in the DF2-DF4 distance estimate, a similar measurement could be obtained for NGC1052 to achieve < 1 Mpc uncertainty.

sources.summary: The paper maps ionised and neutral gas in NGC 1052, finding a prototypical LINER with multiphase gas kinematics and a likely jet-powered ionised outflow.

quotes.quote: Our goal is to analyse the kinematics and ionisation mechanisms of the multi-phase gas of NGC 1052, the prototypical LINER, in order to detect and map ionised and neutral phases of the putative outflow. We obtained Very Large Telescope MUSE and Gran Telescopio Canarias MEGARA optical integral field spectroscopy data for NGC 1052. Besides stellar kinematics maps, by modelling spectral lines with multiple Gaussian components, we obtained flux, kinematic, and excitation maps of both ionised and neutral gas.

The stars are distributed in a dynamically hot disc (V/ 1.2), with a centrally peaked velocity dispersion map ( _ = 201 10 ) and large observed velocity amplitudes ( V = 167 19 ). The ionised gas, probed by the primary component is detected up to 30 ( 3.3 kpc) mostly in the polar direction with blue and red velocities ( V < 250 ). The velocity dispersion map shows a notable enhancement ( > 90 ) crossing the galaxy along the major axis of rotation in the central 10 . The secondary component has a bipolar morphology, velocity dispersion larger than 150 and velocities up to 660 .

Taking into account the energy and kinetic power of the outflow (1.3 0.9 10 ^ 53 erg and 8.8 3.5 10 ^ 40 erg s ^ 1 , respectively) as well as its alignment with both the jet and the cocoon, and that the gas is collisionally ionised (due to gas compression), we consider that the most likely power source of the outflow is the jet, although some contribution from the AGN is possible.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1052 as a bright elliptical LINER and radio-loud galaxy with lobes, nuclear variability, and steady reflection consistent with distant torus material.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052 NGC 1052 is the brightest elliptical galaxy in the Cetus I group. It was optically classified as a LINER by 1980Heckman , then it was classified as LINER type 1.9 1997Ho . 2018cazzoli proposed that this object presents signs of outflowing winds by using optical 2D spectra. This source is a radio loud galaxy 2007maoz . At 1.4 GHz Very Large Array (VLA) image of NGC 1052 shows a core-dominated radio structure, with only about 15 of the flux density in extended emission: there are two lobes spanning 3 kpc, possibly with hot spots 1984wrobel .

At X-rays, 2020osorio presented an extensive study of NGC 1052 using observations from Chandra , XMM-Newton , NuSTAR , and Suzaku . They reported variability in the nucleus and found variations both in the intrinsic continuum flux, photon index, and in the obscuration along the line of sight. The reflection component is a steady emission both in flux and shape, fully consistent with reflection in a distant structure, perhaps the torus.

sources.summary: The paper models DF2 formation through tidal stripping with dynamical friction, finding faster dark-matter deficiency but imperfect agreement with observed globular-cluster distributions.

quotes.quote: It has been shown that NGC 1052--DF2 can form due to this tidal scenario by N-body simulation; however, the dynamical friction has been ignored in the literature. We perform a self-consistent full N-body simulation to investigate the effect of dynamical friction on the formation scenario and compare it with the one without dynamical friction. We find that dynamical friction causes a dramatic decay of the satellite's orbit. This makes the orbital period shorter, and the mass of the satellite decreases more rapidly. As a result, the satellite galaxy suffering from dynamical friction becomes a DMDG in 7 8 , which is 2 3 earlier than that in the simulation without dynamical friction.

Although the distribution of the globular clusters (GCs) in our simulation does not fully agree with that of DF2, the current observations still have large uncertainties. Our result implies that DF2 can be formed by a more circular orbit within a Hubble time, and that DMDGs can be formed by this tidal scenario more often than previously thought.

Since DF2_Nature first found that NGC 1052--DF2 (hereafter DF2) is a dark matter-deficient galaxy (DMDG), its formation has been discussed enthusiastically. Right after the finding, the accuracy of the distance measurement was questioned by Trujillo2019 , but the latest measurement insists on D 22 based on 40 orbits of Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) Advanced Camera for Surveys ( ACS ) data Shen2021b . The stellar mass of DF2 is M_* 2 8 DF2_Nature , and its dynamical mass is estimated to be around M_ dyn 3 8 by several papers DF2_Nature, Danieli2019, Emsellem2019 Note that the measured dynamical masses have large errors ( 40 70 ). . The distribution of globular clusters (GCs) in DF2 has been controversial. There are about ten GCs around DF2 DF2_Nature, Shen2021a with peak luminosities around M_ 606 9 mag in F606W with the ACS Shen2021a , which is brighter than those in normal dwarf galaxies having the same order of stellar mass: M 7.5 mag Miller&Lotz2007, Jordan2007, Rejkuba2012, Saifollahi2022 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1052 as the high-S/N ReveaLLAGN target, a LINER LLAGN with low accretion, broad blueshifted high-ionization lines, a small-scale radio jet, and UV-weak SED.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Our NGC 1052 observations are centered at RA: 02:41:04.798, DEC: -08:15:20.75 taken from very-long-baseline interferometry measurements of the AGN Lambert2009 .

quotes.quote: In this paper, we report the first results from the ReveaLLAGN project based on the Mid-Infrared Instrument (MIRI) medium-resolution spectrometer (MRS) data from our first two targets, Sombrero (also known as M104 and NGC 4594) and NGC 1052. The overall properties of these galaxies are listed in Table table:galprop . Both galaxies are classified as LINERs based on their optical emission lines Heckman1980,Ho1997 , and their low AGN luminosities are due to their low Eddington ratios and accretion rates. In the context of the full ReveaLLAGN sample these two galaxies bracket the survey in terms of their mid-IR fluxes, and thus represent the expected highest (NGC 1052) and lowest (Sombrero) signal-to-noise ratios (S/N).

We find that the emission-line widths in both NGC 1052 and Sombrero increase with increasing ionization potential, with FWHM > 1000 km s ^ 1 for lines with ionization potential 50 eV. These lines are also significantly blue-shifted in both LLAGN.

Both Sombrero and NGC 1052 have well-studied AGN with small-scale radio jets that can create shocked emission which could contribute to the observed nuclear emission e.g., claussen1998,Hada2013,Molina2018,Fern2023 . Additionally, both galaxies' spectral energy distributions (SEDs) show a lack of emission in the UV relative to higher luminosity AGN Fern2023 , consistent with other LLAGN Ho2008 .

sources.summary: The paper reports extended ISO mid-infrared emission in NGC 1052, a de Vaucouleurs mid-IR profile, [CII] detection, prior HI detection, and low far-infrared-to-blue luminosity.

quotes.quote: With the exception of the 15 image of NGC 1052, the mid-IR emission from NGC 1052, NGC 1155, NGC 5866 and NGC 6958 at 4.5, 7 and 15 show extended emission. Mid-IR emission from NGC 1052, NGC 1155, and NGC 6958 follows a de Vaucouleurs profile. The ratio of 15/7 flux decreases with radius in these galaxies, approaching the values empirically observed for purely stellar systems.

All the galaxies are detected in the [ CII ] (158 ) line, and the S0s NGC 1155 and NGC 5866 are detected in the [ OI ] (63 ) line as well. Previous detections of neutral ISM are sparse: only NGC 1052 had been detected in \ and NGC 5866 in CO. The ISO-LWS observations of the [ CII ] line are more sensitive measures of cool, neutral ISM than \ and CO by about a factor of 10-100. Comparison of [ CII ] with H shows that [ CII ] does not arise from HII regions and therefore must arise in the neutral medium. Three of four early type galaxies, namely NGC 1052, NGC 6958 and NGC 5866, have low ratios of far-infrared to blue luminosity and show a lower , which is explained by postulating a softer radiation field from old stellar populations in early type galaxies, compared to spirals and irregulars, where young stars are present.

sources.summary: The paper presents VLBA observations of NGC 1052, finding double-sided jets, redshifted water masers, and evidence that masers trace a circumnuclear torus or disk near the active nucleus.

quotes.quote: We present multi-frequency simultaneous VLBA observations at 15, 22 and 43 GHz towards the nucleus of the nearby radio galaxy NGC 1052. These three continuum images reveal a double-sided jet structure, whose relative intensity ratios imply that the jet axis is oriented close to the sky plane. The steeply rising spectra at 15--43 GHz at the inner edges of the jets strongly suggest that synchrotron emission is absorbed by foreground thermal plasma.

We detected H _2 O maser emission in the velocity range of 1550--1850 km s ^ 1 , which is redshifted by 50--350 km s ^ 1 with respect to the systemic velocity of NGC 1052. The redshifted maser gas appears projected against both sides of the jet, similar to the HI seen in absorption. The H _2 O maser gas is located where the free-free absorption opacity is large. This probably implies that the masers in NGC 1052 are associated with a circumnuclear torus or disk as in the nucleus of NGC 4258.

sources.summary: NGC 1052 was observed with Chandra and XMM-Newton; the paper finds long-term hardness, absorption, normalization, X-ray luminosity, and UV variations but no short-term variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052 We used one Chandra and four XMM Newton observations. These four XMM Newton observations were taken from August 2001 to August 2009, and the Chandra observation was taken in August 2000 (see Table obs ). Variations of 33 (10 ) in HR were obtained between the first and the last XMM Newton ( Chandra ) observations (see Column 8 in Table obs ). The individual fits gave ME2PL as the best fit.

In this case SMF2 was used, being the best representation of the observed differences (see Fig. bestfig ) when varying Norm_2 and N_ H2 (see Tables ftestcol1 , ftestcol2 and bestfit ). Variations were 49 ( Norm_2 ) and 31 ( N_ H2 ) between the first and the last observations. In Fig. luminfig , variations of the soft and hard intrinsic luminosities of the simultaneous fitting are presented.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1052 as a nearby elliptical LLAGN, linking its sub-arcsecond nonthermal SED, absent big blue bump, jets, and required truncated accretion disk.

quotes.quote: The LLAGN in the nearby elliptical galaxy NGC 1052 is an ideal case to study the connection between the accretion flow and the jet emission at low luminosities. A twin-jet system emanates from the central black hole of M 1.55 10^8 M _ 2002ApJ...581L...5W , with its axis oriented close to the plane of the sky e.g. 2016A&A...593A..47B .

The sub-arcsec resolution SED for the nucleus of NGC 1052, a prototypical LLAGN, does not show the big blue bump associated to the presence of an accretion disc. In this work, we took advantage of high-angular resolution observations to estimate the maximum possible accretion power of a standard accretion disc, corresponding to a mass accretion rate of 7.1 10^ 6 M _ yr ^ 1 . In addition, the sub-arcsec measurements with the VLT reveal a SED dominated by non-thermal emission, of synchrotron origin.
In order to provide the necessary accretion power in NGC 1052 , a thin accretion disc with an accretion rate of 7.3 10^ 5 M _ yr ^ 1 truncated at r_ tr 26 r_ g is required.

sources.summary: NGC 1052 is described as the nearby larger elliptical galaxy whose tidal field may strip the studied system; its velocity, distance, and mass set the stripping context.

quotes.quote: At D 20 Mpc, \ is likely associated with the much larger galaxy NGC 1052 at a projected distance of 75 kpc. This large elliptical galaxy is a reasonable candidate for supplying the necessary gravitational tides. It should be pointed out that the relative radial velocity of \ relative to NGC 1052 is 293 vanDokkum2018 , while the l.o.s velocity dispersion of the NGC 1052 group is only 110 vanDokkum2018 , which is consistent with the circular velocity of =200 measured from the \ content of NGC 1052 vanGorkom+86 . Thus the relative speed of \ is close to the escape velocity from NGC 1052. At a projected distance of 100 kpc between the two galaxies, \ is likely to be just skimming past NGC 1052.

However, a larger mass for NGC 1052 is obtained from the SHMR relation. For NGC 1052, the stellar mass of 10^ 11 M_ 2017MNRAS.464.4611F , translates to a halo mass of 5 10^ 12 M_ Wasserman2018 according to the SHMR of .

sources.summary: The paper reports deep GBT 21 cm non-detection of NGC 1052–DF2, deriving a very low neutral-hydrogen upper limit for an ultra-diffuse, gas-poor galaxy proposed to lack dark matter.

quotes.quote: We report deep, single-dish 21 cm observations of , taken with the Green Bank Telescope. , proposed to be lacking in dark matter, is currently classified as an ultra-diffuse galaxy in the NGC 1052 group. We do not detect the galaxy, and derive an upper limit on the mass. The galaxy is extremely gas-poor, and we find that a 3 M_ detection at a distance of 19 Mpc and using a line width of 3.2 km s^ 1 would have an upper limit of M_ ,lim < 5.5 10^5 .

Using globular clusters in , van Dokkum et al. (2018) showed that the intrinsic velocity dispersion measured, was _v = 3.2_ 3.2 ^ +5.5 km s^ 1 . Thus, we would expect the rotational velocity of NGC 1052–DF2 to be of the same order of magnitude. This requires a velocity resolution smaller than _v in order to measure an accurate line profile.

sources.summary: The note treats NGC 1052 as the nearby group elliptical, describing H I components, velocity overlap with NGC 1042, and a likely past interaction that stripped gas from NGC 1042.

quotes.quote: The distances were measured to be 19-20 Mpc cohen:18 , consistent with that of the nearby elliptical galaxy NGC 1052 and with their radial velocities ( cz=1804 \ for NGC1052-DF2 and cz=1445 \ for NGC1052-DF4). In projection it is near NGC 1052 itself, its radial velocity of cz=1371 \ is close to the central group velocity ( cz 1450 ), and in group catalogs such as that of kourkchi:17 it is listed as a bona fide member. An additional argument comes from the H i distribution in NGC 1042 and NGC 1052, as shown in vgork .

NGC 1052 has H i emission in two spatially- and dynamically-distinct components, to the NE and SW of the galaxy. The velocity range of the NE component (1297 - 1442 ) overlaps precisely with that of NGC 1042 see Figs. \ 1 and 15 of vgork . Furthermore, the H i in both galaxies has a disturbed morphology. As noted in vgork NGC 1042 is the only galaxy in the group with a large gas reservoir, and a likely interpretation of Fig. \ 1 is that NGC 1052 stripped gas from NGC 1042 in a past interaction.

sources.summary: The paper targets NGC 1052, a nearby radio galaxy, mapping subparsec HCO+ absorption in its circumnuclear torus and comparing opacity with larger-scale observations.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052 is a nearby radio galaxy with a systemic velocity ( V_ sys = cz ) of 1507 km s ^ 1 jensen03 . It exhibits a nearly symmetric double-sided radio jet from subparsec scales up to kiloparsecs e.g., jones84, wrobel84, kellermann98 , showing outward motions with an apparent velocity of 0.26 vermeulen03 . Past very-long-baseline-interferometry (VLBI) studies have shown that the nuclear structure consists of the eastern approaching and the western receding jets at an inclination angle of 57^ from the line of sight e.g., kellermann99, kameno01, kadler04b .

Atomic and molecular lines are found toward the center of NGC 1052 in emission (H _2 O megamaser for braatz94 ) and in absorption (H i for vangorkom86 ; OH for omar02 ; HCO ^ + ; HCN and CO for liszt04 ) at the radio band. NGC 1052 hosts a well-studied circumnuclear torus surrounding its central engine.

From our KVN observations, the HCO ^ + opacity of 0.4 is derived in the nuclear region of NGC 1052 with the subparsec angular resolution. It is one order higher than that obtained from the PdBI observations liszt04 . This fact suggests that the HCO ^ + covering factor is much larger on subparsec scales.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1052 as a giant elliptical LINER with two-sided relativistic jets, an emission gap attributed to absorption, and VLBI structure reproduced by an over-pressured jet.

quotes.quote: High-resolution Very-Long-Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) observations of NGC 1052 show a two sided jet with several regions of enhanced emission and a clear emission gap between the two jets. This gap shrinks with increasing frequency and vanishes around 43 GHz. The observed structures are due to both the macroscopic fluid dynamics interacting with the surrounding ambient medium including an obscuring torus and the radiation microphysics. In order to model the observations of NGC 1052 via state-of-the art numerical simulations both the macro- and micro-physics have to be taken into account.

The detailed analysis of our simulations shows that the structure and properties of the observed relativistic jets in NGC 1052 can be reconstructed by a slightly over-pressured jet ( d_k 1.5 ) embedded in a decreasing pressure ambient medium galaxies: active, -- galaxies: jets, -- radio continuum: galaxies, -- radiation mechanisms: non-thermal, radiative transfer, hydrodynamics EA model-fitting of rel. jets C. M. Fromm et al. The giant elliptical galaxy NGC 1052 (z=0.005037) harbours a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus (AGN) in its centre. The strong optical emission lines in its spectrum classifies NGC 1052 as a LINER see, 2000ApJ...532..883G .The radio structure at arcsecond scales reveal a two-sided structure, but the central core dominates the emission with about 85 of the flux 1984ApJ...284..531W .

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1052 as a LINER elliptical with kpc-scale twin radio jets and lobes, soft X-ray emission along the jets, and shock-like diffuse emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052 is considered a LINER prototype. It has been studied extensively in the literature (e.g. heckman1980 and kadler2004 ) and been included in numerous X-ray surveys of LINER gonzalez-martin2009b,hernandez-garcia2013,hernandez-garcia2014,gonzalez-martin2014, gonzalez-martin2015, hernandez-garcia2016 . It is hosted by an elliptical galaxy located at a distance of 19.2 Mpc tully2013 with redshift z=0.005 Jensen2003 , and its black hole mass has been estimated to 1.5 10^8 M woo2002 . It exhibits kpc-scale twin radio jets and associated lobes (e.g., kadler2004 ).

We have studied one Chandra observation and four XMM-Newton observations of NGC 1052 in order to explore its innermost structure along the scales of the radio jets and to determine the nature of the emission. Our main conclusions are the following: The Chandra images reveal diffuse emission below 1 keV extended along the radio lobes. However, the diffuse X-ray emission does not correlate well with the radio contours, suggesting that the X-ray and radio emission trace different parts of the particle distribution of the extended jets.

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA observations of NGC 1052, finding a gas-poor edge-on circumnuclear disk, a massive clumpy molecular torus, and ongoing low-luminosity radio AGN activity.

quotes.quote: We report ALMA observations of NGC 1052 to quest mass accretion in a gas-poor active galactic nucleus (AGN). We detected CO emission representing a rotating ring-like circumnuclear disk (CND) seen edge-on with the gas mass of 5.3 10^ 5 M _ . The CND has smaller gas mass than that in typical Seyfert galaxies with circumnuclear star formation and is too gas-poor to drive mass accretion onto the central engine. The continuum emission casts molecular absorption features of CO, HCN, HCO ^+ , SO, SO _2 , CS, CN, and H _2 O, with H ^ 13 CN and HC ^ 15 N and vibrationally-excited (v _2 = 1 ) HCN and HCO ^+ .

Broader absorption line widths than CND emission line widths imply presence of a geometrically thick molecular torus with a radius of 2.4 1.3 pc and a thickness ratio of 0.7 0.3 . We estimate the H _2 column density of (3.3 0.7) 10^ 25 cm ^ 2 using H ^ 12 CN, H ^ 13 CN, and HCO ^ + absorption features and adopting abundance ratio of ^ 12 C-to- ^ 13 C and a HCO ^ + to-H _2 , and derived the torus gas mass of (1.3 0.3) 10^7 M _ , which is 9 of the central black-hole mass. The molecular gas in the torus is clumpy with the estimated covering factor of 0.17^ +0.06 _ 0.03 .

sources.summary: The paper summarizes NGC 1052 as a group-dominant galaxy with streams, loops, a bridge, a dust lane, misaligned kinematics, and a merger-linked central starburst.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052, Fig. fig:1052 , is a dominant member of a galaxy group that became popular recently because it hosts two supposedly dark-matter-free ultra-diffuse galaxies, NGC 1052-DF2 and NGC 1052-DF4 vand18,vand19 . The numerous signs of interactions in NGC 1052 have been summarized in . Their deep optical image Figure 1 in mu19 uncovers a 10 long stream pointing southeast, a loop in the southwest, and a shell-like structure in the northwest between NGC 1052 and NGC 1047. Moreover, an H I feature in the galaxy center seems to be associated with the optical tidal bridge leading to NGC 1047. The galaxy hosts a faint diffuse kpc-scale dust lane situated roughly along the minor axis ca83,di86 .
The star and gas kinematics are largely (about 66 ) misaligned di86 . Finally, the modeling suggests a starburst in the center 1 Gyr ago due to a recent merger with a gas-rich galaxy vang86,pie05 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1052 as a possible massive host for DF2, comparing its mass, projected separation, and tidal radius to expectations for tidal distortion.

quotes.quote: The two most prominent galaxies in the FOV, and therefore the ones that could produce tidal stripping in , are NGC 1052 and NGC 1042. vD_df2 used the vicinity of to NGC 1052 ( 13 7 in projection) to support its distance of 20 Mpc. However, Monelli2019 finds two groups in the same line of sight, one at 13.5 Mpc, containing NGC 1042, and one at 20 Mpc, containing NGC 1052. They associated to the former (NGC 1042), at a projected separation of 20 5 . Even though the projected physical distance of to both galaxies would be equivalent to 80 kpc, the total mass of NGC 1052 M _ 1052,dyn (<40 kpc) = 4.14 0.71 10^ 11 M _ , Forbes2019 is more than 10 times larger than that of NGC 1042 (M _ 1042,dyn ( <8 kpc) = 3.0^ +3.0 _ 1.5 10^ 10 M _ , Trujillo et al. in prep.).

The tidal radius produced by NGC 1042 is r_ t ^ 1042 = 148_ 48 ^ +39 arcsec. On the other hand, the tidal radius if the UDG was associated to NGC 1052 is r_ t ^ 1052 = 55_ 17 ^ +14 arcsec, well within the observed extension of the galaxy in this work.

sources.summary: The paper places the collision near central elliptical NGC1052, using its group velocity, virial radius, satellite population, and tidal field to frame DF2 and DF4 formation.

quotes.quote: It is likely that the event took place near the central elliptical galaxy, NGC1052, as it is roughly halfway between DF2 and DF4 in projection and its deep potential well is conducive to high-speed interactions. It is also likely that at least one of the progenitor galaxies was a satellite of NGC1052 as the probability of a collision of two unbound galaxies is extremely small. We assume that the second progenitor was not bound to NGC1052, and either on first infall or a satellite of another massive galaxy in the group. DF4 has a velocity difference of 43 \ with respect to NGC1052, whereas the velocity of DF2 is +315 .
In this model, DF2 has travelled about 3 Mpc since the collision with an average velocity with respect to NGC1052 that is probably higher than its present-day value of 315 .

We find that the probability of a chance alignment of 11 galaxies is 3 . This calculation assumes that galaxies are oriented randomly with respect to NGC1052, and does not take into account anisotropy associated with the filamentary structure of the cosmic web.

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA detection of luminous 321-GHz water maser emission in radio galaxy NGC 1052, tying its broad line profile and core coincidence to dense gas amplifying continuum emission.

quotes.quote: The Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) serendipitously detected H _2 O J_ Ka, Kc = 10_ 2,9 9_ 3,6 emission at 321 GHz in NGC 1052. This is the first submillimeter maser detection in a radio galaxy and the most luminous 321-GHz H _2 O maser known to date with the isotropic luminosity of 1090 L_ . The line profile consists of a broad velocity component with FWHM = 208 12 km s ^ 1 straddling the systemic velocity and a narrow component with FWHM = 44 3 km s ^ 1 blueshifted by 160 km s ^ 1 . The profile is significantly different from the known 22-GHz 6_ 1,6 5_ 2,3 maser which shows a broad profile redshifted by 193 km s ^ 1 . The submillimeter maser is spatially unresolved with a synthesized beam of 0^ .68 0^ .56 and coincides with the continuum core position within 12 pc.

The radio galaxy NGC 1052 is a unique megamaser found in an elliptical galaxy with double-sided sub-relativistic jets 1994ApJ...437L..99B,1998ApJ...500L.129C , while the majority of megamasers are radio-quiet AGNs in spiral galaxies. NGC 1052 shows the 22-GHz maser spectrum spanning 400 km s ^ 1 1998ApJ...500L.129C,2003ApJS..146..249B,2005ApJ...620..145K which is smooth and broad as for radio-loud minorities such as TXS 2226-184 1995Natur.378..697K , Mrk 348 2003ApJ...590..149P and Centaurus A 2013ApJ...771L..41O , unlike archetypal megamasers which display narrow spikes <20 km s ^ 1 spread over 1000 km s ^ 1 tracing (sub-)Keplerian rotation 1993Natur.361...45N,2008ApJ...672..800H .

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA observations of NGC 1052's 321-GHz water maser, finding brightness-temperature and variability evidence, jet-aligned velocity structure, and possible gravitational acceleration.

quotes.quote: We report sub-pc-scale observations of the 321-GHz H _2 O emission line in the radio galaxy NGC 1052. The H _2 O line emitter size is constrained in < 0.6 milliarcsec distributed on the continuum core component. The brightness temperature exceeding 10^6 K and the intensity variation indicate certain evidence for maser emission. The maser spectrum consists of redshifted and blueshifted velocity components spanning 400 km s ^ 1 , separated by a local minimum around the systemic velocity of the galaxy. Spatial distribution of maser components show velocity gradient along the jet direction, implying that the population-inverted gas is driven by the jets interacting with the molecular torus.

The maser profile showed a radial velocity drift of 127 13 km s ^ 1 yr ^ 1 implying inward gravitational acceleration at 5000 Schwarzschild radii. The results demonstrate feasibility of future VLBI observations to resolve the jet-torus interacting region.

sources.summary: The paper reports VLA OH absorption toward the elliptical LINER NGC 1052, linking redshifted OH with HI absorption and possible infalling molecular gas near its active nucleus.

quotes.quote: VLA observations of OH absorption towards the elliptical galaxy NGC 1052 are presented. Both OH lines, at 1665 and 1667 MHz, were detected in absorption towards the center of NGC 1052. The hyperfine ratio of the two OH lines ( _ 1667 / _ 1665 ) is 2.6 0.8 as compared to 1.8 expected for the excitation under LTE conditions for an optically thin cloud. The column density of OH is estimated to be 2.73( 0.26) 10^ 14 cm^ 2 assuming T_ ex 10 K. The centers of both the OH lines are redshifted from the systemic velocity of the galaxy by 173 km s ^ 1 .
The velocity of OH line coincides with the velocity corresponding to the strongest HI absorption. We suggest that OH absorption is arising from a molecular cloud falling towards the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1052 as a nearby radio-loud elliptical LINER with twin jets, edge-on accretion structures, water masers, and multi-band monitoring of its jet-disk coupling.

quotes.quote: The nearby elliptical galaxy NGC 1052 can be classified as a radio-loud object . It hosts a twin-jet system oriented close to the plane of the sky (e.g., ). NGC 1052 has been classified as the prototypical low-ionisation nuclear emission region (LINER). This source is particularly suited for connecting radio and X-ray observations, since it shows an edge-on accretion disk, water maser emission, an obscuring torus (see below), and it hosts mildly relativistic jets that can be probed by VLBI.

The monitoring campaign of NGC 1052 begun in mid 2005 and is en route to a scrutiny of the jet-disk coupling in this active galaxy. For one-and-a-half years now, the jet-production activity has been monitored at sub-milli-arcsecond angular resolution with the VLBA at 22 and 43 GHz . Over this period, the source was constantly active in the radio.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 1052 as a jet-maser galaxy where maser clouds likely amplify jet-knot continuum from a foreground circumnuclear torus or disk.

quotes.quote: Two examples of jet-maser sources (but more cases are reported in literature) can be briefly described to illustrate the two mechanisms: Mrk 348 and NGC 1052. In both cases, the maser spectrum is composed of a single broad (a few 100 km/s) line redshifted w..t. the systemic velocity of the host galaxy (for the single-dish detection spectra of Mrk 348 and NGC 1052, see and , respectively), and the maser emission was confidently found to be located along the radio continuum of the jet , displaced from the position of the putative nucleus (for Mrk 348, see ; for NGC 1052, see ).

A somewhat different scenario is instead that brought about for NGC 1052 by . According to their picture, the maser clouds are most likely located foreground to the jet in a circumnuclear torus (or disk), thus amplifying the continuum seed emission from the jet knots (Fig. fig1 ). The maser gas is indeed found where the free-free opacity from foreground thermal plasma absorbing the jet synchrotron emission is large. Being the material in the torus/disk also the putative source of accretion onto the nucleus, its contraction toward the central engine accounts for the redshifted velocity of all the maser features.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 1052 as a nearby radio galaxy whose parsec-scale jet components show spectra consistent with free-free absorption plus synchrotron self-absorption.

quotes.quote: Another radio galaxy in which multifrequency VLBA observations have revealed free-free absorbed emission is NGC 1052 vermeulen03,kadler04 . It seems that free-free absorption due to ionized gas in the inner regions of AGN is fairly common and is best detected in nearby radio galaxies, where the angular resolution of the VLBA allows the study of the inner-most regions of the sources which can be obscured by the molecular torus, a source of ionized gas. Radio galaxies are also seen at a larger viewing angle, which allows the detection of the counter-jet and a better view to the nucleus than in blazars where the jet emission dominates.

The spectra of the two remaining components, in the radio galaxies 0238 084 (NGC 1052) and 0316+413 (3C 84), are shown in Fig. fig:spectra . These two sources have been studied extensively with a wider frequency coverage by vermeulen03 and kadler04 (0238 084) and by and (0316+413). studied the pc-scale jet of 0238 084 at seven frequency bands between 1.4 and 43 GHz and found the spectra to be consistent with free-free absorption together with synchrotron self-absorption.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 1052 with PKS 2254 367 as a nearby low-luminosity GPS radio galaxy, emphasizing its compact peaked radio source and parsec-scale bidirectional jets.

quotes.quote: Tingay et al. \ (2003b) listed a number of similarities between NGC 1052 and the host galaxy of PKS 2254 267 (IC 1459). Both galxies have counter-rotating cores (Forbes et al. \ 1994; Bettoni et al. \ 2001) and LINER spectra (Verdoes Kleijn et al. \ 2000; Gabel et al. \ 2000). Both radio sources are predominantly compact and have peaked spectra (de Vries, Barthel, O'Dea 1997; Tingay et al. \ 2003b).

From comprehensive multi-epoch VLBI monitoring, NGC 1052 has been revealed to possess bi-directional jets with apparent speeds of up to 0.38 that appear to be orientated in the plane of the sky (Vermeulen et al. \ 2003; Lister et al. \ 2013).

The largest extent of the jets in NGC 1052 is approximately 3 pc (Vermeulen et al. \ 2003). In PKS 2254 367, the jets have a projected extent of approximately 8 pc. The integrated monochromatic luminosity at 5 GHz of PKS 2254 367 is 7 10^ 22 W Hz ^ 1 , and the compact radio source in NGC 1052 has a very similar luminosity, 5 10^ 22 W Hz ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC1052 as a massive nearby elliptical whose tidal field truncates the diffuse system; simulations test its external field and argue the system is likely skimming past NGC1052 near escape speed.

quotes.quote: The observed \ is expected to be truncated at R_t 7 kpc by the tidal gravitational force field of NGC1052 , a much larger nearby elliptical galaxy at a projected distance of 100 kpc .

We have also run simulations where the whole system is subject to the external field of a nearby larger galaxy like NGC1052 in the vicinity of . The results regarding orbital decay of GCs are unaltered by the inclusion of a (static) external field, provided that they lie within the tidal radius of the simulated . It should be pointed out that the relative radial velocity of \ relative to NGC1052 is 293 vanDokkum2018 , while line-of-sight velocity dispersion of the NGC1052 group is only 110 vanDokkum2018 which is consistent with the circular velocity of 200 measured from the \ content of NGC1052 Gorkom1986 . Thus the relative speed of \ is close to the escape velocity from NGC1052. At a projected distance of 100 kpc between the two galaxies, \ is likely to be just skimming past NGC1052.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC1052 as a possible 20 Mpc host for [KKS2000] 04, then argues the dwarf is unlikely to be its tidal-debris remnant because the stellar masses are too similar.

quotes.quote: The two most prominent galaxies in the line of sight of [ KKS2000 ] 04 are NGC1052 and NGC1042 (see Fig. fig:ngc1042 ). 2018Natur.555..629V have used the vicinity (13 7 in projection) of [ KKS2000 ] 04 to NGC1052 to support their claim that [ KKS2000 ] 04 is at 20 Mpc The redshift-independent distance measurement to NGC1052 is 20 Mpc see e.g. 2001ApJ...546..681T,2001MNRAS.327.1004B . . If this were the case, [ KKS2000 ] 04 would be a satellite located at 80 kpc from NGC1052 2018Natur.555..629V .

This stellar mass is close to the putative host galaxy NGC1052 10^ 11 M_ , e.g., 2011MNRAS.411L..21F . This similarity in stellar mass makes [ KKS2000 ] 04 unlikely to be a debris from the merger that gave rise to NGC1052. This is because newborn TDGs are typically less than a 100 times fainter and less massive than the progenitor galaxy e.g. 2000A&A...358..819W,2001AJ....121.2524M .

sources.summary: Deep imaging finds multiple low-surface-brightness features around NGC 1052, including an interaction with NGC 1047, a loop toward NGC 1042, and a stream toward DF2.

quotes.quote: We find a tidal interaction between NGC 1052 and NGC 1047, confirming a physical association. Furthermore we find a stellar loop around NGC 1052 in the direction of NGC 1042 and a stellar stream pointing in the direction of NGC 1052-DF2 -- but both are not directly connected. No low surface brightness features have been uncovered around the spiral galaxy NGC 1042, which leaves the association (physical or projected) between NGC 1052 and NGC 1042 ambiguous, though they have similar radial velocities.

Most prominently, there is a narrow stream coming from NGC 1052 to the vicinity of NGC 1052-DF2 which we refer to as Stream 1. A loop is visible to the south-west (loop SW), close to NGC 1042 but apparently disconnected from it. The tidal tail as seen in \ around NGC 1052 extends towards NGC 1047 (see Figure lsbs ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1052 as an elliptical or E/S0 galaxy whose curvature may mark a core transition and rotating disk seen in residual imaging.

quotes.quote: NGC1052 is classified as an elliptical galaxy RC3_1991 and as E/S0 sandage1981 . indicates the presence of a core. In fig:pgc0010175rkur the curvature suggests some possible transition around R/R_p 0.15 , in which, therefore, might be between the core structure and the remaining structure of an elliptical.

Furthermore, conclude that NGC 1052 has a rotating disk. The curvature may indicate such kind of component because the mean small values after R/R_p 0.6 , however such disk seems not to be exponential. The disk is also seen in the residual image.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1052 as a peculiar low-excitation radio galaxy with obscuration, possible hybrid accretion, uncertain jet-base resolution, and young-source morphology.

quotes.quote: Concerning the two ``outliers'', NGC 1052 and 3C 84, we note that these are both peculiar LERG. NGC 1052 presents features which are unusual for a low-luminosity AGN, since the nucleus is obscured by a high column density torus 2004A&A...426..481K , and a broad iron line of unclear origin has been detected based on X-ray observations 2009ApJ...698..528B . proposed that that NGC 1052 hosts a hybrid accretion disk in a transition regime, i.e. formed by a central ADAF and an outer thin disk e.g., 1997ApJ...489..865E .

In the case of NGC 1052, one of the two LERG placed on the HERG region, high resolution VLBI observations with the GMVA 2016A&A...593A..47B do not clearly resolve the jet base, thus it is plausible that this jet was initially narrow and has experienced a fast expansion at larger distances. Moreover, did not confirm a parabolic expansion for this source, suggesting instead a transition from cylindrical to conical shape at a distance of 10^4 R_ S .

NGC 1052 is a young source, and the large scale morphology may be consistent with that of a young FRII see 1984ApJ...284..531W ; 3C 84 is a recently reactivated source showing a bright hotspot-like feature e.g., 2017ApJ...849...52N which would approach it to a (temporary, at least) FRII morphology; 3C 120, while is more often classified as an FRI, presents a peculiar morphology, with a strongly bent jet and edge-brightened lobe 1987ApJ...316..546W .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1052 as the massive nearby galaxy whose halo may encompass DF2 and DF4 and whose close encounters could explain their dark-matter deficiency.

quotes.quote: The two observed galaxies lacking dark matter, DF2 and DF4 , are at close projected separations (80 kpc and 165 kpc, which set a lower limit to the 3D separations) from NGC 1052 (stellar mass M_ =1.05 10^ 11 M_ ). Following Ref. , we estimate that NGC 1052 has M_ vir =6.2^ +25.8 _ 4.2 10^ 12 M_ at 90 confidence, which is consistent with the virial masses of our hosts. The stellar mass ratios of DF2 and DF4 relative to NGC 1052 are consistent with our simulation; however, no orbital information is available to determine their minimum halo-centric distance.

Our results support a scenario where DF2 and DF4 became dark-matter deficient due to past close encounters with NGC 1052, the only massive galaxy in their neighbourhood (note that our simulation allows hosts to have multiple dark-matter deficient satellites). Their projected (80 kpc and 165 kpc) and line-of-sight separations ( 2.1 0.5 Mpc) - as well as our estimates of the virial diameter of the NGC 1052 halo -- suggest that DF2 and DF4 could be satellites of the NGC 1052 group. Concretely, using Ref. , we estimate this galaxy group to have a virial diameter of 0.7-1.7 Mpc at 90 confidence, allowing for the possibility that NGC 1052 could encompass both objects.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC1052 as the massive elliptical host/group environment for DF2, DF4, and the UDG trail, with its tidal field possibly making DMDGs diffuse.

quotes.quote: Recent observations of an aligned trail of 7 - 11 UDGs near NGC1052, including DMDGs DF2 and DF4, suggesting a common formation site and time, 8.9 1.5 Gyr ago, provide a test. Hydro/N-body simulations are presented here with initial conditions for colliding satellites tailored to match the observed UDGs in the NGC1052 group, supplemented by galaxy orbit integrations.

We further offer a hint that the tidal field of host NGC1052 may contribute to making DMDGs diffuse. CDM simulation in a 100 cMpc box finds our required initial conditions 10 times at z<3 . These results indicate current observations are consistent with the Mini-bullet scenario.

It was notable, therefore, when vD18a, vD19 reported the existence of two ultra-diffuse galaxies (UDGs), NGC1052-DF2 and NGC1052-DF4 (hereafter DF2 and DF4, respectively), that are located in close proximity to the massive elliptical galaxy NGC1052 and exhibit a deficiency in dark matter, rather than the dark matter-dominance described above UDGs are defined to be dwarf galaxies with effective half-light radius R_ eff 1.5 kpc and surface brightness (g,0) > 24 mag arcsec ^ 2 vD15 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1052 as the sample's best-constrained case, finding a small region near an oblate spheroid while excluding questionable asymmetric outer points.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052. The data for this galaxy is the best for any in the sample. DB present kinematic data on four PAs, Binney et al. \ ( Binney1990 1990) on the major and minor axis and Fried Illingworth ( Bender1994 1994) on the major axis. The turnover radius of the rotation curve is easily seen to be at 15 for this galaxy. A velocity difference of almost 50 km/sec between the southern and northern parts of the galaxy outside of 37 on PA 117 and PA 164 in both the DB and Binney et al. \ ( Binney1990 1990) data sets imply that NGC 1052 may not be antisymmetric outside of this radius as is assumed in our models. The total of 11 questionable points outside of 35 are therefore eliminated.
This galaxy has the best constrained shape in our sample; Figure f.montage shows only a small permitted region around the oblate spheroid with =0.63 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1052 as a nearby radio-loud active galaxy whose broad X-ray iron-line variability coincided with radio-jet plasma ejection, making it a key jet-disk coupling target.

quotes.quote: The nearby, radio-loud, core-dominated active galaxy NGC 1052 exhibits strong, relativistically broadened iron-line emission at X-ray frequencies (Kadler et al. 2004). Pronounced variability of the broad iron line was accompanied by an ejection of relativistic plasma into the radio jet in early 2000. This behaviour can be interpreted as an instability of the inner accretion disk around the central supermassive black hole, which triggered enhanced accretion onto the black hole, while a fraction of the inner-disk material was injected into the jet.

NGC 1052 is the only AGN with strong broad iron line emission and a bright, compact radio jet detected so far. This makes NGC 1052 the key object for future studies, which address the open questions of black hole accretion and jet formation. A dedicated monitoring campaign of NGC 1052 with i) high frequency VLBI observations and ii) high signal-to-noise X-ray spectroscopic observations provides the ideal approach towards an understanding of the unsolved questions in AGN physics.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1052 as a nearby elliptical with a low-luminosity AGN, symmetric subluminal jets, extended VLA jets, and circumnuclear molecular, atomic, and ionized gas.

quotes.quote: 0238 084 (NGC 1052): This is a bright nearby elliptical galaxy that harbors a well-studied low luminosity AGN. have previously discussed the observed outflow in two nearly symmetric jets containing multiple features which propagate away from a central region with relatively slow subluminal velocities. VLA observations show the jets extending symmetrically out to 1.5 kpc on either side CLK07,Wro84 . The central region is surrounded by a rich molecular (OH and H_2O ), atomic, and ionized gas cloud. HI observations indicate the presence of three velocity streams, and multi-frequency VLBA observations show patchy free-free absorption, which may come from the torus surrounding the central black hole VRK03,k04b .

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1052 as a prototypical LINER early-type galaxy whose MIR spectrum has AGN-like hot dust, PAH features, molecular lines, and forbidden emission lines.

quotes.quote: The MIR spectra of NGC 1052, considered a prototypical LINER see e.g. Ho08 , and of IC 5063, a well known Seyfert (see for references on-line notes in R05) are shown in Fig. Fig9 . The continuum of both galaxies is dominated by hot dust, most probably coming from an AGN torus. PAH emission features are also visible; in particular, NGC 1052 shows both the 7.6 and 11.3 m PAH complexes, while it is possible to distinguish only the 7.6 m feature in IC 5063.

H _2 0-0 S(1) and S(3) molecular emission lines are present in both spectra. Ionic forbidden emission lines of [ Ar II ] , [ Ne II ] and [ Ne III ] , [ S III ] and [ O IV ] are revealed in both spectra.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1052 as a LINER type 2 example whose high-resolution SED is power-law-like and jet-dominated from radio to ultraviolet.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052 NGC 1052 is a prototypical example of a LINER type 2 nucleus . Fig. n1052_sed shows the high-spatial resolution SED (black dots), together with large aperture measurements (grey spikes). The nucleus is the brightest source in the radio and mid-IR ranges, and seems to dominate the millimetre and sub-millimetre ranges as well.
A self-absorbed synchrotron , i.e. a very simple model of a jet, describes very well the high-spatial resolution SED of NGC 1052 in the radio-to-UV range (dashed-line in Fig. n1052_sed ).
Although a detailed modelling is desired, this simple exercise show that the energy output in the nucleus of NGC 1052 is driven the jet.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1052 requires variable absorption and normalization in XMM-Newton data, shows modest soft and hard X-ray flux changes, and has UV variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052 : SMF2 is used to fit its XMM -Newton data, with variations of Norm_2 (49 ) and N_ H2 (31 ) over a period of eight years. Flux variations of 20 are obtained for soft and hard energies in the same period. Since the annular region contributes with 10 in Chandra data, Chandra and XMM -Newton data are compared, without changes in one year period. Short term variations are not detected. UV variations from the UVW2 (13 ) and UVM2 (21 ) are found.

sources.summary: The paper places NGC1052 at about 19 Mpc from SBF distances, describes its interaction with NGC1047, and assigns it to the farther projected group in the DF4 field.

quotes.quote: The distance to NGC1052 and NGC1047 The distance to NGC1052 has been estimated using the surface brightness fluctuations (SBF) technique both in the I band 19.4 2 Mpc; 2001ApJ...546..681T and in the F160W band 18 2 Mpc; 2003ApJ...583..712J . The two results agree on a distance compatible with 19 Mpc The Large Scale Structure reconstruction done by 2007A&A...465...71T gives also a compatible distance of 17.4 Mpc for this object. . Interestingly, NGC1052 is interacting with another galaxy in the field-of-view: NGC1047. This is clearly shown in the Dragonfly deep image of the group shown in Fig. 4 of 2019ApJ...874L...5V and the deep image by the Heron telescope provided by Fig. 1 of 2019A&A...624L...6M . This interaction is also very likely responsible for the perturbed distribution of HI around NGC1052 found in 1986AJ.....91..791V .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1052 as the large elliptical parent galaxy near DF2, estimating its stellar and halo masses and arguing its tides could strip material from DF2.

quotes.quote: At at a distance D=18 Mpc, \ is at a projected distance of 75 kpc from the the large elliptical NGC 1052 and with a relative LOS speed of 293 vanDokkum2018 . We emphasize here that based on the measured LOS velocity dispersion in NGC 1052 ( 110 ), the relative speed of \ is close to the escape velocity from NGC 1052 and the two galaxies, \ are likely to be just skimming each other. Still, the gravitational tides of NGC 1052 could certainly be strong enough to cause significant mass mass loss in .

A larger mass for NGC 1052 is obtained from the SHMR relation. The NGC 1052 stellar mass of 10^ 11 M_ 2017MNRAS.464.4611F , translates to a halo mass of 5 10^ 12 M_ Wasserman2018 according to the SHMR.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 1052 with NGC 4151, noting faster, variable radio jets, higher radio luminosity, an elliptical LINER host, and likely ADAF-powered nuclear emission.

quotes.quote: For example, in NGC 1052 jet speeds of 0.34c (western jet) and 0.53c (eastern jet) are found whereas in NGC 4151 no detectable movement is found Ulvestad2005 . Also in NGC 1052, the overall morphologies change over a four year period and flux density variations in jet components are seen on that timescale Baczko2016,Baczko2019 . Moreover the radio luminosity of NGC 1052 is 100 that of NGC 4151 and is unequivocally a radio-loud object Kadler2004 . However although NGC 4151 and NGC 1052 have similar black hole masses ( log (M_ BH ) = 7.7 and 8.2, respectively), they reside in very different host galaxies: NGC 4151 is a Seyfert 1.5 spiral galaxy whereas NGC 1052 is classified as an elliptical LINER galaxy. Thus the majority of the nuclear emission in NGC 4151 is probably powered by an efficient accretion flow whereas that in NGC 1052 is likely powered by an inefficient ADAF flow which more naturally launches jets e.g. see and references therein NarayanYi,YuanNarayan2014 .
