sources.summary: The paper groups NGC 1052 with Sy2 sources showing X-ray absorption variability, high covering factors, and intermediate/high opening angles, preferring a thick clumpy gas-and-dust torus intercepted along the line of sight.

quotes.quote: Panel 5 of Figure fig:cartoon_Sy2 (e.g. NGC 4507, NGC 1052, NGC 4939, and IC 5063): Sy2 sources with absorption variability at X-rays, high covering factors, and half opening angles at both X-rays and mid-IR consistent with intermediate or high values. A clumpy distribution of both gas and dust is preferred for this group of objects. All the properties described above can be explained with a geometrically-thick torus where dust and gas is distributed in clumps. The gas is probably located at both the outer dusty and inner dust-free regions, the latter producing the observed absorption variability. Indeed the inclination angles constrained for these sources are consistent with these clumps being intercepted by the LOS.

sources.summary: NGC 1052 is described as the sample’s only elliptical galaxy, with an ambiguous LINER/Seyfert classification, a nearby radio-loud AGN, old stellar populations, high stellar dispersion, uneven metallicity, and no featureless continuum.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052 NGG 1052 is a giant E4 galaxy Forbes+01,Xilouris+04 , with an ambiguous LINER/Seyfert classification, well known for having one of the nearest radio-loud AGN Heckman80,Ho+97,RiffelRA+17 . The fact that it is the only elliptical galaxy in our sample is consistent with the fact that it is the only galaxy with no traces of young and intermediate-age SSPs. Also, the stellar kinematics are also consistent with its elliptical nature, with the highest stellar ( 250 km s ^ 1 ) and smallest peak velocities ( 60 km s ^ 1 ). The metallicity is uneven, with a peak located 1 0 to the northwest of the nucleus. Also, it is the only object in which no contribution of a FC was detected, which can be taken as a consequence of its dual LINER/Seyfert classification, implying that it harbors a less active AGN if compared to the rest of the sample.

sources.summary: DF4 is presented as another nearby dark-matter-deficient galaxy, similar to DF2 in age, mass, size, globular clusters, and possible formation history.

quotes.quote: There is another DMDG in the neighborhood: NGC 1052--DF4 (hereafter DF4). Surprisingly, DF2 and DF4 are quite similar to each other in that their age, mass, size, number of GCs, the spatial distribution of GCs, and peak of the luminosity function of GCs DF4_discovery, Danieli2020, Shen2021a . This fact may imply that DF2 and DF4 are involved with each other in their formation, or maybe purely a coincidence. We are facing the challenge of whether or not DMDGs such as DF2 and DF4 can form in the cold dark matter ( CDM) universe. One proposed formation scenario for DF2 is `mini-bullet cluster scenario' Silk2019, Shin2020, Lee2021, Otaki2022 . This explains the formation as a consequence of a collision between two gas-rich dwarf galaxies. vanDokkum2022a, vanDokkum2022b applied it to DF2 and DF4, and showed that some of the observed features are consistent with the outcome of the scenario.

sources.summary: The paper presents sub-parsec VLBA imaging and radio SED results for NGC 1052, describing its central radio morphology and spectral shape in the FRAMEx AGN sample.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052 [ 2 ^ h 41 ^ m 04 7985, 08 15 20 751 ] : All four band images revealed a bright center with extended east-west double-sided collimated outflows. Similar double-sided outflows were observed in multiple previous VLBA studies of this source Kameno_2003PASA...20..134K, Sawada_2008ApJ...680..191S,Baczko_2022A&A...658A.119B . NGC 1052 is the only source in our sample showing a prominent double-sided jet-like collimated structure extending outward from the central region, even with the high-resolution ( 1 pc) K-band observation. The four-frequency SED plot shows a GHz-peaked (curved) spectrum with a peak near 5 GHz. The piecewise regression model yields two different spectral slopes ( ) with two linear functions connected to a ``knot value'' indicating steep spectral indices at high frequencies and an inverted spectral slope at lower frequencies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1052 as a nearby elliptical low-luminosity AGN with compact twin radio jets, free-free absorption, and ionized, atomic, and molecular circumnuclear gas.

quotes.quote: We discuss multiple Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) continuum and spectral line imaging observations and Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope spectroscopy of the compact variable nuclear radio jet source in the elliptical galaxy NGC 1052 . Absorption and emission signatures reveal ionised, atomic, and molecular components of the surrounding medium. Ten epochs of Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) data at 15 GHz, spanning almost six years, show bi-symmetric jets, in which multiple sub-parsec scale features display outward motions of typically v_ app 0.26c ( H _0=65 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ) on each side. The jets are most likely oriented near the plane of the sky. Multi-frequency VLBA observations at seven frequencies between 43 and 1.4 GHz show free-free absorption in the inner parsec around the nucleus, probably together with synchrotron self-absorption.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1052 as an elliptical galaxy with a rotating central gas disk plus a blueshifted outflow component tied to its unusual polarization.

quotes.quote: The kinematical gradient of the rotating gas disk in the center of the elliptical galaxy NGC 1052 yields a mass of 6 10 ^8 M _ inside 166 pc. Two components are found: component A arises in a rotating disk, component B is blueshifted by 400 km/s relative to the disk. B likely originates from outflowing gas distributed within a wide cone. There is no need for a broad H component in unpolarized flux. The peculiar polarization seen in [ O i ] 6300 by Barth et al. (1999) may be related to the outflow component.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1052 as a nearby LINER elliptical whose parsec-scale two-sided radio jets show outward motions, free-free absorption, and multiple H I absorption velocity systems.

quotes.quote: The bright radio structure of the LINER elliptical galaxy NGC 1052 is dominated by bi-symmetric jets on parsec scales. Features move outward on both sides of the core at v_ app 0.26c . We have established the occurrence of free-free absorption, and suggest the presence of a patchy, geometrically thick region oriented roughly orthogonal to the jets; components have a wide range of spectral shapes and brightness temperatures. We distinguish three velocity systems in H i absorption. The ``high velocity'' system is the most prominent of these; it is redshifted from systemic by about 150 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper tests its visibility-based brightness-temperature method on NGC 1052 and finds elongation-corrected limits close to model-fit estimates.

quotes.quote: The methodology of this approach is described in Sect. sc:method and is tested with the visibility data from two VLBA Very Long Baseline Array of National Radio Astronomy Observatory, Socorro NM, USA; http://www.nrao.edu observations of the prominent compact radio sources 3C 345 and NGC 1052.

The same correction applied to NGC 1052 (with = 0.4 and _ jet = -110^ ) results in corrected T_ b,min = 1.1 10^ 10 K and T_ b,lim = 2.4 10^ 10 K, with the latter value falling very close to T_ b,mod = 2.8 10^ 10 K estimated from the modelfit. Both these results indicate that T_ b,lim can be improved by correcting for the elongation of the core region. Applying \ the visibility-based brightness temperature estimates to VLBI data on 3C 345 and NGC 1052 has demonstrated that the method is reliable in two particular cases.

sources.summary: The paper reports 5BZUJ 0241-0815 as a transient gamma-ray emitter whose 2013-2014 flare reached TS 12 and aligns with the source position.

quotes.quote: 5BZUJ 0241-0815. In this case, the light curve shows a flaring state during 2013 to first quarter of 2014. From March 7 to April 7, 2014 the \ signature reaches its highest state, at which TS 12. A TS map at E > 500 MeV (fig. bzu0241 ) shows that 5BZUJ 0241-0815 is within the 68 confinement region for the \ signature. This blazar of unknown type is another example of a transient \ emitter, which may contribute to building the currently unresolved \ background.

sources.summary: The survey highlights NGC 1052 as near the radio-loudness threshold, radio-loud in classification, and known to host a low-frequency radio jet with a 2.8 kpc structure.

quotes.quote: Four objects in our sample are near or above this threshold: Arp 102B ( R_O=44 ), NGC 5506 ( R_O=13 ), Mrk 477 ( R_O=10 ), and NGC 1052 ( R_O=9 ). Two of these, Arp 102B and NGC 1052, have small radio jets at low frequencies Helmboldt2007,Wrobel1984 .
The other two had linear structures considerably larger: 37 kpc for NGC 3718 Condon1987 and 2.8 kpc for NGC 1052 Wrobel1984 .
Two of these are radio-loud objects Arp 102B and NGC 1052.

sources.summary: The paper finds broad nuclear emission in NGC 1052, fits its line kinematics with the same Gaussian components as H-alpha, and identifies ratios requiring fast-shock models.

quotes.quote: In NGC 1052, NGC 2217 and IC 1459, a broad is also seen in their spectra. In these cases, we fitted the \ line with the same set of Gaussian functions used to describe the line profile, i.e. we assumed that all Gaussian components of the line have the same kinematics as the Gaussian components found for the line. The fitted profiles for the \ lines of NGC 1052, NGC 2217 and IC 1459 are shown in Fig. B2 of Appendix B, together with the fitted profile of NGC 4958 taken from 2019MNRAS.486.1138R .
However, the / versus / diagram reveals that six objects (NGC 1411, NGC 1553, NGC 1052, NGC 4958, NGC 4546 and NGC 2217) have / ratios that require fast shock models.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1052 as a low-luminosity AGN with faint X-rays, pc-to-kpc radio jets, sub-parsec water masers, a circumnuclear molecular disk, and rich molecular absorption.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052 Galaxy and environment NGC1052 contains a low-luminosity AGN with faint X-ray emission Woo2002 . Despite the AGN's low luminosity, it has produced radio jets on pc to kpc scales which suggest recent and historic accretion Nakahara2020 . Kameno2024 have reported sub-pc scale observations of 321 GHz H _2 O emission, indicating water masers incredibly close to the supermassive black hole's event horizon, but with remarkably similar dynamics to the molecular absorption. Molecular emission High angular resolution ALMA observations presented by Kameno2020 show CO emission from a rotating ring-like circumnuclear disk with a molecular mass of 5.3 10^ 5 M _ .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1052 as a best-fit example, where CDE olivine and Mie olivine components reproduce shifted 18 and 10 micron silicate features.

quotes.quote: As examples of the best fits obtained, Figure fig:best_fits_examples shows the best-fit models for the spectra of I Zw 1 and NGC 1052. We show the fits for these objects because I Zw 1 has the best fit with the lowest ^2_ value and NGC 1052 is a good example to show the behavior of some dust species, which is explained later. The residuals between the observed spectra and the model for both objects are within 5 at all wavelengths and their ^2_ are 0.12 and 0.68, respectively.

In the case of NGC 1052, we can see that CDEOl01 contributes to fit the peak of the 18 \ m silicate feature shifted towards shorter wavelengths and MieOl3 contributes to fit the peak of the 10 \ m silicate feature shifted towards longer wavelengths.

sources.summary: The paper describes J0241 0815 as a low-redshift galaxy with variable radio spectra, high variability, and low-frequency excess consistent with renewed GPS-source activity.

quotes.quote: J0241 0815 (0238 084, NGC1052) is a low redshift galaxy, considered in detail by and . A spectrum compiled from an extensive list of sources is given by , revealing variability by a factor of 2 over a wide range of frequencies. This variable nature is confirmed by the fact that the source has the second highest variability index in Table tab1 . The low frequency points lie above the nominal fit to the spectrum, and are qualitatively consistent with the notion that this source has previously been active, with the GPS spectrum reflecting a new recent stage of activity e.g., tin03a .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1052 as a 1-3 Gyr merger remnant with fine structure, infalling HI and tidal tails, but an old globular-cluster system despite a young central stellar population.

quotes.quote: An interesting comparison is the cases of NGC 1316 and NGC 1052. Both are thought to be 1--3 Gyr old merger remnants. NGC 1316 has a very disturbed morphology with extensive gas and dust. NGC 1052 has an elliptical appearance with some fine structure (Schweizer Seitzer 1992), and evidence for HI gas infalling onto the nucleus and in extended tails (van Gorkom et al. 1986).

NGC 1052 has no equivalent counterpart GCs (Forbes et al. 2001); here the CMD distribution of GCs suggests they are predominately old. Subsequent Keck spectra of 16 NGC 1052 GCs confirm they are old despite the galaxy having a young central stellar population of 2 Gyrs (Pierce et al. 2005).

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 1052 as a radio galaxy whose high core-shift index reflects free-free absorption, with a two-sided parsec jet and obscured receding inner jet.

quotes.quote: found much higher values of k , indicating free-free absorption in the radio galaxy NGC 1052. The key difference between NGC 1052 and the sources observed in our sample is that this radio galaxy shows a two-sided parsec-scale jet. The inner part of the receding jet of NGC 1052 is likely to be obscured by a circumnuclear torus 2004A&A...426..481K .

sources.summary: The paper describes 0238 084/NGC 1052 as a two-sided jet whose 15 GHz core is obscured by free-free absorption from a circumnuclear torus, requiring a virtual core for component positions.

quotes.quote: 0238 084 (NGC 1052): Multi-frequency VLBA observations of this two-sided jet by and indicate that the core feature is obscured at 15 GHz by strong free-free absorption associated with a circumnuclear torus. In order to obtain the component positions at each epoch, the position of a virtual core was found using a least squares minimization method, as described by . The core component entries in Table 3gaussiantable for this source are therefore left blank.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 1052's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052 is a dusty elliptical galaxy at a distance of D= 19.4 1.5 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with a radio-loud LINER nucleus with broad polarized lines, a biconical jet (diameter 3 pc; PA 66 ; kellermann_sub-milliarcsecond_1998, vermeulen_shroud_2003 ) and a flat radio spectrum showing variability by a factor of two in flux tornikoski_high_2000 . A subparsec-scale dense plasma torus and a water maser around the nucleus have been detected with VLBI radio observations braatz_discovery_1994,kameno_dense_2001,kadler_twin-jet_2004,sawada-satoh_positional_2008 . NGC 1052 is commonly regarded as the prototypical LINER and has been studied extensively, in particular at radio wavelengths.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1052 as the brightest group member, with broad optical lines, H2O megamaser emission, multiwavelength variability, and low-ionization GNIRS lines.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052 (L1.9) NGC 1052 is the brightest member of a small group which, together with the NGC 1069 group, makes up the Cetus I cluster Wiklind95 . Broad lines are clearly detected in the optical spectrum Barth99,Ho97,Balmaverde14 . NGC 1052 also shows H_2O megamaser emission Claussen98 , and variability in the radio, Vermeulen03 , ultraviolet Maoz05 , and X-rays Hernandez-Garcia13 .The X-ray data clearly indicate the presence of an unresolved nuclear source in the hard bands Satyapal05,Gonzalez-Martin06 , and Gonzalez-Martin14 recently used artificial neural networks to show that this object might be more similar to a Seyfert galaxy than other LINERs, from the X-ray point of view.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1052 as an elliptical with an unusually extended H I reservoir, likely captured from a gas-rich neighbor, plus misaligned parsec- and kiloparsec-scale radio jets.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052 is an elliptical galaxy with an exceptionally large H i extent, which is about 3 times larger than its optical size ( D_ H 1 = 16 kpc). Both the morphological and the kinematical properties of H i gas are highly suggestive of its capturing the gas from gas-rich dwarf or spiral neighbor vanGorkom1986 . The misalignment of pc-scale and kpc-scale radio jet has been reported Vermeulen2003,Kadler2004 .

sources.summary: The paper represents NGC 1052 as the massive elliptical central potential hosting DF2 and DF4, and tests whether its tidal field can reduce satellite dark matter density.

quotes.quote: In order to overcome the limited resolution provided by large-volume simulations (at the scale of interest), Ogiya2018 performed a series of controlled N body simulations of the tidal interaction between NGC 1052 and a smaller satellite galaxy aimed at representing the progenitor of DF2 and DF4.

We shall use as initial conditions for the interaction simulations the output of fully cosmological hydrodynamical simulations of dwarf galaxy formation, and set them on radial orbits around a central potential aimed at reproducing the properties of NGC 1052.
Since both DF2 and DF4 are satellite galaxies of NGC 1052, a massive elliptical galaxy, there have been suggestions that tidal effects could reduce the central DM density Ogiya2018,Jing2019 .

sources.summary: The paper adopts NGC 1052 as a low-excitation LINER template, citing high-resolution flux measurements, a steep UV continuum lacking a big blue bump, and unobscured wavelength coverage.

quotes.quote: As a reference template for the ionising continuum of low-excitation LINER nuclei, we adopted a power-law piecewise fit to the flux distribution of NGC 1052 (blue solid line in Fig. fig_input ). The fit relies on flux measurements obtained with a remarkably high angular resolution dataset ( 0 4 = 35 pc at the distance of NGC 1052) and compiled by us (black dots in Fig. fig_input ; 2019MNRAS.485.5377F ). The choice of NGC 1052 as a reference for low-excitation nuclei has a twofold motivation: i) the steep power-law UV continuum found to be common among the LINER class (of which NGC 1052 is a prototypical example; 1996ApJ...462..183H,1980A&A....87..152H ), that misses the big blue bump continuum associated with the disc emission; ii) the superior sampling in wavelength achieved for this galaxy due to the lack of dust obscuration, which allows us to detect the near-UV continuum.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1052 as the massive elliptical host whose tidal field shaped the proposed collision origin of nearby dark-matter-deficient galaxies and could strip their globular clusters.

quotes.quote: The second purpose of this paper is to test if the DMDG can retain GCs on extended orbits under the influence of the tidal force of NGC 1052. In the mini bullet cluster scenario, the GC formation is expected to happen on a short timescale, 100 Myr, immediately following the galaxy collision see also e.g. Madau2020 Lee2021 , and as a consequence, the homogeneous age and metallicity of GCs in DF2 and DF4 may be explained Fensch2019_df2,vanDokkum2022_gc . The short timescale of the formation of DMDGs and associated GCs suggests that their birthplace virtually corresponds to the place of the galaxy collision. As one of the progenitor galaxies was a satellite galaxy of NGC 1052, the event should have happened in the potential field of NGC 1052. Thus, GCs belonging to the collision-induced DMDGs are subject to tidal stripping by NGC 1052, depending on the position of the GCs within the DMDG and the location of the galaxy collision.

sources.summary: NGC1052 is modeled as the massive host whose halo tidally strips DF2; the paper estimates its stellar and dark-matter halo masses and accretion history.

quotes.quote: We model the DM halo of NGC1052 using a Navarro-Frenk-White (NFW) profile , ()= _ s r_ s (1+ r_ s )^ 2 , where _ s and r_ s represent the scale density and radius respectively. An NFW profile can also be equivalently described by its viral mass and concentration parameter. The viral mass M_ 200 is defined as the mass enclosed within a radius r_ 200 , in which the average density is 200 times the critical density. The concentration parameter is defined as =r_ 200 /r_ s . NGC1052 has a stellar mass of 10^ 11 M_ . According to the stellar-halo mass relation provided by Ref. , the DM halo mass of NGC1052 is estimated to be approximately 1.1 10^ 13 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1052 as an elliptical brightest group galaxy with galaxy-scale X-ray emission, offset spatially and kinematically within an irregular, dynamically immature group.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052 Group This group shows galaxy-scale X-ray emission associated with the BGG -- NGC 1052. NGC 1052 is an elliptical galaxy, offset both kinematically and spatially from the group centre defined by the FOF algorithm. The group consists of 29 members, however its velocity dispersion and virial mass are relatively low and it has a low early-type fraction. Spatially the group is quite extended and irregular, with NGC 1052 lying in a sub-clump which is offset to the group centroid.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1052 as a LINER whose VLBA core resolves into twin jets, with measured UV fluxes and adopted X-ray properties for its nuclear SED.

quotes.quote: NGC 1052 - Kadler et al. (2004) have used VLBA to resolve the core into milliarcsecond-scale twin jets, with a total flux at 0.7, 3.6, and 6 cm, of 670 mJy, 2390 mJy, and 2410 mJy, respectively. There is a small gap between the jets, and it is not clear which component, if any, can be associated directly with the nucleus. In the single epoch measured by M05 in 2002, the UV flux was 7.7 10^ 17 at 2500 and 9.0 10^ 17 at 3300 .
